### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: C++/CLI Profile

C++/CLI Profile

The profile table and description in this section do not include the tagged value inherited from C++ ANSI profile.

##### Stereotype

C++CLIProperty

«C++CLIProperty» is used to define a CLR property, which has the appearance of an ordinary data member, and can be written to or read from using the same syntax as a data member.

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| C++CLIProperty | Property | The stereotype must not be applied to the same attribute as «C++Attribute» or other stereotypes derived from «C++Attribute» |
| Tag | Type | Descriptions |
| propertyIndexType | String[0..*] | index type keeps the list of property index types. |
| isVirtualProperty | boolean[1] = false | Specify whether the property. is virtual or not. |
| propertyType | PropertyType[1] = property data member | Specify property type between:property data memberproperty block with getproperty block with setproperty block with get and set |
| Inherited Tag | Type | Description |
| accessSpecifier «C++CLIClassMember» | CLIvisibilityKind[0..1] (Enumeration)See | Represents the usage of visibility kind introduced in C++/CLI including:internalprotected publicpublic protectedprivate protectedprotected private |

| Name | Meta Class | Constraints |
| --- | --- | --- |

| Tag | Type | Descriptions |
| C++CLIEvent | boolean[1] = false | Specify whether the event is virtual or not. |
| Inherited Tag | Type | Description |
| accessSpecifier «C++CLIClassMember» | CLIvisibilityKind[0..1] (Enumeration) See | Represents the usage of visibility kind introduced in C++/CLI including:internalprotected publicpublic protectedprivate protectedprotected private |

##### Enumeration

CLI typeCLI Type

**CLI Type** will be used as value of tag named "**CLI Type**" under «C++CLIClass». The possible values are **ref** and **value.**

| Literal | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ref | For defining a CLR reference class |
| value | For defining a CLR value class |

operationoverridespecifieroperation OverrideSpecifier

**operationOverrideSpecifier**will be used as value of tag named "**overrideSpecifier**" under**«VC++Operation»**. The possible values are **new** and **override** which are keywords that can be used to qualify override behavior for derivation.

| Literal | Description |
| --- | --- |
| new | Indicates the use of a new keyword to qualify override behavior for derivation. In VC++, new is a keyword indicating that a virtual member will get a new slot in the table; the function does not override a base class method. |
| override | Indicates the use of the override key to qualify override behavior for derivation. In VC++, override is a keyword indicating that a member of a managed type must override a base class or a base interface member. If there is no member to override, the compiler will generate an error. |

attributefieldattributeField

**attributeField** will be used as the value of the tag named "field" under **«C++CLIAttribute»**. The possible values are initonly and literal, keywords used to qualify the field type of attribute.

| Literal | Description |
| --- | --- |
| initonly | Represents the utilization of initonly keyword. initonly indicates that variable assignment can only occur as part of the declaration or in a static constructor in the same class. |
| literal | Represents the utilization of literal keyword. It is the native equivalent of static const variable. Constraint: Is Static and Is Read Only must be set to true. |

classkeyclassKey

| Literal | Description |
| --- | --- |
| class | Represents the keyword that has the word, class. |
| struct | Represents the keyword that has the word, struct. |

Remark:

- enum class and enum struct are equivalent declarations.
- interface class and interface struct are equivalent declarations.

CLIvisibilitykindCLIvisibilityKind

| Literal | Description |
| --- | --- |
| internal | Represents the internal visibility. |
| protected public | Represents the protected public visibility. |
| public protected | Represents the public protected visibility. |
| private protected | Represents the private protected visibility. |
| protected private | Represents the protected private visibility. |

genericconstrainttypegenericConstraintType

| Literal | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ref class | Represents the usage of the ref class keyword in a generic constraint clause. |
| ref struct | Represents the usage of the ref struct keyword in a generic constraint clause. |
| value class | Represents the usage of the value class keyword in a generic constraint clause. |
| value struct | Represents the usage of the value strict keyword in a generic constraint clause. |
| gcnew | Represents the usage of the gcnew keyword in a generic constraint clause. |

CLIgeneralizationvisibilityC++CLIGeneralizationVisibility

C++CLIGeneralizationVisibility is an enumeration inheriting from C++GeneralizationVisibility and CLIvisibility- Kind. Its literals include:

- none,
- public,
- protected,
- private,
- internal,
- protected public,
- public protected,
- private protected, and
- protected private .

colspan="1">propertyType</td><td colspan="1">PropertyType[1] = property data member</td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Specify property type between:</span></p><ul><li>property data member</li><li>property block with get</li><li>property block with set</li><li>property block with get and set </li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>Inherited Tag</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p>accessSpecifier «C++CLIClassMember»</p></td><td><p>CLIvisibilityKind[0..1] (Enumeration)</p><p>See <ac:link ac:anchor="CLIvisibilitykind"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CLIvisibilityKind]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="C++/CLI Profile" /></ac:link></p></td><td><p>Represents the usage of visibility kind introduced in C++/CLI including:</p><ul><li>internal</li><li>protected public</li><li>public protected</li><li>private protected</li><li>protected private </li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>C++CLIDelegate</span></p><hr /><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">Name</th><th colspan="1">Meta Class</th><th colspan="1">Constraints</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">C++CLIDelegate</td><td colspan="1">Class</td><td colspan="1"><p><span>Declaration of delegate can be only in a managed type. </span>The declaration of delegate cannot have attribute and operation. Represents the utilization of delegate keyword. </p></td></tr><tr><th>Tag</th><th>Type</th><th>Descriptions</th></tr><tr><td><p>C++CLIEvent</p></td><td><p>boolean[1] = false</p></td><td><p>Specify whether the event is virtual or not.</p></td></tr><tr><th>Inherited Tag</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><p>accessSpecifier «C++CLIClassMember»</p></td><td><p>CLIvisibilityKind[0..1] (Enumeration) See <ac:link ac:anchor="CLIvisibilitykind"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CLIvisibilityKind]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="C++/CLI Profile" /></ac:link></p></td><td><p>Represents the usage of visibility kind introduced in C++/CLI including:</p><ul><li>internal</li><li>protected public</li><li>public protected</li><li>private protected</li><li>protected private </li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>C++CLIGeneric</p><hr /><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">Name</th><th colspan="1">Meta Class</th><th colspan="1">Constraints</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">C++CLIGeneric</td><td colspan="1">Class, Interface, Operation, Property</td><td colspan="1"><p>Represents the usage of a <strong>generic</strong> keyword.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">C++CLIGenericParameteredElement</td><td colspan="1">Class</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><th>Tag</th><th>Type</th><th>Descriptions</th></tr><tr><td><p>genericConstraintType</p></td><td><p><span>genericConstraintType[0..*] (Enumeration) </span></p><p><span>See <span><ac:link ac:anchor="genericconstrainttype"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[genericConstraintType]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="C++/CLI Profile" /></ac:link></span></span></p></td><td><p>Specifies generic constraint type, including</p><ul><li>ref class</li><li>ref struct</li><li>value class</li><li>value struct</li><li>gcnew</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>C++CLIGeneralization</p><hr /><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">Name</th><th colspan="1">Meta Class</th><th colspan="1">Constraints</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">C++CLIGeneralizationParameteredElement</td><td colspan="1">Class</td><td colspan="1"><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr><th>Tag</th><th>Type</th><th>Descriptions</th></tr><tr><td><p>genericConstraintType</p></td><td><p>genericConstraintType[0..*] (Enumeration)</p><p>See <ac:link ac:anchor="genericconstrainttype"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[genericConstraintType]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="C++/CLI Profile" /></ac:link></p></td><td><p>Specifies generic constraint type, including</p><ul><li>ref class</li><li>ref struct</li><li>value class</li><li>value struct</li><li>gcnew</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>C++CLIGeneralization</p><hr /><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">Name</th><th colspan="1">Meta Class</th><th colspan="1">Constraints</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">C++CLIGeneralization</td><td colspan="1">Generalization</td><td colspan="1"><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr><th>Tag</th><th>Type</th><th>Descriptions</th></tr><tr><td><p>CLIInheritanceVisibility</p></td><td><p><span>C++CLIGeneralizationVisibility[1] = none </span>(Enumeration)</p><p>See <ac:link ac:anchor="CLIgeneralizationvisibility"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C++CLIGeneralizationVisibility ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="C++/CLI Profile" /></ac:link></p></td><td><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">virtualInheritance</td><td colspan="1">boolean[1] = false</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Enumeration</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdb01735-2c3a-4fbf-84bc-7e457b318cf2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">CLI type</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>CLI Type</p><hr /><p><strong>CLI Type</strong> will be used as value of tag named &quot;<strong>CLI Type</strong>&quot; under «C++CLIClass». The possible values are <strong>ref</strong> and <strong>value.</strong></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Literal</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>ref</td><td>For defining a CLR reference class</td></tr><tr><td>value</td><td>For defining a CLR value class</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4050b0ec-f27b-455d-8bc4-8a0dc3257cb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">operationoverridespecifier</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>operation OverrideSpecifier</p><hr /><p><strong>operationOverrideSpecifier </strong><span>will be used as value of tag named &quot;</span><strong>overrideSpecifier</strong><span>&quot; under </span><strong>«VC++Operation»</strong>. The possible values are <strong>new</strong> and <strong>override</strong> which are keywords that can be used to qualify override behavior for derivation. </p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Literal</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>new</td><td><p><span>Indicates the use of a </span><span><strong>new</strong> </span><span>keyword to qualify override behavior for derivation. </span>In VC++, <strong>new</strong> is a keyword indicating that a virtual member will get a new slot in the table; the function does not override a base class method. </p></td></tr><tr><td>override</td><td>Indicates the use of the <strong>override</strong> key to qualify override behavior for derivation. In VC++, <strong>override</strong> is a keyword indicating that a member of a managed type must override a base class or a base interface member. If there is no member to override, the compiler will generate an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d82cd40-0015-4fa2-b1f3-fade43c313e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">attributefield</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>attributeField</p><hr /><p><br /></p><p><strong>attributeField</strong> will be used as the value of the tag named &quot;field&quot; under <strong>«C++CLIAttribute»</strong>. The possible values are initonly and literal, keywords used to qualify the field type of attribute.</p><table><colgroup><col style="width: 65.0px;" /><col style="width: 517.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Literal</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>initonly</td><td><p><span>Represents the utilization of </span><span><strong>initonly</strong> </span><span>keyword. </span><strong>initonly</strong> indicates that variable assignment can only occur as part of the declaration or in a static constructor in the same class. </p></td></tr><tr><td>literal</td><td><p><span>Represents the utilization of </span><span><strong>literal</strong> </span><span>keyword. It is the native equivalent of </span><strong>static const </strong><span>variable. Constraint: </span><strong>Is Static </strong><span>and </span><strong>Is Read Only </strong><span>must be set to </span><strong>true</strong><span><strong>.</strong> </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="184b7dc0-313b-4b35-8369-4b99f1182ddb"><ac:parameter ac:name="">classkey</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>classKey</p><hr /><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Literal</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>class</td><td><p>Represents the keyword that has the word, <strong>class.</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td>struct</td><td><p>Represents the keyword that has the word, <strong>struct.</strong></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><u>Remark: </u></p><ul><li><strong>enum class</strong> and <strong>enum struct</strong> are equivalent declarations. </li><li><strong>interface class</strong> and <strong>interface struct</strong> are equivalent declarations. </li></ul><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8b9f38d-f024-4a27-adfb-816ca64ab4b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">CLIvisibilitykind</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>CLIvisibilityKind</span></p><hr /><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Literal</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>internal</td><td><p>Represents the <strong>internal</strong> visibility.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>protected public</p></td><td><p>Represents the <strong>protected public</strong> visibility.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">public protected</td><td colspan="1">Represents the <strong>public protected</strong> visibility.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">private protected</td><td colspan="1">Represents the <strong>private protected</strong> visibility.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">protected private</td><td colspan="1">Represents the <strong>protected private</strong> visibility.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0097879a-e1f6-4d34-8154-bf1122fc4334"><ac:parameter ac:name="">genericconstrainttype</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>genericConstraintType</p><hr /><p><br /></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Literal</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>ref class</td><td><p>Represents the usage of the <strong>ref class</strong> keyword in a generic constraint clause.</p></td></tr><tr><td>ref struct</td><td><p>Represents the usage of the<strong> ref struct </strong>keyword in a generic constraint clause.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">value class</td><td colspan="1">Represents the usage of the <strong>value class</strong> keyword in a generic constraint clause.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">value struct</td><td colspan="1">Represents the usage of the <strong>value strict</strong> keyword in a generic constraint clause.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">gcnew</td><td colspan="1">Represents the usage of the <strong>gcnew</strong> keyword in a generic constraint clause.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c9f7aca-87cb-4851-9dce-6bd3ca5df443"><ac:parameter ac:name="">CLIgeneralizationvisibility</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>C++CLIGeneralizationVisibility</p><hr /><p><span>C++CLIGeneralizationVisibility is an enumeration inheriting from C++GeneralizationVisibility and CLIvisibility- Kind. Its literals include:</span></p><ul><li><strong>none,</strong></li><li><strong>public, </strong></li><li><strong>protected, </strong></li><li><strong>private, </strong></li><li><strong>internal, </strong></li><li><strong>protected public, </strong></li><li><strong>public protected, </strong></li><li><strong>private protected,</strong> and<strong> </strong></li><li><strong>protected private</strong><span>. </span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710406 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00138: Calculating metrics in the Metric Table

- page_id: `136710406`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710406/Calculating+metrics+in+the+Metric+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:37.748+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Metric table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

1908376956

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

1908376958

1908376955

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

1908376957

Once you have created the Metric Table, which represents the metrics calculated at the time you created, you can make further calculations and track the evolution of your model development. 
If you need to append new metrics to your table, you can easily open it.

##### Opening the Metric Table

To open the Metric Table

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] , right-click the element whose Metric Table you need to append.
2. On the shortcut menu of the element, click Tools > Metrics and then select the Metric Table you need to append. [ATTACHMENT filename='ms_open_metric_suite_menu.png']

The selected Metric Table opens. Now you are ready to calculate new metrics.

##### Calculating new metrics

To calculate new metrics

1. In the Metric Table, select the row. Below that, you need to add a row with newly calculated metrics.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - On the Edit toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_metrics_whole_button.png'] and then select Calculate New Metric [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_new_metric.png'] from the open menu.
  - Right-click the selected row and then click Calculate New Metric [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_new_metric.png'] on the shortcut menu.

The new metrics are calculated and represented in the table below the selected row.

#### NOTE: Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

By default, validation-based metrics are calculated in used read-only projects (auxiliary resources) as well. To exclude auxiliary resources from metric calculation scope, click[IMAGE alt='' src='']in the Metric Table toolbar and select the**Exclude Elements from Used Read-Only Projects**option.

##### Recalculating metrics

To recalculate metrics

1. In the Metric Table, select the row whose metrics you need to recalculate.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Edit toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_metrics_whole_button.png'] and then select Recalculate [IMAGE alt='' src=''] from the open menu.
  - Right-click the selected row and then click Recalculate [ATTACHMENT filename='recalculate.png'] on the shortcut menu.

The new metrics are calculated and represented in the table, instead of the selected row.

##### Calculating new metrics with different parameter values

To calculate new metrics with different parameter values

1. In the Metric Table, select the row. Below that, you need to add a row for representing newly calculated metrics.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Edit toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_metrics_whole_button.png'] and then select Add New Metric with Different Parameters [ATTACHMENT filename='new_metric_with_diff_pars.png'] from the open menu.
  - Right-click the selected row and then click Add New Metric with Different Parameters [ATTACHMENT filename='new_metric_with_diff_pars.png'] on the shortcut menu.
3. A new row with copied parameters is added to the Metric table. If the new row is empty, it might be that you haven’t selected any row. You may continue the procedure.
4. Change parameter values.
5. Select the newly created row and do one of the following:
  - On the Edit toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src='']and then select**Recalculate [IMAGE alt='' src='']**from the open menu.
  - Right-click the row and then select**Recalculate**[IMAGE alt='' src='']from the shortcut menu.

The new metrics are calculated according to the specified parameter values.

##### Calculating metrics when using multiple models

Metrics can be calculated when the model is stored in multiple projects. For example, the requirements are stored in the used project, and the blocks appears in the main project. If you want to calculate how many requirements are covered by system architecture blocks, you should run the calculations in the main project. Because in the main project you can see all model elements.

To calculate metrics when using multiple model

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MD2024x'], right-click the Requirements Package from the used project, select **Tools** > **Metrics** > **New Metric Table**. In the **Create Metric Table** dialog make sure, the scope and the Metric Table Owner is the same Package, as shown in the figure below. Click **OK**. Initially the scope for calculations is the same package where you choose to store the metrics. Therefore, the metrics may be not calculated on the metrics table creation. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dots.png'] and in the Select Package dialog, choose another scope and click OK . As shown in the figure below, the System Requirements Specification shared Package is selected. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_scope.png']
3. Re-calculate metrics of the first row.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The metrics are calculated by using multiple projects.

table, instead of the selected row.<br /><br /></p><h3>Calculating new metrics with different parameter values</h3><p><br /></p><p>To calculate new metrics with different parameter values</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Metric Table, select the row. Below that, you need to add a row for representing newly calculated metrics.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Edit toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="calc_metrics_whole_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then select <strong>Add New Metric with Different Parameters</strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_metric_with_diff_pars.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> from the open menu.</li><li>Right-click the selected row and then click <strong>Add New Metric with Different Parameters</strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_metric_with_diff_pars.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the shortcut menu.</li></ul></li><li><p>A new row with copied parameters is added to the Metric table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="276e088d-4f65-4ebc-9e66-04b1c2356f75"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the new row is empty, it might be that you haven’t selected any row. You may continue the procedure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Change parameter values.</li><li><p>Select the newly created row and do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">On the Edit toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="calc_metrics_whole_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and then select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Recalculate <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recalculate.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">from the open menu.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Right-click the row and then select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Recalculate</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recalculate.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> from the shortcut menu.</span></p></li></ul></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br />The new metrics are calculated according to the specified parameter values.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><h3 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Calculating metrics when using multiple models</h3><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Metrics can be calculated when the model is stored in multiple projects. For example, the requirements are stored in the used project, and the blocks appears in the main project. If you want to calculate how many requirements are covered by system architecture blocks, you should run the calculations in the main project. Because in the main project you can see all model elements.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To calculate metrics when using multiple model</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click the Requirements Package from the used project, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Metrics</strong> &gt; <strong>New Metric Table</strong>. In the <strong>Create Metric Table</strong> dialog make sure, the scope and the Metric Table Owner is the same Package, as shown in the figure below. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b495cf53-d765-4cdf-aa29-c672a9a56f00"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Initially the scope for calculations is the same package where you choose to store the metrics. Therefore, the metrics may be not calculated on the metrics table creation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_table_owner.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Click<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="13"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring Resource Usage Map URL" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and in the <strong>Select Package</strong> dialog, choose another scope and click <strong>OK</strong>. As shown in the figure below, the <em>System Requirements Specification</em> shared Package is selected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_scope.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Re-calculate metrics of the first row.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:height="159"><ri:attachment ri:filename="recalculate.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The metrics are calculated by using multiple projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710838 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00139: Call Behavior Action

- page_id: `136710838`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710838/Call+Behavior+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:02.533+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Action
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

673708054

673708057

673747585

**On this page**

43

673708056

##### Description

The Call Behavior Action invokes a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x'] directly rather than invoking a behavioral feature that, in turn, causes the Behavior. The argument values of the Action are available to the execution of the invoked Behavior. The execution of the Call Behavior Action waits until the execution of the invoked Behavior is completed and a result is returned on its Output Pin. In particular, the invoked Behavior can be an Activity.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Assigning a Behavior to the Call Behavior Action

To assign a Behavior to the Call Behavior Action, do one of the following

- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024x'] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='automatic_behavior_creation.png'] . An Activity will be created [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x'] .
- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] of the Call Behavior Action, and click the cell of the **Behavior**property value. Then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and, in the opened dialog, select the Behavior. Click **OK** when you are finished.
- On the Activity diagram, right-click the Call Behavior Action shape. On the shortcut menu, click **Behavior,** and then click to select a Behavior that you wish to assign on the element.
- On the Activity diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the opened list, select the Behavior.
- On the Activity diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, and then press Ctrl+T. In the opened list, select the Behavior.

- On the diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, then click the name area. Type the “:” colon, and then type the existing Behavior name. Press Enter, or click anywhere on the diagram. The Behavior is created and assigned.

Type the “:” colon, then press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+Backspace and then, in the opened list, select the Behavior.

- Drag a Behavior from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x'] onto the Call Behavior Action shape on the diagram. The Behavior is assigned to the Call Behavior Action.

- Double click the Call Behavior Action with the assigned Behavior - the Behavior [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] opens, or, if the assigned Behavior is a diagram, the diagram opens in the same diagram tab.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying rake icon' space='MD2024x'] [ATTACHMENT filename='rake.png'] is displayed on the shape if the Call Behavior Action refers to another Activity diagram. The rake icon is not shown by default, therefore, you need to set the Show Rake Icon property value to true in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog .
- A parameter is created automatically for each newly created Pin on the Call Behavior Action. The parameter’s name, type, direction, and multiplicity are synchronized with the Pin name, type, direction, and multiplicity. Note that a parameter for a pin is created automatically if:
  - the Call Behavior Action has the Behavior assigned.
  - the Behavior is editable.
  - the Behavior has only one usage, and it is not used in another project (in server projects).

##### Creating a new diagram for the Call Behavior Action

To create a new diagram for the Call Behavior Action, do one of the following

- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x'] , click the Call Behavior Action shape, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='activity_diagram_smart_manipulator.png'] .The new Activity diagram opens in the same diagram tab.

- The name of the new Activity diagram is the same as the name of the Call Behavior Action.
- If a Behavior was already assigned for the Call Behavior Action, a new Activity diagram under the Behavior will be created. This is valid if the Behavior is editable and the Behavior’s type is Activity.
- If Pin(s) are created on the Call Behavior Action, then the parameters for these Pins are created in a newly created activity. Pins are synchronized with the created parameters, i.e., the Pin name, type, and multiplicity are synchronized with the parameter name, type, and multiplicity.
- If parameters are created for a new Activity, then Activity parameter Nodes are created in a new Activity diagram and displayed on the Activity diagram frame.

- On the Activity diagram, right-click the Call Behavior Action shape, point to **Create Diagram,** and then point to a diagram type.

##### Changing the Name Display Mode on the Call Behavior Action symbol

To change the Name Display Mode on the Call Behavior Action symbol

- Right-click the Call Behavior Action and select the **Symbol Properties**command.
- In the****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog, find the**Name Display Mode** property.
- Select one of the following value: 
 - **Show Action Name** (default only for UPDM projects) - to show action name. 
 - **Show Behavior Name**to show behavior name.**** 
 - **Show Both** - to show action and behavior names.**** 
 - **Show Both or Behavior Name** (default) - to show only the behavior name if the action name is not specified, or if the action name matches the behavior name.

- Click **Close**. 
The selected name is shown on the Call Behavior Action symbol.

673708053

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']

to show action name.<br /> - <strong>Show Behavior Name </strong>to show behavior name.<strong> </strong><br /> - <strong>Show Both</strong> - to show action and behavior names.<strong> </strong><br /> - <strong>Show Both or Behavior Name</strong> (default) - to show only the behavior name if the action name is not specified, or if the action name matches the behavior name.</p></li></ul><ul><li><p>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The selected name is shown on the Call Behavior Action symbol.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cfd6768-5795-4d08-a9d2-c86fb5deb299"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">673708053</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710841 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00140: Call Operation Action

- page_id: `136710841`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710841/Call+Operation+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:02.669+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Action
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907090850

907090852

907090851

The Call Operation Action transmits an operation call request to the target object, where it can cause the invocation of an associated [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x'].

You can display an Action name and/or name of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MD2024x'] on the Call Operation Action shape. For example, if you have two Call Operation Action elements calling the same Operation, you can specify their names to distinguish the Actions from each other.

| Notation | Description |
| --- | --- |
| The Call Operation Action with the getOrder operation | In this example, the Call Operation Action has been assigned for the getOrder operation whose type is the OrdersDB class. |
| The Call Operation Action named Get supplementary order | In this example, the Call Operation Action is named Get supplementary order. It has been assigned for getOrder operation whose type is the OrdersDB class. |
| The Call Operation Action with the hidden classified name | The classified name of an operation is hidden from the Call Operation Action shape. |

To assign an Operation for the Call Operation Action, do one of the following

- On the diagram, right-click the Call Operation Action, and, on the shortcut menu, click Select Operation . In the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024x']**Select Operation** dialog , select an Operation you need to assign to the element, or click the Creation Mode button to create a new Operation. Click OK when you are finished.
- In the Call Operation Action [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] , click to select the Operation property specification cell, and click button [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . In the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024x']**Select Operation** dialog , select the Operation you need to assign to the element, or click the Creation Mode button to create a new Operation. Click OK then you are finished.

MagicDraw allows you to display the Operation name and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x']of the Operation on the Call Operation Action shape.

When the Operation is assigned to the Call Operation Action, there are two name/operation display options available:

| Description | Example |
| --- | --- |
| If the Call Operation Action is not named, or the name of the Call Operation Action is the same as the assigned Operation name, the name of the Class is displayed under the Operation name. |  |
| If the Call Operation Action name differs from the assigned Operation name, then <class of the operation>::<operation name> is displayed under the Call Operation Action name. |  |

To show / hide the Operation name and Class of the Operation from the Call Operation Action shape

- Open the Call Operation Action [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog , and set the Show Qualified Name for Operation property value to true / false.

907090849

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="561c44f5-841a-468c-9e5c-338d883f8311"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907090850</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d61a0c3-f738-4b19-a137-903af5e9ca10"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907090852</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49e8cd79-9e26-4428-816b-4030ec75cd89"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907090851</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Call Operation Action transmits an operation call request to the target object, where it can cause the invocation of an associated <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link>.</p><p>You can display an Action name and/or name of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link> on the Call Operation Action shape. For example, if you have two Call Operation Action elements calling the same Operation, you can specify their names to distinguish the Actions from each other.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Notation</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The Call Operation Action with the <em>getOrder</em> operation</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_op_action_sample1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Call Operation Action" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>In this example, the Call Operation Action has been assigned for the <em>getOrder </em>operation whose type is the <em>OrdersDB</em> class.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The Call Operation Action named <em>Get supplementary </em><em>order</em></p><p><em><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_op_action_sample2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Call Operation Action" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></em></p></div></td><td><p>In this example, the Call Operation Action is named <em>Get supplementary </em><em>order</em>. It has been assigned for <em>getOrder</em> operation whose type is the <em>OrdersDB</em> class.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The Call Operation Action with the hidden classified name</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_op_action_sample3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Call Operation Action" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>The classified name of an operation is hidden from the Call Operation Action shape.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p>To assign an Operation for the Call Operation Action, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram, right-click the Call Operation Action, and, on the shortcut menu, click <strong>Select Operation</strong>. In the opened <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Operation</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select an Operation you need to assign to the element, or click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong> button to create a new Operation. Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are finished.</li><li>In the Call Operation Action <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, click to select the <strong>Operation</strong> property specification cell, and click button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. In the opened <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Operation</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the Operation you need to assign to the element, or click the <strong>Creation Mode</strong> button to create a new Operation. Click <strong>OK</strong> then you are finished.</li></ul><p>MagicDraw allows you to display the Operation name and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the Operation on the Call Operation Action shape.</p><p>When the Operation is assigned to the Call Operation Action, there are two name/operation display options available:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Description</th><th>Example</th></tr><tr><td><p>If the Call Operation Action is not named, or the name of the Call Operation Action is the same as the assigned Operation name, the name of the Class is displayed under the Operation name.</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_op_action_sample1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Call Operation Action" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>If the Call Operation Action name differs from the assigned Operation name, then <em>&lt;class of the operation&gt;::&lt;operation name&gt;</em> is displayed under the Call Operation Action name.</p></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_op_action_sample2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Call Operation Action" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><p>To show / hide the Operation name and Class of the Operation from the Call Operation Action shape</p><hr /><ul><li>Open the Call Operation Action <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, and set the<strong> Show Qualified Name for Operation</strong> property value to <em>true</em> / false.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32a7bbe8-0422-41af-aff3-11f2c5077bb2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907090849</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712396 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00141: Calling operations from the model

- page_id: `136712396`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712396/Calling+operations+from+the+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:32.512+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Creating new operations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1325379609

1325379611

1325379608

**On this page**

****

1325379610

In a structured expression, you can call an operation defined in the model. An operation from the model can be either built-in or custom. The built-in operations, such as Union, Find, or Filter, are stored as executable opaque behaviors in the *Built-In Operations* package, which is owned by *UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Be sure the **Exclude Used Projects** command on the Filter Options button[IMAGE alt='' src=''] menu is unchecked!

1325389280

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='MD2024x']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating script operations' space='MD2024x']

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36cdc1d7-9a16-45e3-a0f9-fab1835cc33e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325379609</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6e695dc-d260-4cb6-8aa5-735ece55345a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325379611</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36f940b9-32ed-4620-8486-7a2fe162cd51"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325379608</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4740461d-120b-4365-a5bb-acea529e16e9" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fd86cdc-93a0-4623-b0c7-3f3eed808853"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325379610</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In a structured expression, you can call an operation defined in the model. An operation from the model can be either built-in or custom. The built-in operations, such as Union, Find, or Filter, are stored as executable opaque behaviors in the <em>Built-In Operations</em> package, which is owned by <em>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile</em>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_opaque_behavior_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calling operations from the model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1669fbc7-3eea-4fe1-9ceb-e3edc9bfdf3b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be sure the <strong>Exclude Used Projects</strong> command on the Filter Options <span>button</span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter.gif"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Calling operations from the model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> menu is unchecked!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e81bef9b-1159-4066-ba5a-61ec11c4b409"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325389280</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating new operations" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating script operations" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712592 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00142: Case 1. Coloring Ports by Type using Contains operation

- page_id: `136712592`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712592/Case+1.+Coloring+Ports+by+Type+using+Contains+operation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:40.911+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

967284465

967284478

967284468

To color ports by their type

1. Create a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='MD2024x']** and a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space='MD2024x']**.
2. Right-click the Legend Item to open its Specification window .
3. C lick three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Adornment property to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Item adornment' space='MD2024x'] . Click OK .
4. Click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Elements by Condition**property to edit it. **Elements by Condition** dialog opens.
5. Select Create operation > **Metachain Navigation** .
6. Click **Insert**.
7. Under **Metaclass or Stereotype**, select **Port**, and under**Property**, select****Type****. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Select Create operation > **Operation from Model** > **Contains** .
9. Drag-and-drop Metachain Navigation onto Input parameter. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-52-55.png']
10. Select Obj > Element > select the port type to verify. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-58-25.png']
11. Click OK .

967284464

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click three dots <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 1. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712435 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00143: Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date

- page_id: `136712435`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712435/Case+10.+Filtering+Diagrams+by+Modification+Date
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:37.013+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Groovy

1360697410

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Groovy

1360697431

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Groovy

1360697421

To filter diagrams by modification date

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Generic Table' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Set the Element Type to Diagram . Make sure to select the Include Subtypes check box. Click OK .
3. Set the Scope where the diagrams will be searched for.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon****in the**Scope**field and s elect **Custom** . The Query dialog opens.
5. Select **Create operation** > **Filter**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Select Filter > Input > Find . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_case_10.png']
7. Set the Type to Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_case_10.png']
8. Expand Find > Scope > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_case_10.png']
9. Select **Filter**> **Predicate** > **Operation from Model** > **GreaterThanOrEquals**. If you cannot see the **GreaterThanOrEquals** operation, make sure the**Apply Filter**box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select Predicate > Body > A > Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='7_case_10.png']
11. From the Language drop-down list, select **Groovy**.
12. Insert the following script as the **Body**: Groovy [IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. Select A > arg1 > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='9_case_10.png']
14. Select A > arg1 > Simple Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='10_case_10.png']
15. Select Modification date and set Is Applied to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='11_case_10.png']
16. Select Predicate > Body > B > Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='12_case_10.png']
17. From the Language drop-down list, select **Groovy**.
18. Insert the following script as the **Body**: Groovy [IMAGE alt='' src='']
19. Click **OK**. To view the column, make sure to display it via the **Columns** button in the diagram toolbar > **Select Columns** > set the **Modification date** column to **true**.

1360697408

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7157f9f9-8655-4f0e-8069-aa50cb9003c7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1360697410</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5056772-6423-4254-92c1-528589506473"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1360697431</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bac1b615-e714-403d-9e08-3c48965987fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1360697421</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To filter diagrams by modification date</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Generic Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generic Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Element Type</strong> to <strong>Diagram</strong>. Make sure to select the <strong>Include Subtypes</strong> check box. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Scope</strong> where the diagrams will be searched for.</li><li><span>Click the</span> <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="scope_query.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </span> <span>icon<strong> </strong>in the<strong> Scope </strong>field and s</span>elect<strong> <strong>Custom</strong></strong>. The <strong>Query</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select <strong>Create operation</strong> &gt; <strong>Filter</strong>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="2_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Select <strong>Filter</strong> &gt; <strong>Input</strong> &gt; <strong>Find</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Set the <strong>Type</strong> to <strong>Diagram</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="4_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Expand <strong>Find</strong> &gt; <strong>Scope</strong> &gt; <strong>Contextual Variable</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="5_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select <strong>Filter </strong>&gt; <strong>Predicate</strong> &gt; <strong>Operation from Model</strong> &gt; <strong>GreaterThanOrEquals</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0e2f652-a487-4f4e-bc0b-62e4c9eed857"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you cannot see the <strong>GreaterThanOrEquals</strong></span> <span>operation, make sure the </span><strong>Apply Filter</strong><span> box is not checked.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="6_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Select <strong>Predicate</strong> &gt; <strong>Body </strong>&gt; <strong>A</strong> &gt; <strong>Script</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>From the Language drop-down list, select <strong>Groovy</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Insert the following script as the <strong>Body</strong>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2fb1c74-986b-4a37-9436-7648f3cf9cd1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Groovy</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import java.text.DateFormat;
import java.util.Date;

if (arg1) {
Date date1 = DateFormat.getDateInstance(DateFormat.SHORT).parse(arg1.iterator().next()); 
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="8_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Select <strong>A </strong>&gt; <strong>arg1</strong> &gt; <strong>Reset</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="9_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>A </strong>&gt; <strong>arg1</strong> &gt; <strong>Simple Navigation</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="10_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Modification date </strong>and set <strong>Is Applied</strong> to <strong>true</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="11_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Predicate</strong> &gt; <strong>Body </strong>&gt; <strong>B</strong> &gt; <strong>Script</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="12_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>From the Language drop-down list, select <strong>Groovy</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Insert the following script as the <strong>Body</strong>:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84e57745-fee0-486b-8739-fb0bc50925b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Groovy</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Date M1 = new Date();
M1.setMonth(M1.getMonth() - 1);
M1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="13_case_10.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>. To view the column, make sure to display it via the <strong>Columns</strong> button in the diagram toolbar &gt; <strong>Select Columns</strong> &gt; set the <strong>Modification date</strong> column to <strong>true</strong>.</p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f0052cb-1f55-4f85-8d46-48785e9a4820"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1360697408</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc224fd4-55dd-45d0-a18c-365f86d8ad24"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712450 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00144: Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram

- page_id: `136712450`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712450/Case+11.+Server+Project+Version+in+Diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:37.387+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: JavaScript Rhino

1476040892

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: JavaScript Rhino

1476040912

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: JavaScript Rhino

1476040902

To retrieve the Server Project Version in a Diagram

1. Right-click the empty space on the diagram pane and open the Specification window .
2. On the left side of the dialog, select Traceability > Create . Expression dialog opens.
3. Select **Create operation**>******Script**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the Name for the derived property.
5. Set the Type as String .
6. Specify the Operation Name .
7. From the Language drop-down list, select JavaScript Rhino .
8. Insert the following script as the **Body**. JavaScript Rhino [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click **OK**.

To display the Server Project Version in a Diagram

1. Create the **Note** symbol in the diagram and anchor it to the diagram.
2. Right-click the **Note** symbol and deselect **Represent Diagram Context (Package)**.
3. (Optional) Right-click the **Note** symbol and deselect **Apply SysML Callout Style**.
4. Right-click the **Note** symbol and select **Edit Compartments**.
5. In the **Element Properties** tab of the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the name of the property you created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click OK . You can see the property name and the server project version in the **Note** symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1361035242

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e097c4b1-b463-4d21-ae0a-c523a6c4048b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040892</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac4b068f-5ee1-4baa-b399-0cc53ce9af2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040912</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0705b70d-5668-46d9-bd64-3b74603d81fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040902</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To retrieve the Server Project Version in a Diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the empty space on the diagram pane and open the <strong>Specification</strong> <strong>window</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the dialog, select <strong>Traceability</strong> &gt; <strong>Create</strong>. <strong>Expression</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select <strong>Create operation </strong>&gt;<strong> </strong><strong>Script</strong>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_create_operation_script.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Specify the <strong>Name</strong> for the derived property.</li><li>Set the <strong>Type</strong> as <strong>String</strong>.</li><li>Specify the <strong>Operation Name</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Language </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">drop-down list, select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">JavaScript Rhino</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Insert the following script as the <strong>Body</strong></span></span>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7534e3e7-a876-4dd7-92cb-a43fd7a08dde"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">JavaScript Rhino</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[var prj = com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.getInstance().getProject().getPrimaryProject();
var ver = com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities.getVersion(prj);
ver.getName();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_expression_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To display the Server Project Version in a Diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create the <strong>Note</strong> symbol in the diagram and </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">anchor it to the diagram.<br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and deselect <strong>Represent Diagram Context (Package)</strong>.<br /></span></li><li><span>(Optional) Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and deselect <strong>Apply SysML Callout Style</strong>.</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab of the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog, select the name of the property you created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="5_edit_compartment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color: rgb(62,63,64);">OK</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. You can see the property name and the server project version in the <strong>Note</strong> symbol.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="6_display_server_project_version.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea815d25-8915-4e8d-8447-68b1b6b3121d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1361035242</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7cf102d3-32cc-4d79-9f1d-6e4906d9ae99"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00145: Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table

- page_id: `136712461`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712461/Case+12.+Activity+Decomposition+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:37.617+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1660070392

1660070413

1660070403

To create an Activity Decomposition Table

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Generic Table' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Set the Element Type to Activity .
3. Set the Scope to the Activity element for which decomposition hierarchy will be displayed.
4. Open the Specification window of the Generic Table and click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Query property to edit it.
5. In the **Query** dialog, select **Custom**.
6. Select **Metachain Navigation**.
7. Click **Insert**.
8. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select CallBehaviorAction , and under Property , select Behavior . [ATTACHMENT filename='1_metachain_navigation.png']
9. Select **Context** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select **Context** > **Operation from Model**>**Behavior Decomposition**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
11. Select **Element** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
12. Select **Element** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. Select the **Build Hierarchy** check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building a multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root.
14. Click **OK**.

1361105380

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true
