<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713259 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00174: Change Sets

- page_id: `136713259`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713259/Change+Sets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:22.653+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Reviewing model history
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editing elements

1192448784

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editing elements

1192448786

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editing elements

1192448785

While editing your server project, you can track project modifications in the **Change Sets**panel, located right after the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x']. A change set is a related group of elements, diagrams and symbol modifications in a project that are created or modified locally and not yet committed to the server. You may have several sets for your project and commit them one by one. This feature is useful when, for example, you are working on a server project and receive a request for an immediate change. You can suspend your changes, complete the immediate task and commit the changes, after which you can resume your workon the previously suspended change set. This also applies when you are working simultaneously on different tasks that are required to be delivered as separate commits.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A set of changes made to a server project.

#### NOTE: Editing elements

Editing elements

Ensure that you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2024x'] to enable editing.

You can create as many change sets as you need. To organize and identify change sets, you may name each one. Only one change set can be active at a time. In order to create a new change set, all other change sets must be suspended. As shown in the preceding figure, colors on an element in the change set indicate whether the edit is an addition, modification or deletion. Also, you may [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comparing projects' space='MD2024x'] between the base project and a project with modifications applied.

To manage an *active* change set

- Right click an active change set and select:
  - Commit - to commit changes to the server
  - Rename - to name a change set
  - Suspend - to suspend a change set for further editions.
  - Discard - to discard the local changes in a change set. Note: all changes will be removed and this action cannot be undone.
  - Tag version with Change ID after Commit - for the Change ID to be automatically reflected in the Project History.

To manage *suspended* change sets

- Right click a suspended change set and select:
  - Resume - to resume working with a suspended change set. Ensure that the active change set is suspended before resuming work on another change set.
  - Discard - to discard the local changes in a change set. Note: all changes will be removed and this action cannot be undone.

To compare a project

1. Select the active change set.
2. On the toolbar of the Change Sets panel, click [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_diff_button.png'] . The list containing three types of comparison appears. Click Compare Projects .
3. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing comparison results' space='MD2024x']**Difference Viewer**dialog opens showing changes in element property values as well as detailed information of directly related elements and new relationships.

##### Resuming change sets in Lock-Free Editing mode

In some cases, it may not be possible to resume the suspended change sets in the Lock-Free Editing mode without taking additional steps. The reason why this happens is the detection of conflicts among the suspended changes and present project version elements. In such a case, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing conflicting changes' space='MD2024x'] before you can resume the changes and continue working on them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resuming suspended change sets.

The project version with the suspended change set has to be opened to review and confirm the update result of the conflicting changes. You can simply do so by clicking the **Review Conflicts** button in the warning that appears:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dialog asking to return to the project with the suspended change set version.

1192448783

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing comparison results' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710648 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00175: Changing a path style

- page_id: `136710648`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710648/Changing+a+path+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:49.871+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['path-style', 'rectilinear', 'oblique', 'bezier']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change a path style, do either

- Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select Reset Labels Positions or Remove Break Points .
- Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_break_points.png'] , [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_labels_positions.png'] , or [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
- Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the Edit menu click Paths , and then choose one of the following:
  - Path Style to select one of the path styles - rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.
  - Change Path Style (shortcut keys Ctrl+L) to change the path style to rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.
  - Reset Labels Positions to reset the changed path labels position to default.
  - Remove Break Points to remove all angles of the path.
- Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select Symbol Properties . In the Symbol Properties dialog, change the Path Style property value by selecting rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To change a path style, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select <strong>Reset Labels Positions</strong> or <strong>Remove Break Points</strong>.</li><li>Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Remove Break Points" ac:alt="Remove Break Points"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_break_points.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, <ac:image ac:title="Reset Labels Positions" ac:alt="Reset Labels Positions"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_labels_positions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, or<strong> <ac:image ac:title="Rectilinear" ac:alt="Rectilinear"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rectilinear.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</li><li>Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the <strong>Edit</strong> menu click <strong>Paths</strong>, and then choose one of the following:<br /><ul><li><strong>Path Style</strong> to select one of the path styles - rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li><li><strong>Change Path Style</strong> (shortcut keys Ctrl+L) to change the path style to rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li><li><strong>Reset Labels Positions </strong>to reset the changed path labels position to default.</li><li><strong>Remove Break Points</strong> to remove all angles of the path.</li></ul></li><li>Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, change the <strong>Path Style</strong> property value by selecting rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710634 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00176: Changing diagram type

- page_id: `136710634`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710634/Changing+diagram+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:48.467+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The diagram type can be changed to another type of diagram if both diagram types are compatible.

#### NOTE: Diagram Elements

Diagram Elements

Diagram elements are not converted.

###### **Uses for changing the diagram type:**

- To migrate with the existing project to a diagram type that was not available until then. For example, migrating a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024x'] .
- To migrate with the existing project from the diagram type, which will be erased from your project. For example, if the user has decided to erase a plugin, they would need to convert that plugin's specific diagram to standard diagrams.

###### **Diagram conversion scenarios:**

- Any static diagram can be converted to another static diagram.
- Any dynamic diagram can be converted to another diagram if and only if both diagrams are based on the same diagram type and both diagrams are compatible.

To change the diagram type

1. Select one or more of the same type diagrams in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] (Containment, Structure, or Diagram tree).
2. From the shortcut menu, choose Refactor > Change Type To . In the open dialog, select the desired diagram type. [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-06 at 5.02.07 PM.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">The diagram type can be changed to another type of diagram if both diagram types are compatible.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10c128c-e709-43cf-b861-e3c7eba7699c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Diagram Elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Diagram elements are not converted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Uses for changing the diagram type:</strong></h4><ul><li>To migrate with the existing project to a diagram type that was not available until then. For example, migrating a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Block Definition diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>To migrate with the existing project from the diagram type, which will be erased from your project. For example, if the user has decided to erase a plugin, they would need to convert that plugin's specific diagram to standard diagrams. </li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Diagram conversion scenarios:</strong></h4><ul><li>Any static diagram can be converted to another static diagram. </li><li>Any dynamic diagram can be converted to another diagram if and only if both diagrams are based on the same diagram type and both diagrams are compatible.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the diagram type</p><hr /><ol><li>Select one or more of the same type diagrams in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> (Containment, Structure, or Diagram tree).</li><li>From the shortcut menu, choose <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt;<strong> Change Type To</strong>. In the open dialog, select the desired diagram type.<br /><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-06 at 5.02.07 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Changing diagram type" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709283 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00177: Changing interface style

- page_id: `136709283`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709283/Changing+interface+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:40.780+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started > Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Look and Feel Themes

1426180851

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Look and Feel Themes

1426180854

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Look and Feel Themes

1426180852

#### EXCERPT: Look and Feel Themes

INLINE

You can change the appearance of your modeling tool windows, dialogs, and menus. The **Look and Feel** menu allows you to personalize the user interface using a number of predefined styles and themes. You can also create a custom theme with your favorite colors and fonts.

To change the interface style

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Look and Feel .
2. Select the desired style from the list of available styles.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Look and Feel menu options.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

- The selected style may not look exactly like the style in related operating systems.
- Depending on your operating system, some style options may be unavailable.

##### Creating a custom theme

Define your own interface style by creating a custom theme. The **Custom** theme option is located in the **Look and Feel Themes** submenu which also offers 8 predefined themes. The **Custom** theme allows you to specify text fonts and sizes of the desired interface elements as well as their colors.

To create a custom theme

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Look and Feel > Metal .
2. In the same menu, select Options > Look and Feel > Look and Feel Themes > Custom .
3. In the open dialog, change the values of the desired style properties.
4. Click OK .

#### NOTE: Look and Feel Themes

Look and Feel Themes

The themes listed in the **Look and Feel Themes** submenu are only available if you select the **Metal** style.

1426180850

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715218 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00178: Changing path break points

- page_id: `136715218`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715218/Changing+path+break+points
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:46:58.240+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change a break points list for the given path element, use the method *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.changePathBreakPoints(PathElement, java.util.List)*. The same method must be used if you want to change a client or supplier connection point.

The following code snippet shows how to do this:

```java

```

.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To change a break points list for the given path element, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.changePathBreakPoints(PathElement, java.util.List)</em>. The same method must be used if you want to change a client or supplier connection point.</p><p>The following code snippet shows how to do this:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81eb7a61-b3f9-4fd8-b334-5374f9e7d02b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    PathElement element = ...;
    ArrayList points = new ArrayList(); 
    points.add(new Point(100, 100));
    points.add(new Point(100, 150));
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession("Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().changePathBreakPoints(element, points);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>The order of points in the list must be from the supplier to client connection point. The list may or may not include the client and supplier connection points. At first the given points list will be adopted for the current path style (Rectilinear, Bezier<em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">. </em>or Oblique) and only then applied for the path.</div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7d0be1b-f166-46b8-8b28-4554d9e0577c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715220 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00179: Changing properties of presentation elements

- page_id: `136715220`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715220/Changing+properties+of+presentation+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:08:35.048+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Most of presentation elements can have properties (for example *Autosize* for shapes or *Path Style*for paths):

- To check if an element has some properties (or uses properties from its parent), use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.useParentProperties() .
- To get all properties of this element, use the method PresentationElement.getPropertyManager() .
- To get a property of this element with a given ID, use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.getProperty(java.lang.String) .
- To change element properties, use the method com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.setPresentationElementProperties( PresentationElement, PropertyManager) . The given com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager can have only few element’s properties (for example, just properties you want to change).

The Ids of all used properties are defined in the class *com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyID*.

Look at [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space=''] page for more details about properties structure.

There is no information about what exactly properties every presentation element has. Use java debugger and analyze *PresentationElement.getPropertyManager()* object. You can also guess property ID from the Symbol Properties name.

If you want to change a value of existing property, recommendation would be clone that property, change value for a clone and set it. Don't use constructor if possible.

The following code example shows how to change element properties:

```java

```

The properties must be new instances. You cannot do something like this:

```java

```

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>Most of presentation elements can have properties (for example <em>Autosize</em> for shapes or <em>Path Style </em>for paths):</p><ul><li>To check if an element has some properties (or uses properties from its parent), use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.useParentProperties()</em>.</li><li>To get all properties of this element, use the method <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">PresentationElement.getPropertyManager()</em>.</li><li>To get a property of this element with a given ID, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.getProperty(java.lang.String)</em>.</li><li>To change element properties, use the method <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager.setPresentationElementProperties(</em><em>PresentationElement, PropertyManager)</em>. The given <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyManager</em> can have only few element’s properties (for example, just properties you want to change).</li></ul><p>The Ids of all used properties are defined in the class <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.PropertyID</em>.</p><p>Look at <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link> page for more details about properties structure.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76534000-96db-443a-afbc-2a32a34c27d5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>There is no information about what exactly properties every presentation element has. Use java debugger and analyze <em>PresentationElement.getPropertyManager()</em> object. You can also guess property ID from the Symbol Properties name.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36c6fcc7-ed86-41f2-8ecf-5fe252f7261c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to change a value of existing property, recommendation would be clone that property, change value for a clone and set it. Don't use constructor if possible.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following code example shows how to change element properties:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb4de8c9-109d-46bb-a8d8-dd03610cf555"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    Property property = element.getProperty(PropertyID.AUTOSIZE);
    if (property instanceof BooleanProperty)
    {
        BooleanProperty autoSize = (BooleanProperty) property.clone();
        autoSize.setValue(true);

        PropertyManager properties = new PropertyManager();
        properties.addProperty(autoSize);

        SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Test");
        PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().setPresentationElementProperties(element, properties);
        SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9153917b-9322-4ccd-bd7f-879d217fcb51"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The properties must be new instances. You cannot do something like this: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad358495-8ca5-47aa-abba-1c0b8258ebc9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ShapeElement element = ...;
    PropertyManager properties = element.getPropertyManager();
    properties.getProperty(PropertyID.AUTOSIZE).setValue(new Boolean(true));
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Test");
    PresentationElementsManager.getInstance().setPresentationElementProperties(element, properties);
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa467e03-adb1-48ac-9d6c-74ab1f87088b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711341 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00180: Changing state to composite/submachine/orthogonal state

- page_id: `136711341`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711341/Changing+state+to+composite+submachine+orthogonal+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:34.036+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['changing-states']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change your current State to a simple State, composite State, orthogonal State, and submachine State. 
 
 #simpletocompositeTo change a State to a composite State

- Drag a vertex on a simple State symbol. A region is created and the simple State is converted to a composite State.

To change a State to a orthogonal State

- Add two regions to the State. Read more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024x'].

To change a State to a submachine State

1. On the diagram, right-click the State, then on the shortcut menu, click **Submachine**.
2. In the dialog, click to select a submachine that you wish to apply on the State or click the **Create** button, to create the submachine.

To change a State to a submachine State, the State must contain no regions.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709957 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00181: Changing the context

- page_id: `136709957`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709957/Changing+the+context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:17.782+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-change-context', 'relation-map-context']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Context is the element, from which a relation map is drawn and the analysis is started. You can set any element as the context.

To set an element as the context of the relation map

- Drag the selected element from the Model Browser to the relation map.
- In the Criteria area, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='select_context_element_button.png'] button next to the Context box and then select the element in the Select Context Element dialog.
- If the **Make Element as Context on Selection** option is turned on, click any node to make it the new context of the structure. To turn this option on, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the toolbar and select **Make Element as Context on Selection**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Context is the element, from which a relation map is drawn and the analysis is started. You can set any element as the context.</p><p>To set an element as the context of the relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag the selected element from the Model Browser to the relation map.</li><li>In the Criteria area, click the <ac:image ac:title="Select Context Element" ac:alt="Select Context Element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_context_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button next to the Context box and then select the element in the <strong>Select Context Element</strong><span> </span>dialog.<span> </span></li><li><p>If the <strong>Make Element as Context on Selection</strong> option is turned on, click any node to make it the new context of the structure. To turn this option on, click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar and select <strong>Make Element as Context on Selection</strong>.</p></li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711873 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00182: Changing the tooltips style

- page_id: `136711873`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711873/Changing+the+tooltips+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:00.406+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify the tooltips display mode of the symbols on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024x'] by selecting to hide it, to show only the element name, or its documentation text. The element tooltip in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] displays only documentation text, if it is specified for an element. Otherwise, the tooltip is not shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'].

#### INFO: Writing documentation for an element

Writing documentation for an element

You can write a documentation for an element by using the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Documentation tab' space='MD2024x'] .
- Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] > Documentation/Hyperlinks property group.
- Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] > Documentation property.

To change the tooltips style of the symbol on the diagram pane

1. On the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='MD2024x']**Project Options** dialog find the Tooltips Style option.
3. Select one of the following values:
  - Do Not Show (default) - the tooltip is not shown when hovering a mouse on any element.
  - Show Element Name - The tooltip displays the name of the element when hovering a mouse on it.
  - Show Element Documentation - The tooltip displays the documentation text of the element when hovering a mouse on it.
4. Click **OK**. 
The symbol tooltips display mode is changed in the entire project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can specify the tooltips display mode of the symbols on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by selecting to hide it, to show only the element name, or its documentation text. The element tooltip in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> displays only documentation text, if it is specified for an element. Otherwise, the tooltip is not shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71741dd5-c1fa-46c6-9794-ac42b18dacf7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Writing documentation for an element</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can write a documentation for an element by using the:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Documentation tab" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Element <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Documentation/Hyperlinks</strong> property group.</li><li>Element <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Documentation</strong> property.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change the tooltips style of the symbol on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> find the <strong>Tooltips Style</strong> option.</li><li>Select one of the following values:<br /><ul><li><strong>Do Not Show</strong> (default) -  the tooltip is not shown when hovering a mouse on any element.</li><li><strong>Show Element Name</strong> - The tooltip displays the name of the element when hovering a mouse on it.</li><li><strong>Show Element Documentation </strong>- The tooltip displays the documentation text of the element when hovering a mouse on it.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The symbol tooltips display mode is changed in the entire project.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713287 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00183: Changing the used project version

- page_id: `136713287`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713287/Changing+the+used+project+version
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:23.989+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Shortcut menu

762721493

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Shortcut menu

762721495

762721492

**On this page**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Shortcut menu

762721494

#### WARNING: Permissions

Permissions

To change the used project version, you must have:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024x'], [Edit Resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024x/Permissions#Permissions-EditResourcespermission), [Edit Resource Properties](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024x/Permissions#Permissions-EditResourcePropertiespermission), and Administer Resources permissions on the main project and
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024x'] permission on the used project whose version you want to change.

You can set an option to receive a notification when a new used project version appears. You may then choose to update the used project version to the latest version or keep working with the current one.

##### Setting notifications about version changes

By default, when you open your server project you will get a notification if there are used project versions later than the used one in your project that meet notification criteria. When this notification appears, click the **Update used projects** link and select the projects you want to update in the open **Update Used Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can change the default notification settings to receive notifications when a server project is opened and when updates are done, as well as disable them if they are not necessary.

To set when to show notifications about newer used project versions

1. On the main menu, click **Options** > **Environment** to open the **Environment Options**dialog.
2. In the options group list, select **Collaboration**, then select the **Notify About Newer Project Usages**option, and specify the commands to be notified:
  1. 
    - on project opening and updating;
    - on project opening; or
    - never.

##### Notifications for any new version

If you do not need to specify a used project version with tags, you can choose to get notifications for any new version.

To get notifications for any new version

1. From the main menu, select **Collaborate** > **Project Usages** > **Project Usages**.
2. Select a used project and click to select the check box next to **Notify on**.
3. From the drop down list select **Notify for Any New Version**.
4. Click **OK** to close the dialog.

##### Notifications for new tagged versions

When you want to get notifications about updated used projects during your modeling process, you must define the used projects for routine version checking.Youare allowed to specify certain commit tags for every used project. The modeling tool will show a notification when a new version of a used project with a specified commit tag is available.

To get notifications about changes to a certain tagged used project version

1. From the main menu, select **Collaborate** > **Project Usages** > **Project Usages**.
2. Select a used project and click to select the check box next to **Notify on**.
3. From the drop down list select**Notify on New Tagged Version**.
4. Click **Specify Tags**, near the drop-down list.
5. In the **Specify Tags**dialog, double click the first row. To add or delete rows, do one of the following:
  1. To add a row, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], or press Alt + Enter.
  2. To delete a row, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], or press Alt + Backspace.
6. Specify the tag, e.g., *2018Q*(tags are case insensitive), and then click **OK** to close the dialog. If you do not specify tags, the modeling tool will show a warning icon.It is recommended that tags specification be done in patterns, because the modeling tool searches for partial matches. See the example below for more information.ExampleAfter a new version of the used project is committed to the server with a tag that fully or partially matches the specified tag, you will be notified about updates of these tagged projects.Let’s say you specify these tags: 
*2018Q* 
*2019Q* 
*2020Q* 
*Major*New versions and notification status:New version without tags - no notificationNew version with the tag [ Major ] – notification about a new versionNew version with the tag [ 2018q1 ] – notification about a new versionNew version with tags [Release][Major] – notification about a new versionNew version with the tag [Release] – no notificationNew version with the tag [Q_2018Q2T] - notification about a new version
7. Click **OK** to close the dialog.

##### Updating versions of used projects

If a used project has tags specified for notification, only the version meeting the used projects notifications criteria (tags) will be shown in this dialog.

To update the used project version

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. Click the Update Used Projects button.
3. In the open Update **Used Projects** dialog, select the project you want to update. [ATTACHMENT filename='Update Used Projects_target version.png']
4. Wait while the version changes to the latest and click OK to close the dialog. The used project is automatically locked. The lock will be released after the project commit.

##### Changing versions of used projects

To change the used project version

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2024x'] .
2. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select a used project.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to change the version of a used project. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via shortcut menu. Right-click project and then select **Change version.** [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the open dialog, select the used project version to switch to and click OK .
6. Wait while the version changes to the selected one and click OK to close the dialog.

Versions are displayed in the following format:*#<used version>/<latest version>.*

The term #1/3 denotes that 1 is the used version and 3 is the latest version.

You can set an option to receive a notification when a new used project version appears. You may then choose to update the used project version to the latest version or keep working with the current one.

##### Setting notifications about version changes

By default, when you open your server project you will get a notification if there are used project versions later than the used one in your project that meet the notification criteria. When the notification appears, click the **Update used projects** link and select the projects you want to update in the open **Update Used Projects** dialog.

You can change the default notification settings to receive notifications when a server project is opened and when updates are done, as well as disable them if they are not necessary.

To set when to show notifications about newer used project versions

1. On the main menu, click **Options** > **Environment** to open the **Environment Options**dialog.
2. In the options group list, select **Collaboration.** Then select the **Notify About Newer Project Usages**option, and specify the commands to be notified:
  - 
    - on project opening;
    - on project opening and updating; or
    - never.

##### Notifications for any new version

If you do not need to specify a used project version with tags, you can choose to get notifications for any new version.

To get notifications for any new version

1. From the main menu, select **Collaborate** > **Project Usages** > **Project Usages**.
2. Select a used project and click to select the check box next to **Notify on**.
3. From the drop down list select **Notify for Any New Version**.
4. Click **OK** to close the dialog.

##### Updating versions of used projects

To update the used project version

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
2. Click the Update Used Projects button [ATTACHMENT filename='Update_used_projects.PNG'] .
3. In the open Update Used Projects dialog, select the project you want to update. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_used_project.png']
4. Wait while the version changes to the latest and click OK to close the dialog. The used project is automatically locked. The lock will be released after the project commit.

##### Changing versions of used projects

To change the used project version

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2022x'] .
2. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. Select a used project.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to change the version of a used project. Shortcut menuThis step can be done via a shortcut menu. Right-click the project and then select **Change version.** [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the open dialog, select the used project version to switch to and click OK .
6. Wait while the version changes to the selected one and click OK to close the dialog.

Versions are displayed in the following format:*[Branch] Revision.UsedIteration/[Branch] Revision.LatestIteration.*

In this example, [1] A.1 is the used version and [1] A.2 is the latest version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d28ab25c-1547-4ba5-a3d3-601aa623b211"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">762721493</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9275c5cc-aed8-441c-9c2f-68c964115930"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">762721495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b668d5f8-eb8c-4b8a-94b8-26edc74fa384"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">762721492</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e4b2ce1-50a2-4050-8866-300cdddc2337" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d38d824-86e2-4bdd-969c-3a3c41ea0a6c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">762721494</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can set an option to receive a notification when a new used project version appears. You may then choose to update the used project version to the latest version or keep working with the current one.</span></p><h3>Setting notifications about version changes</h3><p>By default, when you open your server project you will get a notification <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">if there are used project versions later than the used one in your project that meet the notification criteria.</span> When the notification appears, click the <strong>Update used projects</strong> link and select the projects you want to update in the open <strong>Update Used Projects</strong> dialog. </p><p>You can change the default notification settings to receive notifications when a server project is opened and when updates are done, as well as disable them if they are not necessary.</p><p>To set when to show notifications about newer used project versions</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> to open the <strong>Environment Options </strong>dialog.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the options group list, select <strong> Collaboration.</strong> Then select the <strong>Notify About Newer Project Usages </strong>option, and specify the commands to be notified:</span> <br /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li>on project opening;</li><li>on project opening and updating; or</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">never.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ol><h3><span>Notifications for any new version</span></h3><p><span>If you do not need to specify a used project version with tags, you can choose to get notifications for any new version. </span></p><p>To get notifications for any new version</p><hr /><ol><li><span>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</span></li><li><span>Select a used project and click to select the check box next to  <strong> Notify on </strong>.</span></li><li><span>From the drop down list select <strong>Notify for Any New Version</strong>. </span></li><li><span>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog.</span></li></ol><h3>Updating versions of used projects </h3><p>To update the used project version</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Update Used Projects</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Update_used_projects.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Changing used project version in 3DEXPERIENCE" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the open <strong>Update Used Projects </strong> dialog, select the project you want to update.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_used_project.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Changing used project version in 3DEXPERIENCE" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Wait while the version changes to the latest and click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog.<br />The used project is automatically locked. The lock will be released after the project commit.</li></ol><h3 style="margin-top: 30.0px;">Changing versions of used projects</h3><p>To change the used project version</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>Select a used project.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_version_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to change the version of a used project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac0e76a5-8ae3-4829-8bdb-a3a08b2944bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Shortcut menu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step can be done via a shortcut menu. Right-click the project and then select <strong>Change <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2464e5b4-2d7c-4d64-b8ca-26d11f1c11fa">version</ac:inline-comment-marker>. </strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_used_project_version.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Changing used project version in 3DEXPERIENCE" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>In the open dialog, select the used project version to switch to and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Wait while the version changes to the selected one and click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog.</li></ol><p>Versions are displayed in the following format:<em> [Branch] Revision.UsedIteration/[Branch] Revision.LatestIteration.</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2697b4ef-c299-452f-a059-2895b57d804d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="90f6759e-7984-47a7-aead-42c249397433"> In this example, [1] A.1 is the used version and [1] A.2</ac:inline-comment-marker> is the latest version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00185: Chart tool

1348176924

1348176935

1348176934

The Chart tool provides functions to prepare tag values from models in matrix tabular form to be mapped into a variety of chart types and styles. This tool is supported in the DOCX template. Like other Custom tools, the Chart tool (chart.JAR) must be installed in the **extensions** folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information about Custom tool and installation, see [Custom tool](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Custom+tool).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Chart tool using tag values from models in matrix tabular form to be mapped into chart types and styles.

To use the Chart tool in MagicDraw

1. Place the JAR file in the extensions folder of the Report Wizard plugin folder.
2. Start MagicDraw.
3. Use the following line of code to import the Chart tool to your report: text

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715146 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00186: Checking element editing permissions

- page_id: `136715146`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715146/Checking+element+editing+permissions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:24:20.639+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is a programmer responsibility to check model modification permissions before doing model changes. Some Elements in model can be read-only, but API **does not prevent** modifications of these elements.

The Element is read-only if:

- The Element belongs to the read-only used project (module)
- The Element is not locked for editing in a Teamwork Cloud environment
- Some other restrictions are added in some specific modeling case

##### Checking editing permission

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.isEditable()* to check if Element's properties can be changed.

Editing permissions should be checked if primitive Element properties are modified (like a name, for example) or references are modified (like a Property type).

Check the adding permission if you are going to modify some containment property (like *Element.ownedElement*).

##### Checking adding permission

Use *BaseElement.canAddChild()* to check if children can be added into the Element.

Use *BaseElement.canAdd(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement)* to check if a parent can own a given element and a parent has permissions to own. This method will fail, for example, if you will try to add an Operation into a Package.

##### Checking removing permission

Use *BaseElement.isEditable()* to check if an Element can be removed (disposed) from the model.

##### Checking moving permission

The moving permission is similar to the adding permission, but checks more conditions.

##### Plugging custom permissions handler

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager.addPermissionsHandler(**ElementPermissions)* to register a custom element permissions handler. Using this API you can handle custom rules for putting a permission on the top of some specific elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>It is a programmer responsibility to check model modification permissions before doing model changes. Some Elements in model can be read-only, but API <strong>does not prevent</strong> modifications of these elements. </p><p>The Element is read-only if:</p><ul><li>The Element belongs to the read-only used project (module)</li><li>The Element is not locked for editing in a Teamwork Cloud environment</li><li>Some other restrictions are added in some specific modeling case</li></ul><h3>Checking editing permission</h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement.isEditable()</em>  to check if Element's properties can be changed.</p><p>Editing permissions should be checked if primitive Element properties are modified (like a name, for example) or references are modified (like a Property type).</p><p>Check the adding permission if you are going to modify some containment property (like <em>Element.ownedElement</em>). </p><h3>Checking adding permission</h3><p>Use <em>BaseElement.canAddChild()</em>  to check if children can be added into the Element. </p><p>Use <em>BaseElement.canAdd(com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement)</em> to check if a parent can own a given element and a parent has permissions to own. This method will fail, for example, if you will try to add an Operation into a Package.</p><h3>Checking removing permission</h3><p>Use <em>BaseElement.isEditable()</em> to check if an Element can be removed (disposed) from the model.</p><h3>Checking moving permission</h3><p>The moving permission is similar to the adding permission, but checks more conditions. </p><h3>Plugging custom permissions handler</h3><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.permissions.ElementPermissionsManager.addPermissionsHandler(</em><em>ElementPermissions)</em> to register a custom element permissions handler. Using this API you can handle custom rules for putting a permission on the top of some specific elements.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711327 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00187: Choice

- page_id: `136711327`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711327/Choice
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:33.103+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The choice vertices, when reached, result in the dynamic evaluation of the guards or the triggers of its outgoing transitions. This realizes a dynamic conditional branch. It allows splitting of transitions into multiple outgoing paths such that the decision on which path to take may be a function of the results of prior actions performed in the same run-to-completion step.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

This realizes a dynamic conditional branch. It allows splitting of transitions into multiple outgoing paths such that the decision on which path to take may be a function of the results of prior actions performed in the same run-to-completion step.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710898 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00188: Class

- page_id: `136710898`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710898/Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:05.633+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1408904160

1408904163

1408904162

A Class is drawn as a solid-outline rectangle with three compartments separated by horizontal lines. The top name compartment holds the Class name and other general properties of the Class (including [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']); the middle list compartment holds a list of properties; the bottom list compartment holds a list of operations. The property and operation compartments are optional, and you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes' space='MD2024x'] them.

A Class is a descriptor for a set of objects with similar structures, behaviors, and relationships. The model is concerned with describing the intention of the Class, which are the rules that define it. The run-time execution provides its extension that are its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024x'].

A Class represents a concept within the system being modeled. It has a data structure, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x'], and relationships to other elements. The name of a Class has a scope within the package in which it is declared, and the name must be unique, among Class names, within its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x'].

Classes are declared in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] and are used in most of other diagrams. UML provides a graphical notation for declaring and using these Classes as well as a textual notation for referencing Classes within the descriptions of other model elements.

1408904159

**What to do with classes**

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class Diagram Wizard' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']

The top name compartment holds the Class name and other general properties of the Class (including <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>); the middle list compartment holds a list of properties; the bottom list compartment holds a list of operations. The property and operation compartments are optional, and you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[suppress]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> them.</p><p>A Class is a descriptor for a set of objects with similar structures, behaviors, and relationships. The model is concerned with describing the intention of the Class, which are the rules that define it. The run-time execution provides its extension that are its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[instances]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p>A Class represents a concept within the system being modeled. It has a data structure, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link>, and relationships to other elements. The name of a Class has a scope within the package in which it is declared, and the name must be unique, among Class names, within its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Classes are declared in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and are used in most of other diagrams. UML provides a graphical notation for declaring and using these Classes as well as a textual notation for referencing Classes within the descriptions of other model elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ead6794-4554-4572-95a4-72e82fa49043"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1408904159</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>What to do with classes</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8abc0228-7a6e-4089-b852-b7fa2237c54c" /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class Diagram Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709768 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00189: Class diagram

- page_id: `136709768`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709768/Class+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:06.895+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: ['class', 'diagram', 'uml']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306054507

306054509

306054508

**Overview**

A Class diagram falls under the structural diagramming family. It is a graphic representation of the static structural model. It shows [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MD2024x'], along with their internal structure and relationships. The classes represent types of objects that are handled in a system. The instances of those types (objects) are instantiated only on the runtime and are represented by an object and the interaction diagrams. A Class diagram does not show temporal information, it describes only the classification.

Classes can be related to each other in a number of ways: associated (connected to each other), dependent (one class depends/uses another class), specialized (one class is a subtype of another class), or packaged (grouped together as a unit – package). A Class diagram does not express anything specific about the relationships of a given object, but it does abstractly describe the potential relationships of one object with other objects.

A system typically has a number of Class diagrams – not all the classes are inserted into a single Class diagram. A class can have multiple levels of meaning and participate in several Class diagrams. A Class diagram is the logical map of an existing or future source code.

**Purpose**

A class diagram describes the types of objects (e.g., people, materials, information, and behaviors) in the system and various kinds of static relationships that exist among them. Class diagrams also show the attributes and operations of a class and the constraints that apply to the way objects are connected.

**Usage**

A class diagram can be used to:

- illustrate both simple and complex models for information systems
- understand the general overview of the schematics of an application
- visually express the specific needs of a system
- highlight the specific code that needs to be programmed and implemented to the structure
- provide an implementation-independent description of types used in a system
- share relevant information with stakeholders
- detect errors early in the lifecycle.

**Summary:**

Class diagrams are valuable because they:

- define the essential resources of the system
- define relationships between resources
- generate code
- model code (reverse engineering)
- provide a focus for all the other diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Class diagram

306054506

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30924a02-78e9-4c98-ad29-3d6375aa0d90"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306054507</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07da3c4e-b2c0-4506-8fd9-a5279f2a3cd2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306054509</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="209dc25c-f607-40f4-b3fb-4ce952958e03"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306054508</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Overview</strong></p><p>A Class diagram falls under the structural diagramming family. It is a graphic representation of the static structural model. It shows <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interfaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, along with their internal structure and relationships. The classes represent types of objects that are handled in a system. The instances of those types (objects) are instantiated only on the runtime and are represented by an object and the interaction diagrams. A Class diagram does not show temporal information, it describes only the classification. </p><p>Classes can be related to each other in a number of ways: associated (connected to each other), dependent (one class depends/uses another class), specialized (one class is a subtype of another class), or packaged (grouped together as a unit – package). A Class diagram does not express anything specific about the relationships of a given object, but it does abstractly describe the potential relationships of one object with other objects.</p><p>A system typically has a number of Class diagrams – not all the classes are inserted into a single Class diagram. A class can have multiple levels of meaning and participate in several Class diagrams. A Class diagram is the logical map of an existing or future source code.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>A class diagram describes the types of objects (e.g., people, materials, information, and behaviors) in the system and various kinds of static relationships that exist among them. Class diagrams also show the attributes and operations of a class and the constraints that apply to the way objects are connected. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A class diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>illustrate both simple and complex models for information systems </li><li>understand the general overview of the schematics of an application</li><li>visually express the specific needs of a system</li><li>highlight the specific code that needs to be programmed and implemented to the structure</li><li>provide an implementation-independent description of types used in a system</li><li>share relevant information with stakeholders </li><li>detect errors early in the lifecycle. </li></ul><p><strong>Summary:</strong></p><p>Class diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>define the essential resources of the system</li><li>define relationships between resources </li><li>generate code</li><li>model code (reverse engineering)</li><li>provide a focus for all the other diagrams. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Class diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdbc5c26-9baa-4aab-8ebe-f1760f1a8623"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306054506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712949 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00190: Class Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `136712949`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712949/Class+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:09.349+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

561698190

561698194

561698193

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Class Diagram Wizard** helps you create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] when all classes and their relationships are already created and specified. You can select which classes, packages, and relationships will be included in a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'], as well as the details of the class representation to be configured (attributes, operations, accessibility). The **Class Diagram Wizard** then guides you through several steps and collects information along the way. It will automatically create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] with your selected elements, freeing you from creating the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] manually.

To create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] using the **Class Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Class Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Class Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Class Diagram Wizard and click Start.
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

561698189

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']

| --- | --- |
| Old Value | Translation |
|  |  |

| struct | Apply the «C++Struct» stereotype. |
| union | Apply the «C++Union» stereotype. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710247 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00192: Clearing values from rows

- page_id: `136710247`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710247/Clearing+values+from+rows
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:33.970+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Working with rows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To clear values from an entire row

1. Right-click the number cell to select the entire row.
2. From the shortcut menu, click Clear . Alternatively, you can press Delete on your keyboard after selecting the entire row.

#### WARNING: Data types

Data types

Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.

To clear a value from a cell

1. Right-click the specific cell of the row.
2. From the shortcut menu, click Clear . Alternatively, you can press Delete on your keyboard after selecting the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">To clear values from an entire row</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f5205aa9-b573-4983-984b-8ec9e03d6c2c">Right-click the number cell to select the entire row. </ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the shortcut menu, click <strong>Clear</strong>.<br />Alternatively, you can press<strong> Delete</strong> on your keyboard after selecting the entire row. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63035fe7-faf5-41da-b952-aaf332340a76"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Data types</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To clear a value from a cell</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the specific cell of the row.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, click<strong> Clear</strong>. <br />Alternatively, you can press <strong>Delete</strong> on your keyboard after selecting the cell. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713030 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00193: Cloning projects

- page_id: `136713030`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713030/Cloning+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:14.301+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

54100852

54100855

54100853

You can clone the latest version of your server project as a separate project or the latest version of a project branch as a separate branch. The cloned project can be used as a "template" to create a new project or as an independent base for new development. IDs of a cloned project and elements are reset automatically. Project permissions are reset as well; the project is available only for the user who has cloned the project. All references to used projects (if there are any) are maintained.

- You can only clone either the latest version of a project or the latest version of a project branch.
- You need at least double the amount of memory to clone a project in comparison to opening a project.

To clone a project or a branch

1. On the main menu, click **Collaborate**> **Projects**. The **Manage Projects** dialog opens.
2. To clone a project, select the project you want to clone. You can use the search bar to filter used projects fasterby name.
3. (Optional) To clone a branch of a chosen project, select the project and do one of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='1_select_branch.png']
  - Click the Select Branch [ATTACHMENT filename='select_branches_button.png'] button.
  - Click the three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button in the Branch section.
  - Right-click the project and select Select Branch . Select the branch you want to clone in the Select Branch dialog and click OK .
4. To clone the selected project or branch, do one of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='2_clone_server_project.png']
  - Click the Clone button.
  - Right-click the project and select Clone Server Project .
5. The Clone Server Project dialog opens, wherein you need to type the name of the cloned project, select a category to save the project and/or add a comment. Click More , if you want to add a tag for the project.
6. (Optional) To clone with used projects, click**More**, select the**Used Projects**tab and check the**Clone Used Projects**check box. You can change the names of the cloned used projects in the**New Name**section once the**Clone Used Projects**check box is selected. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Cloned used projects do not include package permissions, hidden packages, and code engineering sets.
7. Click Clone when you are done.
8. Close the Manage Projects dialog.

Before using the cloned project with Teamwork Cloud web applications, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024x'].

54100851

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding projects to Teamwork Cloud' space='MD2024x']

## PAGE 00194: Cloning selected element

- page_id: `136711762`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711762/Cloning+selected+element

- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Selecting elements

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

402269317

402269319

402269318

In the element selection dialog, you can clone an existing element. It means you can create a new element on the basis of the selected element having the same data as the selected element.

To clone a selected element

1. In the Tree tab, click the Creation Mode button.
2. In the model tree, select the element which you want to clone and do one of the following:
  - Click the Clone button.
  - From the selected element's shortcut menu, select Clone .
3. The Specification window of a new element opens with all properties derived from the cloned element. By default, the number is added to the element name.
4. Change or define desired properties and click Close when you are done. The new element is included to the model tree.

402269316

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='MD2024x']

## PAGE 00195: Code Engineering

- page_id: `136715361`
- space_key: `MD2024x`

- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:12:00.117+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

807320373

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

100.00002%

807320375

820270240

**Demos**

- Code Generation
- Code Reverse

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

807320374

##### Abbreviations

- UML Unified Modeling Language
- GUI Graphical User Interface
- CE Code Engineering
- CES Code Engineering Set
- AST Abstract Syntax Tree
- JLS Java Language Specification
- JVM Java Virtual Machine
- RT Round-Trip forward and backward code engineering without code loss CLR Common Language Runtime
- DSL Domain-Specific Language

#### INFO: Functionality Availability

Functionality Availability

Code Engineering is available in Professional and Enterprise editions only.

The code generation from behavior is not supported.

Code engineering provides a simple and intuitive graphical interface for merging code and UML models, as well as preparing both code skeletons out of UML models and models from code.

There are several cases where code engineering may be very useful:

- You already have code that needs to be reversed to a model.
- You wish to implement the created model.
- You need to merge your models and code.

The tool may generate code from models or create models out of code. Changes in the existing code can be reflected in the model, and you can see model changes in your code. You can also merge independent changes to a model and code without destroying data in the code or model.

Code engineering supports Java, C++, CORBA IDL, DDL, and XML Schema languages. Your models can be converted to any of those languages, or you can create UML models from the source code written in those languages. Additionally, the tool supports reversing from Java Bytecode.

807320372
