<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715318 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00196: Code engineering set

- page_id: `136715318`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715318/Code+engineering+set
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T09:24:39.861+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Code engineering using API
- labels: ['forward-engineering', 'reverse-engineering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ce.CodeEngineeringSet* object represents a code-engineering object as a resource for forward or reverse engineering.

Each *CodeEngineeringSet* provides properties, which can be read:

- Name . Represents a name of the code engineering set, visible in the Browser.
- Model elements . Represents elements that are added into the code engineering set from the model.
- W orking directory . Represents a code engineering directory where generated/reversed files are located.

###### **Forwar****d Engineering**

To perform the code generation, elements should be added to the *CodeEngineeringSet* object. Use the *CodeEngineeringSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List<BaseElement[>)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ce/CodeEngineeringSet.html#addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet-java.util.List-)*method to add a list of model elements to the code engineering set.

Model elements should be in the working package, otherwise the model element is not be added to the code engineering set. The working package is set when creating a code engineering set.

**Revers****e Engineering**

Source code files are required to perform the Reverse Engineering. There are 2 methods available for adding files into the code engineering set:

1. CodeEngineeringSet.addFilesToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List<java.io.File>); This method adds the given list of files to the code engineering set.
2. CodeEngineeringSet.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(java.io.File); This method adds all specific source code files to the code engineering set, starting from the given directory.

Source code files should be in the working directory, in order to have the successful reverse.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Represents a name of the code engineering set, visible in the Browser.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Model elements</strong>. Represents elements that are added into the code engineering set from the model.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">W</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">orking directory</strong>. Represents a code engineering directory where generated/reversed files are located. </li></ul><h4><strong>Forwar</strong><strong>d Engineering</strong></h4><p>To perform the code generation, elements should be added to the <em>CodeEngineeringSet</em> object. Use the <em>CodeEngineeringSet.addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List&lt;BaseElement<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ce/CodeEngineeringSet.html#addElementsToCodeEngineeringSet-java.util.List-">&gt;)</a> </em>method to add a list of model elements to the code engineering set.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81f4f76b-be13-4403-bd5d-4ad93de08e70"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Model elements should be in the working package, otherwise the model element is not be added to the code engineering set. The working package is set when creating a code engineering set.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">Revers</strong><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);line-height: 1.4285715;">e Engineering</strong></p></div><p>Source code files are required to perform the Reverse Engineering. There are 2 methods available for adding files into the code engineering set:</p><ol><li><em>CodeEngineeringSet.addFilesToCodeEngineeringSet(java.util.List&lt;java.io.File&gt;); </em>This method adds the given list of files to the code engineering set.</li><li><em>CodeEngineeringSet.addAllFilesRecursivelyToCES(java.io.File); </em>This method adds all specific source code files to the code engineering set, starting from the given directory.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee5c32f1-5d22-4d6c-b6ec-db6c906584b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p> Source code files should be in the working directory, in order to have the successful reverse.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715362 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00197: Code Engineering Sets

- page_id: `136715362`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715362/Code+Engineering+Sets
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:50:58.648+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: ['code-engineering-manager', 'round-trip-set']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418339696

418339698

418339697

You can manage code engineering through the**Code Engineering Sets**in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'].The **Code Engineering Sets** tree contains the list of all sets created in the project as well as instruments for managing those sets.

To add a new set

1. Choose New from the Code Engineering Sets shortcut menu.
2. Choose the language you want:

- 
  - Java
  - Java Bytecode
  - C++
  - CORBA IDL
  - DDL (Cloudscape, DB2, Microsoft Access, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle, Pervasive, Pointbase, PostgreSQL, Sybase). For DDL, the is required
  - XML Schema, the is required [ATTACHMENT filename='CE_language_options.png']

Edit sets in the **Round Trip Set**dialog.

To open the **Round Trip Set**dialog

- Right-click the selected set and choose Edit from the set shortcut menu.
- Select the set and press Enter.

The **Round Trip Set** dialog allows you to manage entities to be added/removed to your set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Round Trip Set dialog. Add files tab

Specify the **Working Directory** for displaying source files. This option indicates files and required sub-directories where a code generation output goes. Type a path manually or browse in the directory tree by clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.

The **Working Package** box allows you to define any package for reverse output or code generation. A model is reversed or code generated from this specified package.

You can only select or change the working package prior to the addition of files from the working directory to the code engineering set.

The **Round Trip Set** dialog has two tabs: **Add Files** and **Add Data from Model**.

The **Add Files tab** helps you manage the source code files involved in your code engineering set.

| Element name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| All files | Lists directories with the source files for the set. |
| Files of type | Contains possible file name extensions for the chosen language. |

The **Add Data from Model** tab helps you manage elements located in the UML model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Round Trip Set dialog. Add data from the model tab.

The **All Data** list contains the hierarchy of UML model packages with model elements (or other packages) inside. Your code engineering set can be combined out of model and code elements. The following buttons are available in the **Round Trip Set** dialog:

| Add | The selected file in the All Files or All Data list is added to the set. |
| --- | --- |
| Add All | All files in the opened or selected directory are added to the set. |
| Add Recursively | All files in the selected directory and its subdirectories are added to the set. |
| Remove | Removes the selected entity from the set. |
| Remove All | Removes all entities from the set. |

418339695

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715317 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00198: Code engineering using API

- page_id: `136715317`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715317/Code+engineering+using+API
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:49:05.620+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can perform Code Engineering using the Open API.

Code engineering allows generation of the source code from the specific model, and source code reversing into model elements. API is provided for:

- Code engineering sets creation for particular programming languages.
- Automatic component creation for every class involved in forward engineering and every file involved into reverse engineering.
- The specification of either working, or output, or temporary directories for processing source code files. The destination of the code reverse operation output can be any model package.

In the following topics we will review how to manage code engineering and how to do reverse and forward engineering.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\codeengineering

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can perform Code Engineering using the Open API.</p><p>Code engineering allows generation of the source code from the specific model, and source code reversing into model elements. API is provided for:</p><ul><li>Code engineering sets creation for particular programming languages.</li><li>Automatic component creation for every class involved in forward engineering and every file involved into reverse engineering.</li><li>The specification of either working, or output, or temporary directories for processing source code files. The destination of the code reverse operation output can be any model package.</li></ul><p>In the following topics we will review how to manage code engineering and how to do reverse and forward engineering.</p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\codeengineering</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="435c38b8-d959-4e50-ab09-d696275959df"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eb4629e6-9e17-496a-aa8e-a97a73d4d905"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00199: Code example for Group Tool

- page_id: `136714478`
- space_key: `MD2024x`

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with Group Tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c459681d-485c-4c4c-b3e0-38222922fdec"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[##---------------------------------
## Execute $group.init() once in the script
#set( $void = $group.init())
## Add new entries to the group tool
#foreach($e in $elements)
#if($e.elementType == "class")
#set( $void = $group.put("class", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "package")
#set( $void = $group.put("package", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "interface")
#set( $void = $group.put("interface", $e) )
#end
#end
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $group.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $group.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
---------------------------------
##set( $void = $group.init()) ## not use init group 
## Add new entries to the group tool
#foreach($e in $elements)
#if($e.elementType == "usecase")
#set( $void = $group.put("usecase", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "stereotype")
#set( $void = $group.put("stereotype", $e) )
#end
#end
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $group.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $group.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
---------------------------------
#set( $void = $group.init()) ## use init group
## Add new entries to the group tool
#foreach($e in $elements)
#if($e.elementType == "usecase")
#set( $void = $group.put("usecase", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "stereotype")
#set( $void = $group.put("stereotype", $e) )
#end
#end
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $group.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $group.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
##---------------------------------
## Create new group tool
#set( $newGroupTool = $group.create())
##set( $void = $newGroupTool.init()) ## no need to init at first time
## Add new entries to the group tool
#foreach($e in $elements)
#if($e.elementType == "class")
#set( $void = $newGroupTool.put("class", $e) )
#elseif($e.elementType == "interface")
#set( $void = $newGroupTool.put("interface", $e) )
#end
#end
==============================================================================
New Group
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $newGroupTool.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $newGroupTool.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
OLD Group
## Iterate over the groupTool instance
#foreach( $groupName in $group.groupNames() )
Grupp with name $groupName contains the following elements
#foreach( $e in $group.get($groupName) )
    Element: $e.name
#end
#end
##---------------------------------]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714491 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00200: Code examples for generic table tool

- page_id: `136714491`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714491/Code+examples+for+generic+table+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:37.903+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generic table tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with a Generic Table tool. The code may not be executable or may not return the correct result. This is only the idea of how to use the Generic Table tool and how it works.

The code shown below prints the row, column, and its value in the form of an HTML table.

```text
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))
	            #
	        #foreach($colName in $table.columnNames)
		            $colName
	  #end
	  
	  #foreach($row in $table.rows)
	        
		            $foreach.count
	        #foreach($col in $table.columnIds)
		            $table.getValueAsString($row, $col)
	        #end
	        
	    #end
	    
	#end]]>
```

###### How to learn more about the GenericTableTool

1. From MagicDraw main menu choose Tools > Report Wizard .
2. In the Select Template pane, choose Tutorial > Tutorial 27 - Generic Table Export .
3. Click Next , and choose Built-in for Select Report Data.
4. Click Next again, Select Element Scope, and then click Next again. Select output options and click Generate .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Generic Table Export dialog.

You can retrieve other diagram table types by using the complete code shown below for getting values from the Generic Table:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The complete code for getting values from the Generic Table.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can customize line of code, e.g., by adding diagram table types to get the Metric Table or Instance Table, as shown below:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with a Generic Table tool. The code may not be executable or may not return the correct result. This is only the idea of how to use the Generic Table tool and how it works.</p><p>The code shown below prints the row, column, and its value in the form of an HTML table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62eece43-0081-481a-9595-f5e7bbb87043"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Generic Table"))
	  <table>
	  <tr>
	        #set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))
	            <td>#</td>
	        #foreach($colName in $table.columnNames)
		            <td>$colName</td>
	  #end
	  </tr>
	  #foreach($row in $table.rows)
	        <tr>
		            <td>$foreach.count</td>
	        #foreach($col in $table.columnIds)
		            <td>$table.getValueAsString($row, $col)</td>
	        #end
	        </tr>
	    #end
	    </table>
	#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7bd4a1d6-16e2-4902-9a2f-2a4f8c66590c"><ac:rich-text-body><h4>How to learn more about the GenericTableTool</h4><hr /><ol><li>From MagicDraw main menu choose <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</li><li>In the Select Template pane, choose <strong>Tutorial</strong> &gt; <strong>Tutorial 27 - Generic Table Export</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong>, and choose <strong>Built-in </strong>for Select Report Data.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> again, Select Element Scope, and then click <strong>Next</strong> again. Select output options and click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Generic Table Export dialog" ac:alt="Generic Table Export dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generic-table-export-a.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Code examples for generic table tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The Generic Table Export dialog.</h6><p>You can retrieve other diagram table types by using the complete code shown below for getting values from the Generic Table:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Complete Code Tutorial.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Code examples for generic table tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The complete code for getting values from the Generic Table.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9055601-868c-46be-9f51-d9404ed2eb7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can customize line of code, e.g., by adding diagram table types to get the Metric Table or Instance Table, as shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0847f94e-cf82-430c-b1f1-bd0279d2002c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#if($diagram.diagramType == “Metric Table” || $diagram.diagramType == “Instance Table”)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714477 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00201: Code examples for model validation tool

- page_id: `136714477`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714477/Code+examples+for+model+validation+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:37.512+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Model validation tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with Model Validation tool.

To print all validation results from a project by using the “UML completeness constraints” suite, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

To print only the validation results from the Class by using the “UML Correctness” suite and the "warning" severity, for example, type the following code:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with Model Validation tool.</p><p>To print all validation results from a project by using the “UML completeness constraints” suite, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="999f5217-ae6d-4e1d-a6b2-1e073d9b5597"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('validator', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ModelValidationTool')
#set ($results = $validator.validate("UML completeness constraints"))
#foreach ($result in $results)
Element: $result.element.name
Severity: $result.severity
Abbreviation: $result.abbreviation
Message: $result.message
IsIgnored: $result.isIgnored
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To print only the validation results from the Class by using the “UML Correctness” suite and the &quot;warning&quot; severity, for example, type the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d92650c-baee-4090-8255-82ece5480352"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('validator', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ModelValidationTool')
#set ($results = $validator.validate("UML Correctness", $Class, "warning"))
#foreach ($result in $results)
Element: $result.element.name
Abbreviation: $result.abbreviation
Message: $result.message
IsIgnored: $result.isIgnored
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710939 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00202: Collaboration

- page_id: `136710939`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710939/Collaboration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:07.764+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: More Information

1357439945

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: More Information

1357439958

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: More Information

1357439947

A Collaboration describes the structure of elements that implement a certain behavior. In the Collaboration, the elements are the properties connected with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024x'].

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of a collaboration

In this example, you can see the*Loan* Collaboration. The Collaboration contains two properties - *CatcherInTheRye* and *Adele*. These are the properties (attributes) and the*LibraryItem*and*AudioRecord* arethe types of each property. The *CatcherInTheRye*and *Adele* are connected with the connector relationship.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information on how to assign a behavior to a Collaboration Use, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Behavior diagrams automatically' space='MD2024x'].

To create the property and connector in the collaboration

1. Create a Collaboration symbol [IMAGE alt='' src='']in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'], you may name it *Loan*, for example.
2. On the C lass diagram , select the Collaboration, right-click on it to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Element Shortcut Menu' space='MD2024x'] . Select the **Symbol Properties** command.
3. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, c lick to clear the **Suppress Structure** check box. Click **Close**.
4. On the diagram pane, or in the**Model Browser**,select the elementand drag-and-drop it to the Collaboration symbol on the diagram pane.
5. The new property is created. The type of the property is the element that was dragged and dropped.
6. To create aconnectorbetween properties, expand the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x']Composite Structurediagrampallet and click the button.
7. Draw the connector relationship betweenproperties.
8. Now you have the structure represented in the Collaboration.

##### Collaboration properties

You can format collaboration symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x'].

You can specify collaboration properties in the collaboration Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024x'].

1357439941

**Related Diagrams**

- Class Diagram
- Composite Structure Diagram
- Activity
- Sequence
- State Machine
- Protocol State Machine
- Model Elements

**Related Procedures**

- Customizing Environment Options

**Related Refrences**

- Specification Window
- Editing property values

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713065 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00203: Collaboration between disconnected teams

- page_id: `136713065`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713065/Collaboration+between+disconnected+teams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:25:42.081+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['disconnected-collaboration']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1177149143

1177149146

1177149144

Teamwork Cloud server allows for collaborating on projects, supporting multiple contractors who cannot work on the same Teamwork Cloud installation. There are two ways to collaborate on projects – using either the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using server project file format' space='']server project file format *.szip***or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using local project file format' space='']local project file format *.mdzip**.*

Read more about different ways to collaborate on projects in the following sections:

1177149142

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving a local copy of Teamwork Cloud project' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Branching projects' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713430 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00204: Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `136713430`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713430/Collaboration+powered+by+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-02-04T15:10:40.185+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

CATIA Magic and No Magic modeling tools support collaboration on UML/SysML/UAF models in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform (the feature also known as Power'By). You can use a modeling tool to create and store your projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. Moreover, different team members can work on the same project simultaneously, commit new versions of the project to the platform, and update the project when new versions become available.

There are two ways you can start a collaboration session in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform:

- Open a modeling tool and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform .
- Use the Design with CATIA Magic app in the 3D EXPERIENCE platform to open a modeling tool installed on your computer.

600400

To learn how to collaborate on projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, see:

- If collaboration powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform is enabled, you cannot work with Teamwork Cloud or Cameo Collaborator.
- Unsupported plugins:
  - Cameo Collaborator Publisher
  - Model Obfuscator.
  - Cameo Concept Modeller. Contact [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='NMDOC'] for the workaround.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>CATIA Magic and No Magic modeling tools support collaboration on UML/SysML/UAF models in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(the feature also known as Power'By)</span>. You can use a modeling tool to create and store your projects in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform. Moreover, different team members can work on the same project simultaneously, commit new versions of the project to the platform, and update the project when new versions become available.</p><p>There are two ways you can start a collaboration session in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform:</p><ul><li>Open a modeling tool and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging in to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Use the <a href="https://help.3ds.com/2023x/english/dsdoc/CATMagcUserMap/CATMagc-c-ov.htm?contextscope=cloud">Design with CATIA Magic app</a> in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform to open a modeling tool installed on your computer.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05e9fe92-b3a8-4879-a7b6-76cce3f26998"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="http://youtube.com/watch?v=52ANuF6gOH0" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">400</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br />To learn how to collaborate on projects in the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8bf2953b-ed7f-405c-ae25-0f6062de1d79" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29d89163-9955-4e7c-a561-e0ce0574f2b5"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If collaboration powered by the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform is enabled, you cannot work with Teamwork Cloud or Cameo Collaborator.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Unsupported plugins:</span><ul><li>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</li><li>Model Obfuscator.</li><li>Cameo Concept Modeller. Contact <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="Support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Customer Support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the workaround.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710942 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00205: Collaboration Use

- page_id: `136710942`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710942/Collaboration+Use
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:07.974+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Collaboration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557643

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557654

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557644

A Collaboration Use represents the implementation of a pattern described by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space='MD2024x'] in a specific situation. In this context, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MD2024x'] means that the roles in the Collaboration are bound by concrete elements. A Collaboration can be used many times in different Collaboration Uses.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of multiple Collaboration Uses along with Parts

#### TIP: Model Tip

Model Tip

These instructions will work best if you have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] created already.

To assign a Collaboration to a Collaboration Use

Before assigning a Collaboration to a Collaboration Use, you **must** have a Collaboration created in your model.

#### INFO: Collaboration

Collaboration

Read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space='MD2024x'] on how to create a Collaboration in your model.

After creating a Collaboration, do one of the following:

- On the Composite Structure diagram palette, click the Collaboration Use button [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 3.24.55 PM.png'] , and then in the opened dialog, select an existing Collaboration or click the Creation Mode button [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 3.35.47 PM.png'] to create a new Collaboration. Click OK when you are done.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-13 at 6.14.09 PM.png'] symbol. Select a Collaboration. Press Enter or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, and then press Ctrl+T . Type the name of the new Collaboration and press Enter , or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, after that, click the name area. Type the “:” colon and then type the Collaboration name. Press Enter , or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- In the Containment tree , select the Collaboration and drag and drop it on the diagram pane. A Collaboration Use with the assigned Collaboration is created.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, open the Specification window of the Collaboration Use and click the cell of the Type property value. In the opened dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 2.59.14 PM.png'] , and select the Collaboration. Click OK when you are done.

##### Collaboration use properties

You can format Collaboration Use symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: Formatting Symbols

Formatting Symbols

For more information about symbol representation properties, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x'].

You can specify Collaboration Use properties in the Collaboration Use's **Specification window**. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: Specification Window and Property Values

Specification Window and Property Values

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024x'].

1353557641

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a relationship' space='MD2024x']

**Related Diagrams**

- [Composite Structure Diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Composite+Structure+diagram)
- Activity
- Sequence
- State Machine
- Protocol State Machine
- Collaboration
- Model Elements

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712989 space=MD2024x version=6 -->
## PAGE 00206: Collaborative modeling

- page_id: `136712989`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712989/Collaborative+modeling
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2024-01-31T10:12:53.436+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418781380

418781391

418781378

**On this page**

418781381

INLINE

Team members can individually develop separate parts of a single model and then merge them together to form a complete model. In comparison to individual modeling, collaborative modeling is more effective and produces better results because each member employs his/her best skills and experience.

We offer you a server for your team's collaboration – [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Teamwork Cloud' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='']collaboration capabilities powered by **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

Both servers provide a repository for storing projects and users. They also support the management of users' permissions as well as sharing and versioning projects.

Projects stored in the server repository can be accessed through the network from multiple clients who have MagicDraw or any Cameo Suite product installed. Different users, depending on their role in a team or enterprise, can have different permissions to the projects.

The same model or even the same diagram can be accessed and modified in parallel. Every user may instantly obtain the newest version of the model as well as commit his/her own changes.

##### Basic concepts

For better understanding further material, get acquainted with basic concepts of collaborative modeling.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Repository | A place for storing projects and users managed by Teamwork Cloud. |
| User | A collaboration team member, who has credentials to log into Teamwork Cloud and holds permissions to access one or more server projects. |
| Teamwork Cloud project | A project stored in Teamwork Cloud repository. |
| Server project | A common name for Teamwork Cloud projects. |
| Version | A snapshot of a project at a particular point in time. A new version of a project is created after successful commit of changes made in this project. All versions of the same project have unique numbers. |
| Comment | An optional description about changes in a project version. |
| Tag | Information about the status of a project version (for example, approved or initially tested) or other. |
| Commit | A process of sending the changes made in the project to the server. Each commit creates a new project version. |
| Author | A user who has committed a particular project version. |
| Update | A process of getting from the server the latest version of a project you are working with. |
| Category | A concept for grouping projects in a Teamwork Cloud repository. |
| Locked item | A part of the model that can be edited by the user, who has locked it. Other users cannot edit locked items. The locks can be released during the commit of a new version. |
| Server project usage | Another server project used in the project you are working with. It can be a library, profile or regular project. |
| Branch | A duplicate of a server project version. Branching allows the users to work in parallel on the same project version. |
| Trunk | A permanent branch of a server project that is a base for all other branches. It cannot be deleted. |

##### Scenario to get started

Independent of what server your team or enterprise uses, we offer you to follow these steps to get started with collaborative modelling:

1. Obtain user's credentials. Creating users and deciding the access rights of the users against projects is an administrative task. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User Guide' space='TWCloud2024x'].
2. Log into a server.
3. Add a project to the server and/or open it.
4. Lock the elements you want to edit (to prevent other users could not change the elements you are working with) and edit them. You can skip this step if you are working in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Lock-Free Editing mode' space=''].
5. Edit these elements and/or create new ones.
6. Commit the changes to the server so that other users could see these modifications.

419214738

**Related pages**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7061b7a1-63f4-4aea-a7d7-67b2b52c6aee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418781380</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c87d60c9-b181-41bc-a20a-feab7fbdf26c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418781391</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e87e19c7-8bf6-47eb-ad55-f7dfe70ad9e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418781378</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38e17bfc-501c-48d4-962e-ff2dd6e48a97" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba511c13-eddf-4bd6-bd9b-ec72dba9e5ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418781381</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7422ab67-e5de-471f-bac7-a427cdd946e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Team members can individually develop separate parts of a single model and then merge them together to form a complete model. In comparison to individual modeling, collaborative modeling is more effective and produces better results because each member employs his/her best skills and experience.</p><p>We offer you a server for your team's collaboration – <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:link-body>collaboration capabilities powered by <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>Both servers provide a repository for storing projects and users. They also support the management of users' permissions as well as sharing and versioning projects.</p><p>Projects stored in the server repository can be accessed through the network from multiple clients who have MagicDraw or any Cameo Suite product installed. Different users, depending on their role in a team or enterprise, can have different permissions to the projects.</p><p>The same model or even the same diagram can be accessed and modified in parallel. Every user may instantly obtain the newest version of the model as well as commit his/her own changes.</p><h3>Basic concepts</h3><p>For better understanding further material, get acquainted with basic concepts of collaborative modeling.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Concept</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><h4>Repository</h4></td><td>A place for storing projects and users managed by Teamwork Cloud.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>User</h4></td><td colspan="1">A collaboration team member, who has credentials to log into <span>Teamwork Cloud </span>and holds permissions to access one or more server projects.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><h4><span>Teamwork Cloud</span> project</h4></td><td>A project stored in <span>Teamwork Cloud</span> repository.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Server project</h4></td><td colspan="1">A common name for <span>Teamwork Cloud </span>projects.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Version</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>A snapshot of a project at a particular point in time. A new version of a project is created after successful commit of changes made in this project. All versions of the same project have unique numbers.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Comment</h4></td><td colspan="1">An optional description about changes in a project version.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Tag</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>Information about the status of a project version (for example, <em>approved</em> or <em>initially tested</em>) or other.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Commit</h4></td><td colspan="1">A process of sending the changes made in the project to the server. Each commit creates a new project version.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Author</h4></td><td colspan="1">A user who has committed a particular project version.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Update</h4></td><td colspan="1">A process of getting from the server the latest version of a project you are working with.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Category</h4></td><td colspan="1">A concept for grouping projects in a <span>Teamwork Cloud </span>repository.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Locked item</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>A part of the model that can be edited by the user, who has locked it. Other users cannot edit locked items. The locks can be released during the commit of a new version.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Server project usage</h4></td><td colspan="1">Another server project used in the project you are working with. It can be a library, profile or regular project.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Branch</h4></td><td colspan="1">A duplicate of a server project version. Branching allows the users to work in parallel on the same project version.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><h4>Trunk</h4></td><td colspan="1">A permanent branch of a server project that is a base for all other branches. It cannot be deleted.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Scenario to get started</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(64,64,64);">Independent of what server your team or enterprise uses, we offer you to follow these steps to get started with collaborative modelling:<br /></span></p><ol><li><p>Obtain user's credentials.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdf54cab-334a-4c97-bf90-2e090c9170ce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Creating users and deciding <span style="color: rgb(64,64,64);">the access rights of the users against projects is an administrative task. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="User Guide" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Teamwork Cloud User Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Log into a server.</li><li>Add a project to the server and/or open it.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Lock the elements you want to edit (to prevent other users could not change the elements you are working with) and edit them.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d67b2692-c71a-45d6-bc55-e968302f380f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can skip this step if you are working in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Lock-Free Editing mode" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock-Free Editing mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Edit these elements and/or create new ones.</li><li>Commit the changes to the server so that other users could see these modifications.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ed50fcc-4c1f-4c83-bdeb-23e99c2c8b7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419214738</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="63cfd5a4-d5c2-42db-b8e2-d155cbb4f010" /><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715222 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00207: Collecting presentation elements, finding usages in diagrams

- page_id: `136715222`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715222/Collecting+presentation+elements+finding+usages+in+diagrams
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:45:45.382+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Very often you need to find out if some Element has a presentation element in a diagram. You also might need to collect all existing presentation elements for Element. There are different approaches for doing it. Choose the one which suites your needs.

Keep in mind that diagrams in MagicDraw uses lazy content loading approach. It means that content (presentation elements) are loaded only on demand. For example when diagram is opened.

**Use SymbolElementMap**

You can take this map using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getSymbolElementMap()*. Map has API for collecting all symbols in all diagrams for some specific Element.

Presentation elements can be collected only from loaded diagrams. If diagram is not loaded, presentation elements of this diagram are not registered in a map.

Load diagram using *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement.ensureLoaded()*

****

**Find element usages in loaded and not loaded diagrams (do not load diagrams)**

Use this approach if you are not sure if presentation element exists in a diagram. This approach checks existing of presentation elements without loading a diagram.

*DiagramPresentationElement* API provides very useful methods for that. This API works both in loaded and not loaded diagrams.

*DiagramPresentationElement.getUsedModelElements(boolean)* to get all displayed elements in the diagram

Use** *DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(* *Element)* to check if Element has a presentation element in a diagram

If you have not loaded diagram and you need to get presentation element of some symbol, you can do the following

- Use DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram( Element) to check if element is in diagram
- Use DiagramPresentationElement.*ensureLoaded()* to load a diagram
- Use Project.getSymbolElementMap() API to get a reference to presentation element in a diagram

****

**Find presentation elements in diagrams and load them**

Use this approach only if you are sure that presentation element of Element exists in diagram, because diagram will be loaded always.

Use *DiagramPresentationElement.findPresentationElement(Element, java.lang.Class)* method

More information is available in javadoc.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Very often you need to find out if some Element has a presentation element in a diagram. You also might need to collect all existing presentation elements for Element. There are different approaches for doing it. Choose the one which suites your needs.</p><p>Keep in mind that diagrams in MagicDraw uses lazy content loading approach. It means that content (presentation elements) are loaded only on demand. For example when diagram is opened.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Use SymbolElementMap</strong></p><p>You can take this map using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getSymbolElementMap()</em>. Map has API for collecting all symbols in all diagrams for some specific Element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7f94ca3-a7c2-4ffc-b4a3-46dc51de4759"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Presentation elements can be collected only from <u>loaded</u> diagrams. If diagram is not loaded, presentation elements of this diagram are not registered in a map.</p><p>Load diagram using <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement.ensureLoaded()</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"> <br /></strong></p><p><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Find element usages in loaded and not loaded diagrams (do not load diagrams)</strong></p><p>Use this approach if you are not sure if presentation element exists in a diagram. This approach checks existing of presentation elements without loading a diagram.</p><p><em>DiagramPresentationElement</em> API provides very useful methods for that. This API works both in loaded and not loaded diagrams.</p><p><em>DiagramPresentationElement.getUsedModelElements(boolean)</em> to get all displayed elements in the diagram</p><p>Use<em> </em> <em>DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(</em> <em>Element)</em> to check if Element has a presentation element in a diagram</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34d13d2a-0f3a-478a-afb7-93e04f472406"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have not loaded diagram and you need to get presentation element of some symbol, you can do the following</p><ul><li>Use<em> </em> <em>DiagramPresentationElement.isElementInDiagram(</em> <em>Element)</em> to check if element is in diagram</li><li>Use <em>DiagramPresentationElement.<em>ensureLoaded() </em> </em> <em> </em>to load a diagram</li><li>Use <em>Project.getSymbolElementMap()</em> API to get a reference to presentation element in a diagram</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong> <br /></strong></p><p><strong>Find presentation elements in diagrams and load them </strong></p><p>Use this approach only if you are sure that presentation element of Element exists in diagram, because diagram will be loaded always.</p><p>Use <em>DiagramPresentationElement.findPresentationElement(Element, java.lang.Class)</em> method</p><p><br /></p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711684 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00208: Collecting symbol styles from used projects

- page_id: `136711684`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711684/Collecting+symbol+styles+from+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:50.071+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Stereotype properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

As of MagicDraw version 17.0.3, a used project in which the symbol style is defined is displayed next to the symbol style in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x']** and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties**dialogs. This functionality also allows for copying a symbol style from a used project to the current project symbol style.

You can see the particular used project from which the symbol style is used and copy the symbol style from the used project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the example, you can see that in the project there are two Subsystem symbol styles - one style from the *BPMN Profile.mdzip* used project and the other from the *UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip* used project. The Subsystem symbol style from the *BPMN Profile.mdzip* used project is used in the current project.

The symbol style from used project is not editable because the used project by itself is read-only. By copying the symbol style from the used project to the project you can create a new symbol style and then edit it.

Note that there are no rules for the symbol style usage from used projects - symbols styles are used according to used projectsloading order.

To copy a symbol style to the project style by using the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x']** dialog

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MD2024x'] dialog, select Symbol styles > Default > Stereotypes options group, choose a symbol style that you want to copy. [ATTACHMENT filename='styles_from_modules3.png']
2. Click the Copy to Project Style button. The new symbol style is created in the project. This symbol style is now used in the project and this is the editable symbol style. [ATTACHMENT filename='styles_from_modules4.png']

To copy a symbol style from a used project in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog , click the Apply Style button and from the drop-down list, select a style from a used project that you want to copy.
2. Click the Make Default button.
3. Edit the symbol style.
4. Click OK to close the dialog.
5. Reopen the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog . In the expanded Apply Style drop-down list, you can see the newly created symbol style. This is the copy of the selected style from the used project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>As of MagicDraw version 17.0.3, a used project in which the symbol style is defined is displayed next to the symbol style in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>dialogs. This functionality also allows for copying a symbol style from a used project to the current project symbol style.</p><p>You can see the particular used project from which the symbol style is used and copy the symbol style from the used project.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of symbol styles from modules that are displayed in the Project Options dialog" ac:alt="Example of symbol styles from modules that are displayed in the Project Options dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="styles_from_modules8.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Collecting symbol styles from used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of symbol styles from modules that are displayed in the Symbol Properties dialog" ac:alt="Example of symbol styles from modules that are displayed in the Symbol Properties dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="styles_from_modules.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Collecting symbol styles from used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In the example, you can see that in the project there are two Subsystem symbol styles - one style from the <em>BPMN Profile.mdzip</em> used project and the other from the <em>UML_Standard_Profile.mdzip</em> used project. The Subsystem symbol style from the <em>BPMN Profile.mdzip</em> used project is used in the current project.</p><p>The symbol style from used project is not editable because the used project by itself is read-only. By copying the symbol style from the used project to the project you can create a new symbol style and then edit it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df5cd90c-d7b3-4c46-a538-203c80a67c22"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Note that there are no rules for the symbol style usage from used projects - symbols styles are used according to <span>used projects </span>loading order.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To copy a symbol style to the project style by using the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Symbol</strong> <strong>styles</strong> &gt; <strong>Default</strong> &gt; <strong>Stereotypes</strong> options group, choose a symbol style that you want to copy.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Example of copying symbol style from module" ac:alt="Example of copying symbol style from module"><ri:attachment ri:filename="styles_from_modules3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Collecting symbol styles from used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Copy to Project Style</strong> button. The new symbol style is created in the project. This symbol style is now used in the project and this is the editable symbol style.<br /><ac:image ac:title=" Example of copied symbol style" ac:alt=" Example of copied symbol style"><ri:attachment ri:filename="styles_from_modules4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Collecting symbol styles from used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p> </p><p>To copy a symbol style from a used project in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, click the Apply Style button and from the drop-down list, select a style from a used project that you want to copy.</li><li>Click the <strong>Make Default</strong> button.</li><li>Edit the symbol style.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog.</li><li>Reopen the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. In the expanded Apply Style drop-down list, you can see the newly created symbol style. This is the copy of the selected style from the used project.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of copied symbol style that is listed in Symbol Properties dialog" ac:alt="Example of copied symbol style that is listed in Symbol Properties dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="styles_from_modules7.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Collecting symbol styles from used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711038 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00209: Combined Fragment

- page_id: `136711038`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711038/Combined+Fragment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:16.893+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Fragment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

876860645

876860648

876860646

Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.

The UML Combined Fragment element allows the expressions of interaction fragments to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']. The Combined Fragments provide a means to define particular conditions and subprocesses for any sections of lifelines in the Sequence diagram by specifying an area where the conditions or subprocesses apply. Using the Combined Fragment, a fragment of a Sequence diagram can be separated.

To draw a Combined Fragment

1. In the Sequence diagram toolbar, select the Alternatives element to draw.
2. From the Combined Fragment shortcut menu, select the Covered Lifelines command and in the Covered Lifelines dialog, select the lifelines to display.
3. In the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the Options group select the Parallel Combined Fragment to draw.
4. Draw the Critical Region Combined Fragment from the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the Option button group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

876860644

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alternative Fragment' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fragment' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e596c3f3-30dc-4049-9952-af852b79cacc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">876860645</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32c26da5-0192-40fd-82ae-04327e63d099"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">876860648</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="324e6c1d-97c2-44de-8999-4c34a1243c54"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">876860646</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion. </p><p>The UML Combined Fragment element allows the expressions of interaction fragments to be defined in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link>. The Combined Fragments provide a means to define particular conditions and subprocesses for any sections of lifelines in the Sequence diagram by specifying an area where the conditions or subprocesses apply. Using the Combined Fragment, a fragment of a Sequence diagram can be separated. </p><p><br /></p><p>To draw a Combined Fragment</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Sequence diagram toolbar, select the Alternatives element to draw.</li><li>From the Combined Fragment shortcut menu, select the <strong>Covered Lifelines</strong> command and in the Covered Lifelines dialog, select the lifelines to display.</li><li>In the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the <strong>Options</strong> group select the <strong>Parallel</strong> Combined Fragment to draw. </li><li>Draw the <strong>Critical Region</strong> Combined Fragment from the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the <strong>Option</strong> button group. </li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.08.00 PM.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd976d2d-6bed-4b89-8be6-f75277a7b1be"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">876860644</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Alternative Fragment" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Fragment" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710950 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00210: Comment

- page_id: `136710950`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710950/Comment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:08.284+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A **Comment** is a UML element used to specify various remarks of an element in the model. In the diagram palette, under **Common**, the Comment command appears under the Note command. You can display text in the Comment as plain or HTML text.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a comment.

You can format the Comment's symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

You can specify a Comment in the Comment's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'], which also displays the description of each Comment property in the description area of the Specification window.

To create a comment in the element Specification window

1. Open the Specification window of the selected element.
2. In the panel on the left, select the Documentation/Comments property group.
3. Under the Comments pane, click Add . The Comment's Specification window opens. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Type the Comment content as the **Body** property value and click **OK**.
5. Close the Specification window.

To view the **Documentation/Comments** symbol on the diagram pane, expand the**Documentation/Comments**property group and drag the selected documentation/comment on the diagram pane.

To switch a comment to HTML or Plain Text

Do one of the following:

- On the diagram pane, select the Comment symbol. Click the button that appears on the lower-left corner of the shape:
  - The HTML button - to switch the text to the HTML text. The Plain button - to switch the text to the Plain text. [ATTACHMENT filename='comment_switch_to_html.png']

#### TIP: HTML text

HTML text

You can edit the HTML text in the comment using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MD2024x'].

To display an anchored element documentation in a comment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Anchor' space='MD2024x'] a Comment to a symbol on the diagram pane.
2. Select the Comment on the diagram pane. Open its shortcut menu.
3. Select the Retrieve Documentation command. The anchored symbol's documentation is displayed on the Comment’s symbol.

#### TIP: Retrieve Documentation

Retrieve Documentation

You can enable the **Retrieve Documentation** option **only** if you already have documentation in the Comment's Specification window. Read the first set of instructions on this page to see how to create the comment in the element's Specification window.

#### TIP: Edit

Edit

You can edit element documentation directly on the Comment symbol.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Click the button that appears on the lower-left corner of the shape:<ul><li>The <strong>HTML</strong> button - to switch the text to the HTML text.<br />The <strong>Plain</strong> button - to switch the text to the Plain text.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="111"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_switch_to_html.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Comment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="471f6d9b-6129-4147-ad25-9f84cece96fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTML text</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can edit the HTML text in the comment using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display an anchored element documentation in a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Anchor" /></ac:link> a Comment to a symbol on the diagram pane.</li><li>Select the Comment on the diagram pane. Open its shortcut menu.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="30d0409a-5445-4345-93e0-f4cc22f8786b">Select the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="30d0409a-5445-4345-93e0-f4cc22f8786b">Retrieve Documentation</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="30d0409a-5445-4345-93e0-f4cc22f8786b"> command. </ac:inline-comment-marker><br />The anchored symbol's documentation is displayed on the Comment’s symbol.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80451aa7-c458-4c7e-8de8-9e8483d6d683"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Retrieve Documentation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can enable the <strong>Retrieve Documentation</strong> option <strong>only</strong> if you already have documentation in the Comment's Specification window.
