<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714537 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00211: Comment and unparsing code

- page_id: `136714537`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714537/Comment+and+unparsing+code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:40.033+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Velocity templating language
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Comment

Comment is used for explaining or making notes regarding the code. You can include text comments in the report. The report engine will not parse comments.

To make a single line comment

- Type **##** before the comment. The report engine will skip all the text in the line, as follows:

To make multi-line comments

- Type **#*** followed by the multi-line comments and end them with ***#**. The report engine will skip all the lines between these symbols, as follows:

#### Unparsing code

You can declare the code in Velocity template without parsing it into the report. The report engine will not parse the code.

To unparse code

- Type **#[[** followed by the code to unparse and end it with **]]#**, as follows: #[[
 session_start();
 YOUR CODE HERE
 ?
 ]]#

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h2>Comment</h2><p>Comment is used for explaining or making notes regarding the code. You can include text comments in the report. The report engine will not parse comments.</p><p><br /></p><p>To make a single line comment</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Type <strong>##</strong> before the comment. The report engine will skip all the text in the line, as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ded1f61e-1866-4f15-bf90-58c03653b281"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[##Comment here]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p>To make multi-line comments</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Type <strong>#*</strong> followed by the multi-line comments and end them with <strong>*#</strong>.  The report engine will skip all the lines between these symbols, as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2e93bf9-6bbf-4e48-860c-3594bf830819"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#*
	Comment line1
	Comment line2
	Comment line3
*#]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p><br /></p><h2>Unparsing code</h2><p>You can declare the code in Velocity template without parsing it into the report. The report engine will not parse the code.</p><p><br /></p><p>To unparse code</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Type <strong>#[[</strong> followed by the code to unparse and end it with <strong>]]#</strong>, as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c739a728-55e5-4f10-b714-22838c6ba8f2"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<html>
	<body>
		#[[
			<?php
			session_start();
			YOUR CODE HERE
			?>
		]]#
	</body>
</html>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713453 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00212: Committing changes to 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `136713453`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713453/Committing+changes+to+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-12-12T14:18:17.220+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After you finish editing a server project, you must commit the changes to the **3D**EXPERIENCE****platform so that other users working on the same project would be able to see them. When you commit project changes, new project data from your modeling tool is merged with the project stored on the **3D**EXPERIENCE****platform. As a result, a new project iteration (version) is created.

To commit project changes to the **3D**EXPERIENCE****platform

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Collaborate > Commit Changes to Server .
2. In the **Comment** box of the open dialog, optionally describe the changes you are going to commit.
3. If you want to keep the locks on all diagrams, leave the option Keep locks selected.
4. If you want to view the local changes you are about to commit, click the**More**button in the**Commit Project to the Server**dialog. In the**Local Changes**tab, added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue. [ATTACHMENT filename='commiting_project_to_server.png']
5. Click the Commit button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After you finish editing a server project, you must commit the changes to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE<strong> </strong>platform so that other users working on the same project <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="875c48d0-21f7-4e1d-b308-0a70c1a183d7">would be able to see them</ac:inline-comment-marker>. When you commit project changes, new project data from your modeling tool is merged with the project stored on the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE<strong> </strong>platform. As a result, a new project iteration (<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="71d50800-d636-4e04-8797-42ee0ec6d1b5">version)</ac:inline-comment-marker> is created.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To commit project changes to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE<strong> </strong>platform</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Commit Changes to Server</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Comment</strong> box of the open dialog, optionally describe the changes you are going to commit.</p></li><li>If you want to keep the locks on all diagrams, leave the option <strong>Keep locks </strong>selected.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>If you want to view the local changes you are about to commit, click the </span><strong>More</strong><span> button in the </span><strong>Commit Project to the Server</strong><span> dialog. In the </span><strong>Local Changes</strong><span> tab, added elements are highlighted in green, deleted elements are highlighted in red, and modified or moved elements are highlighted in blue.</span><br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commiting_project_to_server.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713197 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00213: Committing changes to Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `136713197`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713197/Committing+changes+to+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:13:11.452+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After you finish editing the model, you must commit the changes to the server to enable other team members to see these changes when editing other parts of the model. When you commit the changes, new data from your client application is merged with the project on the server repository. As a result, a new version of that project is created. Other team members must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Updating changes from Teamwork Cloud' space=''] from the server to see the committed changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When committing changes, you can choose to release or keep the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='']. If you are going to continue editing after the commit is over, keep the locks.

To commit changes to Teamwork Cloud

1. From the Collaborate menu, select Commit Changes To Server , or press Ctrl+K. The Commit Project to the Server dialog opens.
2. If you need to describe the changes you are going to commit, type a comment in the open dialog. You can select a comment from the recently used comments list. Click the Choose Comment button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] above the **Comment** box, and select any comment from the list in the **Choose Comment** dialog.Last comment written in the **Comment** box is saved as draft after user clicks**Cancel**instead of**Commit**in a **Commit Project To the Server** dialog. Next time when user opens **Commit Project To the Server**dialog, he will be notified about saved comment draft, withChoose Comment button [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In addition, in the**Choose Comment**dialog user will see saved comment marked as a draft.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. If you want to keep locks on the model and continue editing after the commit is over, select the **Keep locks** check box. The **Keep locks** check box remembers your last choice. Thus, if you want to release the locks every time when committing changes, once you make your choice during the first commit you don't need to unselect this check box each time.
4. If you want to tag the new project version, such as an *Loan final* tag, do the following: If you want to tag the new project version with a **Major** tag, do the followingClick **More**. The **Version****Tags** tab appears on the **Commit Project to the Server** dialog.Check the **Create major version** checkbox. This will create the *Major* tag.[IMAGE alt='' src='']falseNotifications on new versionNow you can specify tags that will trigger a notification if a new used project version with a specified tag is committed to the Teamwork Cloud. Learn more about [Setting for notification about version changes](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Changing+the+used+project+version#Changingtheusedprojectversion-Settingfornotificationaboutversionchanges).
  1. Click **More**. The **Version****Tags** tab appears on the **Commit Project to the Server** dialog.
  2. Click the **Add** button.
  3. In the open dialog, type a text and click**OK**.
5. You can also see local changes of the new project version. Click **More**and the **Local Changes** tab appears on the **Commit Project to the Server** dialog. All project elements with changes are listed in this tab. You may do one of the following: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Click the legend to see changes to elements.
  - Click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing element history and inspecting changes' space=''] to explore changes to a selected element more deeply.
6. In the **Commit Project to the Server** dialog, click **Commit**.
7. Wait while a new project version containing the committed changes is sent to the Teamwork Cloud repository. In case of commit failure commit message and commit tags are saved for next commit attempt, so user does not have to re-type commit information.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After you finish editing the model, you must commit the changes to the server to enable other team members to see these changes when editing other parts of the model. When you commit the changes, new data from your client application is merged with the project on the server repository. As a result, a new version of that project is created. Other team members must <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Updating changes from Teamwork Cloud" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update their projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the server to see the committed changes.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Committing is necessary for other team members to see the changes you've made " ac:alt="Committing is necessary for other team members to see the changes you've made "><ri:attachment ri:filename="working_scheme.png" /></ac:image></p><p>When committing changes, you can choose to release or keep the<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[locks on the model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>. If you are going to continue editing after the commit is over, keep the locks.</p><p><br /></p><p>To commit changes to Teamwork Cloud</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Collaborate</strong> menu, select <strong>Commit Changes To Server</strong>, or press Ctrl+K. The <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3807a123-4284-4fce-9423-c066258c5672">Commit Project to the Server</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>If you need to describe the changes you are going to commit, type a comment in the open dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e17a0e0-89a1-4664-bbe9-a5228935ce50"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select a comment from the recently used comments list. Click the Choose Comment button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choose_comment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> above the <strong>Comment</strong> box, and select any comment from the list in the <strong>Choose Comment</strong> dialog.</p><p>Last comment written in the <strong>Comment</strong> box is saved as draft after <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">user clicks </span><strong>Cancel </strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">instead of</span><strong> Commit </strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">in a <strong>Commit Project To the Server</strong> dialog. Next time when user opens <strong>Commit Project To the Server </strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">dialog, he will be notified about saved comment draft, <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">with </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Choose Comment button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Comment draft_1.PNG" /></ac:image>. <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">In addition, in the </span><strong>Choose Comment </strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">dialog user will see saved comment marked as a draft.</span></span></span></span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Comment draft.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>If you want to keep locks on the model and continue editing after the commit is over, select the <strong>Keep locks</strong> check box.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba7daa40-c2b2-4a19-862d-49867331864d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Keep locks</strong> check box remembers your last choice. Thus, if you want to release the locks every time when committing changes, once you make your choice during the first commit you don't need to unselect this check box each time.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>If you want to tag the new project version, such as an <em>Loan final</em> tag, do the following:<br /><br /></p><ol><li><p>Click <strong>More</strong>. The <strong>Version </strong><strong>Tags</strong> tab appears on the <strong>Commit Project to the Server</strong> dialog.</p></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</p></li><li><p>In the open dialog, type a text and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="84003adc-7224-42cf-a196-36ba86dd89d8">click </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="84003adc-7224-42cf-a196-36ba86dd89d8">OK</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="84003adc-7224-42cf-a196-36ba86dd89d8">.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae98d9bc-1b00-497e-9f41-6e6b807a77a7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to tag the new project version with a <strong>Major</strong> tag, do the following</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>More</strong>. The <strong>Version </strong><strong>Tags</strong> tab appears on the <strong>Commit Project to the Server</strong> dialog.</p></li><li><p>Check the <strong>Create major version</strong> checkbox. This will create the <em>Major</em> tag. </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_Major_Version.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b47b801-0f56-41b4-aaa3-036b2c5be55f"><ac:parameter ac:name="icon">false</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Notifications on new version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Now you can specify tags that will trigger a notification if a new used project version with a specified tag is committed to the Teamwork Cloud. Learn more about <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Changing+the+used+project+version#Changingtheusedprojectversion-Settingfornotificationaboutversionchanges">Setting for notification about version changes</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>You can also see local changes of the new project version. Click <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">More </strong>and the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Local Changes</strong> tab appears on the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Commit Project to the Server</strong> dialog. All project elements with changes are listed in this tab. You may do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Click the legend to see changes to elements.</li><li>Click <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Comparingelementversions"><ri:page ri:content-title="Reviewing element history and inspecting changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Quick Diff]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>to explore changes to a selected element more deeply.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Reviewing local changes while committing the project" ac:alt="Reviewing local changes while committing the project"><ri:attachment ri:filename="commiting_viewing_changes.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Commit Project to the Server</strong> dialog, click <strong>Commit</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Wait while a new project version containing the committed changes is sent to the Teamwork Cloud repository.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1be947bd-c3ad-476e-aa2a-8308a62b7ec7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In case of commit failure commit message and commit tags are saved for next commit attempt, so user does not have to re-type commit information.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709776 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00214: Communication diagram

- page_id: `136709776`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709776/Communication+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:07.352+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

307612157

307612159

307612158

**Overview**

A Communication diagram is a type of Interaction diagram, which falls under the behavioral diagramming family.The Communication diagram is the UML 2.0 version of the Collaboration diagram. It illustrates various static connections between objects, models, and their interactions. It also presents a collaboration that contains a set of instances as well as their required relationships given in a particular context, and includes an interaction that defines a set of messages. These messages specify the interaction between the classifier roles within a collaboration that will serve to achieve the desired result.

A Communication diagram is given in two different forms: at the instance level or at the specification level.

**Purpose**

A Communication diagram can be used to model the interactions between objects or parts in terms of sequenced messages. It represents a combination of information taken from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x'] diagrams describing both the static structure and dynamic behavior of a system.

**Usage**

A Communication diagram can be used to:

- model message passing between objects or roles that deliver functionalities of Use Cases and operations
- capture interactions that show the messages passed between objects and roles
- model alternative scenarios within Use Cases or operations that involve the collaboration of different objects and interactions
- model mechanisms within the architectural design of the system
- identify objects, their attributes, and operations that participate in Use Cases.

**Summary**

Communication diagrams are valuable because they:

- reveal the structural requirements that are necessary to complete a task
- explicitly identify the objects that participate in an interaction
- reveal the interface requirements of the participating classes
- identify the structural changes required to satisfy an interaction
- identify data that passed as part of interactions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Communication diagram

307612155

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709431 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00215: Communication diagram elements

- page_id: `136709431`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709431/Communication+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:48.818+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Special Transformation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

492179710

492179713

492179709

**On this page**

3

492179711

##### Object, Boundary, Entity, and Control

All Object, Boundary, Entity, Control elements in EA are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024x']. After conversion, their UML element types will remain, and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MD2024x']and an OwnedAttribute element will be created to represent each of them in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='MD2024x'] in which they occur.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Object, Boundary, Entity, and Control (diagram view).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Object, Boundary, Entity, and Control (Containment tree).

Other elements that can be drawn in a Communication diagram will be handled in a similar manner.

##### Exception Elements

Some of the elements that can be drawn in a Communication diagram using EA are not supported by MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. Consequently, their displaying parts will not be imported. These elements include [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram frame' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exception Handler' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object Node' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing regions' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object Node' space='MD2024x'], Trigger, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes' space='MD2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024x'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exception elements.

###### Realization and Nesting

The Realization element type in EA is *uml:Realization*. The Nesting element type in EA is exported in XMI as *uml:Dependency*. The Realization and Nesting lines in EA are not supported in the Communication diagram; therefore, only their model data will be imported, not the displaying parts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Realization and Nesting.

##### Association

Every Association relationship drawn in a Communication diagram in EA will have a Connector line created for each of them. The elements attached to both ends of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association End' space='MD2024x']line will have a Lifeline element created to represent each of them. The Association lines and the elements attached to them will not be removed. However, the elements that will be shown in the diagram frame will be the Lifeline elements and the Connector lines created to represent them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Association.

If an Association line is connected to the InstanceSpecification elements, its data will be removed. If an Association line is linked between two InstanceSpecification elements, it will be transformed into an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024x']element. This is one of the constraints belonging to the Communication diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Association Line between InstanceSpecifications.

##### Message

Messages can be created on Connectors and will be imported to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Message.

##### Operations

An InstanceSpecification cannot contain Operation elements. If the XMI file from EA has some InstanceSpecification elements containing Operation elements, those Operations will be removed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Operations.

492185720

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710891 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00216: Communication Path

- page_id: `136710891`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710891/Communication+Path
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:05.031+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Association
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Communication path is a type of association between nodes in a deployment diagram that shows how the nodes exchange messages and signals.

When you specify communication paths, you can define the number of nodes that can be connected at each end. You can also use labels to identify the type of protocol or network that is used in the communication.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Communication path is a type of association between nodes in a deployment diagram that shows how the nodes exchange messages and signals.</p><p class="p">When you specify communication paths, you can define the number of nodes that can be connected at each end. You can also use labels to identify the type of protocol or network that is used in the communication.</p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00217: Comparing projects

- page_id: `136712736`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712736/Comparing+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-12-01T13:45:52.302+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis
- labels: ['base', 'compared', 'compare-concepts']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

641794038

641794050

641794040

The Compare Projects functionality allows you to compare:

- Two server project versions
- Open server project with a local or server project version
- Open a local project with a server project version

Various different properties of the elements that share the same ID in different projects, are compared.

##### Basic concepts

For a better understanding of the further material, learn the following concepts.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Base | The original project version without changes. |
| Compared | A project version that contains modifications added to the original project version. |

641794035

**Related pages**

2

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715080 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00218: Comparing projects for testing purposes

- page_id: `136715080`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715080/Comparing+projects+for+testing+purposes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:04.094+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Plugins > Creating test cases
- labels: ['projects-comparator', 'model-comparator', 'model-comparator-filter', 'diagram-comparator']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To compare two projects for testing purpose, the test framework provides the [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ProjectsComparator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html) class with the *[compare(](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html#compare-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)[Project](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html)[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html#compare-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)[Project](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html)[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html#compare-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project-)* method for comparing two projects (see the following figure). The default implementation of [*ProjectComparator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html)can be created using the[*createProjectComparator(java.lang.String)*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/MagicDrawTestCase.html#createProjectComparator-java.lang.String-) method of the *MagicDrawTestCase* class. Projects can be compared by checking their model elements and element symbols in diagrams for equality.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The model elements comparison can be configured using the [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ModelComparator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ModelComparator.html) interface. Developers may exclude some model elements from the comparison by providing the custom [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.ModelComparatorFilter*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ModelComparatorFilter.html) interface implementation. Please note that the default implementation of [*ProjectComparator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html)does not compare the diagram information table, root model comment, and elements from used projects.

The default implementation of [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.tests.common.comparators.DiagramComparator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/DiagramComparator.html) compares diagrams for changes by analyzing a location and a size of the symbols presented in diagrams. The graphical differences of compared diagrams can be saved in the graphical PNG file using the *[saveDiffToImageFiles(java.io.File)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/DiagramComparator.html#saveDiffToImageFiles-java.io.File-)* method of the [DiagramComparator](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/DiagramComparator.html)interface.

Developers may create their own comparators for the custom diagrams and model elements comparison by implementing [*ModelComparator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ModelComparator.html)and [*DiagramComparator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/DiagramComparator.html)interfaces. The custom *Model* and *Diagram* comparators implementation can be set to [*ProjectComparator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/tests/common/comparators/ProjectsComparator.html)using appropriate setter methods.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710670 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00219: Compartments

- page_id: `136710670`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710670/Compartments
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:50:44.293+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

569915046

569915048

569915047

The compartment is an area on the shape or next to the shape that is dedicated to the specific type of information.

There are the following types of compartments:

- Compartment boxes. For example, the class shape has compartments that put attributes, operations, and signals into boxes. [ATTACHMENT filename='compartments.png']

- You can hide compartments by suppressing them or expand the hidden compartments to see their data.
- Compartment names are displayed by default. However, you can hide them by changing their visibility settings either in the Symbol Properties dialog or the Project Options dialog. Each compartment has its own property for visibility settings (for example, Show Attributes Compartment Name ).

- Compartments that are displayed next to the element name on the shape. For example, a shape has compartments for stereotypes, constraints, and others.

- Stereotypes (or stereotypes compartment) are displayed above the element name.
- Constraints (or constraints compartment) are displayed under the element name.
- By default, tagged values are displayed under the element name, or you can select to display tagged values in the compartment box.
- Element Properties are displayed in the compartment box.

- Compartments that are displayed next to the element name, next to the shape. For example, an Actor has a compartment for element properties, a compartment for stereotypes, behaviors, and others.
- Compartments on paths that are displayed next to the path label. For example, a compartment for element properties, a compartment for stereotypes, and others.

569915045

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Path compartments' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715059 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00220: Compilation classpath

- page_id: `136715059`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715059/Compilation+classpath
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:02.634+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Plugins
- labels: ['patch', 'brand', 'jar']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Core libraries**

The correct classpath must be specified when compiling your (plugin) code or running `*.jar` files from *`<modeling tool installation directory>\lib`*, as well as all its subdirectories must be included into the classpath.

You can open the properties file in`*<modeling tool installation directory>\bin*`and check the `CLASSPATH` property to be sure what files must be included.

Make sure that following *.jar files are added in such order:

1. patch.jar
2. brand.jar (if the file exists) . This file is needed if the Cameo Enterprise Architecture program is used. MagicDraw does not requires this file.
3. brand_api.jar (if the file exists)*
4. Rest *.jar files. Order of them is not important

Make sure that only the *.jar files coming with the current program version you are included. If you have upgraded your modeling tool from an earlier version, the *lib* directory may contain files from the earlier version. Do not include these obsolete *.jar files.

**Plugins libraries**

Keep in mind that some Open API classes may come from plugins (like the SysML plugin, for example). In this case, make sure that *jar*files from a plugin directory are added to the compiled classpath. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying plugin dependencies' space='MD2024x'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Order of them is not important<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="379fd914-ffb0-475a-8fe2-bf0ddd4645e7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure that only the *.jar files coming with the current program version you are included. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710794 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00221: Complete diagrams

- page_id: `136710794`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710794/Complete+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:01.293+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Updating multiple paths

31649184

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Updating multiple paths

31649186

31649182

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Updating multiple paths

31649185

##### Marking diagrams as complete

When you finish working on a diagram, you expect that the diagram layout will not change. However, if the content of your model is changed, the diagram layout changes as well; shapes are automatically resized to fit new content and labels are automatically displayed on paths. This can cause symbols to overlap and paths to be left hanging in a diagram, as illustrated below. So you must go back to the finished diagram and fix its layout.To save time, you can mark diagrams as complete andprevent their layout from being automatically updated.

700500

To mark a diagram as complete

- Right-click the diagram on the diagram pane or in the model browser and from the menu select Complete .

When a diagram is marked as complete, the following happens:

- The «complete» stereotype is applied to a diagram. This way you can easily indicate complete diagrams in the model browser without opening them.
- Auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels is suspended. This means that the diagram layout is not automatically changed when the model content changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure displays an example of a complete diagram.

#### NOTE: Alternative

Alternative

You can suspend auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in a diagram without marking it as complete in one of the following ways:

- To suspend auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels for individual symbols, o pen the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog of a symbol or a group of symbols, and set the Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes and/or Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels properties to true .
- To suspend auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels for all the diagram symbols, o pen the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying diagram style properties' space='MD2024x']**Diagram Properties** dialog of a diagram, and set the Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes and/or Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels properties to true.

##### Updating complete diagram layout

When a diagram is marked as complete, the symbols with suspended content related to model changes are highlighted in blue (see the figure above). You can update these symbols manually, as described below.

To update shapes

1. In a complete diagram, select the highlighted shape you want to update.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Drag one of the selection handles on the corner of the shape to resize it. The suspended content is dynamically displayed after resizing the shape.
  - Click the smart manipulator on the bottom of the shape, as shown below. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_shape.png']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='solver.png'] on the shape toolbar and select Resize to Fit Content .

#### TIP: Updating multiple shapes

Updating multiple shapes

To update multiple shapes in a complete diagram, select the highlighted shapes you want to update and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the diagram toolbar.

To update paths

1. In a complete diagram, select the highlighted path you want to update.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the smart manipulator on the path, as shown below. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_path.png']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='solver.png'] on the path toolbar and select Show Suspended Labels .

#### TIP: Updating multiple paths

Updating multiple paths

To update multiple paths in a complete diagram, select the highlighted paths you want to update and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the diagram toolbar.

##### Marking diagrams as incomplete

If you need to resume normal work on a diagram, mark it as incomplete, as described below.

To mark a diagram as incomplete

1. Right-click a diagram on the diagram pane or in the model browser and select the Complete option to clear its check box.
2. When the question dialog is displayed, do one of the following:
  - Select **Yes**, to continue suspending auto-resizing of shapes and/or auto-displaying of labels.
  - Select No , to resume auto-resizing of shapes and/or auto-displaying of labels.

If you select**No**in step 2, all diagram symbols are automatically resized to fit suspended content, andyou can continue working on the diagram as usual. If you select **Yes**, auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in the diagram are still suspended, even though the diagram is not complete.

##### Customizing complete diagram behavior

By default, auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in a complete diagram are suspended. Also, the symbols with suspended content due to model changes are highlighted in blue. You can change this default behavior in the **Project Options** dialog.

To customize complete diagram behavior

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, expand the General node and select Diagrams .
3. On the right side of the dialog, specify the desired options in the Complete Diagrams group:
  - Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes - set to true to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model.
  - Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels - set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels in a diagram while editing the model.
  - Highlight Suspended Symbols - set to true to highlight symbols that have suspended content related to model changes.

When you specify project options listed in step 3, they are applied to all diagrams that are marked as complete after specification.

##### **Check complete diagrams**

To check complete diagrams

- In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Analyze > Check Complete Diagrams .

The **Check Complete Diagrams**action checks for complete diagrams without completely visible content by running the following three validation rules:

| Validation rule | Error message | Severity | Profile |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Suspended Autodisplaying | The symbol content is not completely visible. Auto-displaying of labels is suspended. | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile |
| Suspended Autoresizing | The symbol content is not completely visible. Auto-resizing of shapes is suspended. | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile |
| Suspended Autochanges | The diagram contains symbols whose content is not completely visible. | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe6478ea-38ed-4100-a010-476f2f82af3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">31649184</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65c27722-ae16-4182-ad68-70e6a9d58995"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">31649186</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f60a082b-0878-442a-988c-aacb5c806494"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">31649182</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a3d3a0d-87b9-4be2-a1e6-fff6474fc74c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7bde8e3-a27c-4d12-bd7b-ce56f19ad8ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">31649185</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Marking diagrams as complete</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="834fe518-37ef-4695-8dc1-9d724225be7d">When you finish working on a diagram, you expect that the diagram layout <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8699c10a-64e7-443e-97d2-bf23bbe489f4">will not change</ac:inline-comment-marker>. However, if the content of your model is changed, the diagram layout changes as well; <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">shapes are automatically resized to fit new content and labels are automatically displayed on paths. This can cause symbols to overlap and paths to be left hanging in a diagram, as illustrated below. So you must go back to the finished diagram and fix its layout. </span>To save time, you can mark diagrams as complete and </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d5ee668f-8ddb-4894-b55e-7caa5b86134e"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="834fe518-37ef-4695-8dc1-9d724225be7d">prevent their layout from being automatically updated.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="171704bf-9248-4ad2-8488-15ce80247af2"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lPj4knb0JfY&amp;feature=youtu.be" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">500</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);"><br /></span></p><p>To mark a diagram as complete</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the diagram on the diagram pane or in the model browser and from the menu select <strong>Complete</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d31772ee-38ea-4cc3-b158-7937f5d04955">When a diagram is marked as complete</ac:inline-comment-marker>, the following happens:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4dee7dec-5fe0-4f29-b7ed-1446985121db">The «complete» stereotype is applied to a diagram. This way you can easily indicate complete diagrams in the model browser without opening them.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4dee7dec-5fe0-4f29-b7ed-1446985121db">Auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels is suspended. This means that the diagram layout is not automatically changed when the model content changes.<br /><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="complete_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The figure displays an example of a complete diagram.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40bbb078-4d3c-4f42-af4a-076adb667cad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Alternative</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can suspend auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in a diagram without marking it as complete in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>To suspend <span>auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels for individual symbols, o</span>pen the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> of a symbol or a group of symbols, and set the <strong>Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</strong> and/or <strong>Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels</strong> properties to <em>true</em>.</li><li>To suspend <span>auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels for all the diagram symbols, o</span>pen the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specifying diagram style properties" /><ac:link-body><strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> of a diagram, and set the <strong>Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</strong><span> and/or </span><strong>Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels</strong><span> properties to </span><em>true.</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Updating complete diagram layout</h3><p>When a diagram is marked as complete, the symbols with suspended content related to model changes are highlighted in blue (see the figure above). You can update these symbols manually, as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="86efe6dd-ebdb-47b2-917c-0eac9ad4bc1b">update</ac:inline-comment-marker> shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>In a complete diagram, select the highlighted shape you want to update.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Drag one of the selection handles on the corner of the shape to resize it. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="31395402-df94-4a3c-bdce-e2cbbbf321e9">The suspended content is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8c9350f9-dd0d-4d84-a1fc-cfc36665fee5">dynamically</ac:inline-comment-marker> displayed after resizing the shape.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Click the smart manipulator on the bottom of the shape, as shown below.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_shape.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="solver.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the shape toolbar and select <strong>Resize to Fit Content</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a6d417b-8437-4169-904e-12c917ebf25a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating multiple shapes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d9afad5e-1a0c-4f99-814d-0c31b200c81d">update multiple shapes</ac:inline-comment-marker> in a complete diagram, select the highlighted shapes you want to update and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resize_to_fit_content.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram toolbar.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To update paths</p><hr /><ol><li>In a complete diagram, select the highlighted path you want to update.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Do one of the following:</span><br /><ul><li>Click the smart manipulator on the path, as shown below.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_path.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="solver.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the path toolbar and select <strong>Show Suspended Labels</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ebb5b2fd-3e1d-43b1-9dea-cd4b4e4b35b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating multiple paths</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>To update multiple paths in a complete diagram, select the highlighted paths you want to update and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_suspended_labels.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram toolbar.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Marking diagrams as incomplete</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e19ed52e-ea35-4234-8846-966198a91fa1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d2f2d779-d6ba-46a3-ae59-1c0f2ca3b3f7">If</ac:inline-comment-marker> you need to resume normal work on a diagram, mark it as incomplete, as described below.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="27f48bad-1eea-4d44-96a8-a8312f1f29a3">To mark a diagram as incomplete</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><span>Right-click a diagram on the diagram pane or in the model browser and select the </span><strong>Complete</strong><span> option to clear its check box.</span></li><li><span>When the question dialog is displayed, do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span>Select <strong>Yes</strong>, to continue suspending auto-resizing of shapes and/or auto-displaying of labels.</span></li><li>Select <strong>No</strong>, to resume auto-resizing of shapes and/or auto-displaying of labels.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de16dfcb-8c59-4e2a-8e1c-eb4f72b31c85">If you select </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de16dfcb-8c59-4e2a-8e1c-eb4f72b31c85">No</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de16dfcb-8c59-4e2a-8e1c-eb4f72b31c85"> in step 2, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">all diagram symbols are automatically resized to fit suspended content, and </span>you can continue working on the diagram as usual.</ac:inline-comment-marker> If you select <strong>Yes</strong>, auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in the diagram are still suspended, even though the diagram is not complete.</p><h3><br />Customizing complete diagram behavior</h3><p>By default, auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in a complete diagram are suspended. Also, the symbols with suspended content due to model changes are highlighted in blue. You can change this default behavior in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize complete diagram behavior</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> node and select <strong>Diagrams</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the dialog, specify the desired options in the <strong>Complete Diagrams</strong> group:<ul><li><strong>Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</strong> - set to <em>true</em> to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model.</li><li><strong>Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels</strong> - set to <em>true</em> if you do not want to automatically show labels in a diagram while editing the model.</li><li><strong>Highlight Suspended Symbols</strong> - set to <em>true</em> to highlight symbols that have suspended content related to model changes.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="900c833b-d566-4ac3-9fa8-00af21ec26d9">When you specify project options listed in step 3, they are applied to all diagrams that are marked as complete after specification.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><h3><strong>Check complete diagrams</strong></h3><p>To check complete diagrams</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, select <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Check Complete Diagrams</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="900c833b-d566-4ac3-9fa8-00af21ec26d9">The <strong>Check Complete Diagrams </strong>action checks for complete diagrams without completely visible content by running the following three validation rules:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 78.296%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 20.2714%;" /><col style="width: 30.6063%;" /><col style="width: 9.77742%;" /><col style="width: 24.2493%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Validation rule</th><th colspan="1">Error message</th><th colspan="1">Severity</th><th>Profile</th></tr><tr><td>Suspended Autodisplaying</td><td colspan="1">The symbol content is not completely visible. Auto-displaying of labels is suspended.</td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td>UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile</td></tr><tr><td>Suspended Autoresizing</td><td colspan="1">The symbol content is not completely visible. Auto-resizing of shapes is suspended.</td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td>UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile</td></tr><tr><td>Suspended Autochanges</td><td colspan="1">The diagram contains symbols whose content is not completely visible.</td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td>UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710957 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00222: Component

- page_id: `136710957`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710957/Component
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:08.575+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Component represents all kinds of elements that make up the system. A Component can always be considered as an autonomous unit within a system or subsystem. It is a module having the following features:

- A Component is a replaceable and independent part of the system performing a specific action.
- A Component acts in a context of a well-defined architecture.
- Components interact among each other using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MD2024x'] .

Components provides compartments for listing its provided and required Interfaces, realizations, and artifacts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A Component can be specified by changing its property values in the Component [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

To show/hide the interfaces, realizations, and artifacts on the Component’s shape, do one of the following

- On the diagram, right-click the Component’s shape and then from the shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties . In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog , set the Suppress Interfaces , Suppress Realizations , or Suppress Artifacts property values to true or false .
- On the diagram, click the Component’s shape and then click the Compartments button [ATTACHMENT filename='compartments.png'] . Then in the menu, click to select or clear the Interfaces , Realizations , or Artifacts check box.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Component represents all kinds of elements that make up the system. A Component can always be considered as an autonomous unit within a system or subsystem. It is a module having the following features:</p><ul><li>A Component is a replaceable and independent part of the system performing a specific action.</li><li>A Component acts in a context of a well-defined architecture.</li><li>Components interact among each other using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interfaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p>Components provides compartments for listing its provided and required Interfaces, realizations, and artifacts.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="An example of a Component" ac:alt="An example of a Component"><ri:attachment ri:filename="component_listed_properties.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Component" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>A Component can be specified by changing its property values in the Component <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</p><p> </p><p>To show/hide the interfaces, realizations, and artifacts on the Component’s shape, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram, right-click the Component’s shape and then from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, set the <strong>Suppress Interfaces</strong>, <strong>Suppress Realizations</strong>, or <strong>Suppress Artifacts</strong> property values to <em>true</em> or <em> false</em>.</li><li>On the diagram, click the Component’s shape and then click the Compartments button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. Then in the menu, click to select or clear the <strong>Interfaces</strong>, <strong>Realizations</strong>, or <strong>Artifacts</strong> check box.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Component diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709806 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00223: Component diagram

- page_id: `136709806`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709806/Component+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:08.748+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: ['implementation', 'implementation-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308582438

308582440

308582439

**Overview**

A Component diagram falls under the structural diagramming family.A Component diagram represents pieces of software in the implementation environment in terms of code components.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MD2024x'] contains information about the logical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] or Classes that it implements, thus creating a mapping from a logical view to a component view. Dependencies between the Components make it easy to analyze how a change in one component affects the others. The Components may also be shown with any of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MD2024x'] that they expose. They, as with almost any other model elements, can be grouped into [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x'], much like Classes or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024x'].

**Purpose**

A Component diagram models the implementation view. It is used in the later phases of the software development when there is a need to divide up Classes among different Components. When working with the CASE facilities, the Components are used for file-class mapping during code generation, reverse engineering, and round-trip engineering operations.****

**Usage**

A Component diagram can be used to:

- analyze system components and how they relate
- analyze system's physical structure
- understand the exact service behavior provided by software.

**Summary**

Component diagrams are valuable because they:

- reveal possible software configuration issues
- provide an accurate picture of existing systems prior to making changes/enhancements
- model the real software in the implementation environment
- help to reveal bottlenecks in an implementation.

As of MagicDraw 17.0.1, the Component diagram (or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='MD2024x']) replaces the Implementation diagram, which is no longer supported in UML standard. An Implementation diagram created with earlier versions of MagicDraw, now opens as

- Deployment diagram, if Nodes were used in the Implementation diagram
- Component diagram, if Nodes were not used in the Implementation diagram

Customized diagrams that were based on the Implementation diagram are now based on the Component diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Component diagram

308582437

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Dependencies between the Components make it easy to analyze how a change in one component affects the others. The Components may also be shown with any of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interfaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that they expose. They, as with almost any other model elements, can be grouped into <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, much like Classes or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>A Component diagram models the implementation view. It is used in the later phases of the software development when there is a need to divide up Classes among different Components. When working with the CASE facilities, the Components are used for file-class mapping during code generation, reverse engineering, and round-trip engineering operations.<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A Component diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>analyze system components and how they relate</li><li>analyze system's physical structure</li><li>understand the exact service behavior provided by software. </li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Component diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>reveal possible software configuration issues</li><li>provide an accurate picture of existing systems prior to making changes/enhancements</li><li>model the real software in the implementation environment</li><li>help to reveal bottlenecks in an implementation. <br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aaed2bc2-2897-474c-b567-d871b34da546"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="confluence-link">As of MagicDraw 17.0.1, the Component diagram (or the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Deployment diagram" /></ac:link>) replaces the Implementation diagram, which is no longer supported in UML standard. An Implementation diagram created with earlier versions of MagicDraw, now opens as</p><div class="confluence-information-macro-body"><ul><li>Deployment diagram, if Nodes <em>were used</em> in the Implementation diagram</li><li>Component diagram, if Nodes <em>were not used</em> in the Implementation diagram</li></ul><p>Customized diagrams that were based on the Implementation diagram are now based on the Component diagram.</p></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="component_diagram_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Component diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Component diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08d36afe-7d48-42c1-b72e-10091cb4c272"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308582437</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710959 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00224: Component realization

- page_id: `136710959`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710959/Component+realization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:08.735+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Component Realization concept is specified in the Components package to (optionally) define the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the classifier' space='MD2024x']. The Classifier defines the contract offered by a component in terms of its provided and required interfaces. The component forms an abstraction from these various Classifiers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A Component Realization concept is specified in the Components package to (optionally) define the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specifying the classifier" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Classifier]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The Classifier defines the contract offered by a component in terms of its provided and required interfaces. The component forms an abstraction from these various Classifiers.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709611 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00225: Composing model

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709824 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00226: Composite Structure diagram

- page_id: `136709824`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709824/Composite+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:52:04.351+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308768822

308768824

308768823

**Overview**

A Composite Structure diagram falls under the structural diagramming family.The Composite Structure diagram allows a decomposition and modeling of the internal structure of the classifiers, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MD2024x'], and other elements that are able to own other elements.

**Purpose**

A Composite Structure diagram can be used to visually represent the parts of a class, component, or collaboration, including the interaction points (called ports) that are used to access features of the structure. Additionally, Composite Structure diagrams model collaborations that describe behavior, resources used to perform the behavior, and the roles the participating resources take during the behavior.

**Usage**

Composite Structure diagram can be used to:

- analyze an object to see exactly what it is composed of
- detail internal actions of a class, including the relationships of nested classes
- show objects that need to be defined as a composition of other classified objects.

**Summary**

Composite Structure diagrams are valuable because they:

- show complex components design
- show components interfaces separately from their structures
- describe the roles played by elements in the structure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Composite Structure diagram

308768820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']

Additionally, Composite Structure diagrams model collaborations that describe behavior, resources used to perform the behavior, and the roles the participating resources take during the behavior. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>Composite Structure diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>analyze an object to see exactly what it is composed of</li><li>detail internal actions of a class, including the relationships of nested classes</li><li>show objects that need to be defined as a composition of other classified objects.</li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Composite Structure diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>show complex components design</li><li>show components interfaces separately from their structures</li><li>describe the roles played by elements in the structure.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite_structure_diagram_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Composite Structure diagram</h6><p style="text-align: center;" /></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8757ca47-889e-4532-a8fc-fde1ea1e61fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308768820</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="37910c25-6072-43db-9977-ce601fbb01ae" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00227: Composite Structure Diagram context

- page_id: `136709826`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709826/Composite+Structure+Diagram+context
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-29T14:58:15.796+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > UML diagrams > Composite Structure diagram
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space=''] for the element that cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='']), the new context element (which is a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']) is created automatically.

INLINE

##### Turning off automatic creation of a diagram context element

To turn off automatic creation of a diagram context element

1. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] , select Project > Options .
2. In the Project Options dialog, find the Diagram Context option.
3. Select the **Do Not Create** value.
4. Click OK . The automatic creation of a diagram context element is turned off.

This means that when youcreate a newdiagramforan element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. [Package](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Package)),the**Select Diagram Context**dialog will open automatically.It allows you to select an existing element as a context, create a new context element by enabling the**Creation Mode**, or click the**Cancel**button if you do not want to specify the diagram context. The diagram will be created in the Package.

If you want to enable the automatic creation of a diagram context element, select the **Create Automatically** value for the **Diagram Context** option.

##### Changing the diagram context element

After the diagram is created, you can change its context element.

To change the diagram context element

1. Right-click the diagram and select Specification .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']Specification window , find the Context property.
3. Click on its value cell, and select [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] .
4. In the **Select Diagram Context** dialog, do one of the following: 
 - Select an existing element as a context in the **Tree** or **List** tab 
 - Create a new context element by enabling the **Creation Mode**>**Create**and select it.
5. Click **OK**. The diagram context element is changed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
