<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709460 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00228: Composite Structure diagram elements

- page_id: `136709460`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709460/Composite+Structure+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:49.927+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Special Transformation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

559286067

559286070

559286066

**On this page**

3

559286069

This section presents additional EA specific Composite Structure diagram information.

##### Import elements

EA differs from MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM in the Composite Structure diagram's content elements design. The table below shows the differences by focusing on the EA’s elements and how they will be transformed or converted into a format MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM can correctly load and display. The element names shown in the table are the same in both EA and MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM GUI. The contents in the brackets ([…]) are the XMI element type references.

| Enterprise Architect | MagicDraw, CEA, CSM |
| --- | --- |
| Interaction |  |
| Class [uml:Class] | Class [uml:Class] |
| Interface [uml:Interface] | Interface [uml:Interface] |
| Part [uml:Class]Part [uml:Property] | Part [uml:Property] |
| Port [uml:Port] | Port [uml:Port] |
| Collaboration [uml:Collaboration] | Collaboration Use [uml:Collaboration] |
| Expose Interface [not exist] |  |
| Connector [element type is not exist] | Connector [uml:Connector] |
| Assembly [element type is not exist] |  |
| Delegate [element type is not exist] | Connector [uml:Connector] |
| Role Binding [uml:Dependency] | Role Binding [uml:Dependency] |
| Represents [uml:Dependency] | Dependency [uml:Dependency] |
| Occurrence [uml:Dependency] | Dependency [uml:Dependency] |

##### Conversion details

###### Dummy class

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] needs a Context element to contain itself. However, in EA there is no Context element. After conversion, a dummy [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x']element will be created to represent the Context element of Composite Structure diagram.

A dummy Class will be named after the closest owner package of the Composite Structure diagram. The Composite Structure diagram and all of the Composite Structure elements will be placed inside the dummy Class element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dummy class.

###### Class

The Class element data will be normally copied even if the Class element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Class element. The Property element will be named after the Class element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Representing a Class Element.

###### Class with Attributes and Operations

The Class view in EA will be converted to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x']in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='MD2024x']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MD2024x']will not be shown in the Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part Element after Conversion.

###### Interface

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MD2024x']element data will be normally copied even if the Interface element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Interface element. The Property element will be named after the Interface element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Representing Interface Element.

###### Part

There are two types of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x']:

- Part with a port; and
- Part that sets a type to other elements.

###### **Part with Port**

If you assign the type of a Part element to a Class, Component, or Node, the Part element can have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x']. If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. Therefore, this Part element can have a Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that does not Nest Element(s) with Ports.

If a Part is nested to an element and the type is not set, then the Part type will be used to set type to a dummy Class. This particular Part can have a Port. A dummy Class will be created at the same level of the Part element that sets type to it. The dummy Class will be named after the Part concatenating with *_type*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Nests Element(s) with Post.

###### Part that sets type to other elements

The type of a Part can be set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Part element and selecting **Advanced**> **Set Property Type**> **Select Property Type.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting Part Type.

If the Part type is set to a type that cannot be the owner of a port, the port will be removed.

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Property element data cannot have any elements. However, in EA, it can have elements. Therefore, if you draw a Part element and it has an element inside it, that particular element will be relocated to an element, which is the Part type.

If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. The element inside the Part will not be relocated. If that particular Part assigns a type to other elements, its type will always be set to Class.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Sets Type to Other Elements.

If a Nested Part whose type is set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, or Usecase) and this Part contains Nested elements, all of the Nested elements of the Part will be relocated to the element that is the type of this Part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Part that Sets Type to Other Elements has Nested Element Inside Itself.

###### portPort

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a Property element that can have a Port is the Property that sets type to a Class, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MD2024x'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MD2024x'], and other elements cannot have a Port. However, in EA, most of the elements can have a Port.

If a Port is created with an element that is not a Class, Component or Node, the Port will be removed from the diagram view. However, its data will be preserved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Only Class, Component, and Node Can Have a Port.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that its type is set to Class, Component and Node only.

###### Port whose type is set to other elements

You can set the type of Port element to another element (a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Port element and selecting **Advanced**> **Set Property Type**> **Select Property Type.**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Setting Port Type.

###### Port whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces

If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the Port type is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element. For example, if the Port1 type is set to Actor, an Interface Realization (the Expose Interface data) will be placed inside the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MD2024x']after conversion.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Port that Sets Type to Other Element Contains Expose Interface.****

###### Collaboration

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space='MD2024x']element data and its nested data will normally be copied. However, if the Collaboration element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram, a Collaboration use element will be created to represent the Collaboration element. The Collaboration use element will be named after the Collaboration element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration use.

**Collaboration containing other elements**

A Collaboration can contain only Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction. Elements other than these will be removed. If any element is drawn inside the Collaboration, it will be removed from the diagram view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration Containing Other Elements.

The following transformation messages will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine and Interaction only.Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Element view. Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.

###### Expose Interface

Expose Interfaces are the Provided and Required interfaces in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. You can draw a Provided Interface or Required Interface in a Port only and set the type of the Port to another element (an Actor, Use Case, or Class). You can create that particular Port on a Part element whose type is set to a Class, Component, or Node only.

###### Expose Interface with Port

If you draw an Expose Interface in a Port and the Port does not assign a type to any elements, that particular Port will be used to set type to a dummy Class. The dummy Class will be created and named after the Port concatenating with *_type*. The Interface element will be moved to the same level of the Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Port.

If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the type of the Port is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram elements' space='MD2024x']whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces for more information).

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, some elements can be used as a Port type. These elements can have either a Provided Interface or Required Interface, or both.

The following is a list of the Port's type elements with specific conditions:

- Interfaces can only have a Provided Interface.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Artifact' space='MD2024x'] cannot have a Provided Interface.
- Information Items cannot have a Provided Interface.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Signal Event' space='MD2024x'] cannot have a Provided Interface.
- Components always have both Provided and Required Interfaces.
- The other elements can have both Provided and Required Interfaces.
- After conversion, if the type of a Port is set to an Interface and the Port has an Expose Interface, only the Provided Interface will be shown and the Interface Realization data will be removed.
- Artifacts, Information Items, and Signals cannot hold Interface Realization. If the type of a Port is set to one of them, the Provided Interface will be removed. However, this does not affect the Required Interface.
- If the type of a Port is set to a Component and the Port has an Expose Interface, it will always show both the Provided and Required Interfaces.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Port’s Type Element with Specific Conditions.

The following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.

If an Expose Interface is created with an element that is not a Port, the element will be removed both from the Containment tree and diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Element that is not Port.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.

###### Expose Interface with relationship

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Provided and Required Interfaces cannot be connected with any relationship. If there is any relationship connecting to the Expose Interface, it will be removed from both the Containment tree and diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expose Interface with Lines.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.

###### Package

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x']cannot be drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. If it is drawn in EA, it will be removed from the diagram once it has been converted to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Package.

###### Assembly

In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, there is no Assembly line. It will be updated to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024x'].

The following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Assembly updated to Connector.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Updating the Assembly Line to the Connector Line.

###### Dependency

There are many relationships that EA exporter exports to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MD2024x'] as the following elements:

- Delegate
- Role Binding
- Represents
- Occurrence
- Nest
- Derive
- Import
- Instantiate
- Usage
- Realize
- Trace

The above relationships will be shown in the diagram view as Dependencies with a stereotype.

###### Removed relationships

If an Association, Direct Association, Aggregation, Composition, Generalization, or Specialization is created in a Composite Structure diagram, it will be removed from the diagram view, but its data will be preserved.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Removing lines.

The following transformation messages will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.

##### Transformation report

A report containing the conflicts solved during transformation, along with other transformation information (such as special mapping and removal of some irrelevant data), is required to be provided to the users.

The following is a list of the transformation messages:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that set type to Class, Component, and Node only.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction only.Removed view <xmi:id>: Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.Removed view <xmi:id>: UseCase cannot be contained in any element view.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.Updated element <xmi:id>: Assembly updated to Connector.Updated element <xmi:id>: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.Removed view <xmi:id>: Association cannot be shown in CompositeStructure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.Removed view <xmi:id>: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.

559293207

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="158f7b73-1490-48a9-a999-4df2fe58b187"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62ac6b24-c1ed-4e94-9c0d-fe56da72572b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286070</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e56e68c8-3d58-444e-98f7-591b87e1679c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286066</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4913ad71-84d1-4193-be83-4f29b776f185"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9578db90-5035-4e33-a77a-fdc90bb7e706"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559286069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><p>This section presents additional EA specific Composite Structure diagram information.</p><h3>Import elements</h3><p>EA differs from MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM in the Composite Structure diagram's content elements design. The table below shows the differences by focusing on the EA’s elements and how they will be transformed or converted into a format MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM can correctly load and display. The element names shown in the table are the same in both EA and MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM GUI. The contents in the brackets ([…]) are the XMI element type references.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Enterprise Architect</th><th>MagicDraw, CEA, CSM</th></tr><tr><td>Interaction</td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Class [uml:Class]</span></td><td><span>Class [uml:Class] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Interface [uml:Interface]</span></td><td><p>Interface [uml:Interface]</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Part [uml:Class]</p><p>Part [uml:Property]</p></td><td><p>Part [uml:Property]</p></td></tr><tr><td><span>Port<span> </span>[uml:Port]</span></td><td><span>Port<span> </span>[uml:Port]</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Collaboration [uml:Collaboration]</span></td><td><span>Collaboration Use [uml:Collaboration] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Expose Interface [not<span> </span>exist]</span></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Connector [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><span>Connector [uml:Connector] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Assembly [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><span>Delegate [element type is not exist]</span></td><td><span>Connector [uml:Connector]</span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Role Binding [uml:Dependency]</span></td><td><span>Role Binding [uml:Dependency] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Represents [uml:Dependency]</span></td><td><span>Dependency [uml:Dependency] </span></td></tr><tr><td><span>Occurrence [uml:Dependency]            <span> </span></span></td><td><span>Dependency [uml:Dependency]</span></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Conversion details</h3><h4>Dummy class</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> needs a Context element to contain itself. However, in EA there is no Context element. After conversion, a dummy <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element will be created to represent the Context element of Composite Structure diagram.</p><p>A dummy Class will be named after the closest owner package of the Composite Structure diagram. The Composite Structure diagram and all of the Composite Structure elements will be placed inside the dummy Class element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dummyClass.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dummy class.</h6><h4>Class</h4><p>The Class element data will be normally copied even if the Class element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created  to represent the Class element. The Property element will be named after the Class element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="propertyrepresent_class.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Representing a Class Element.</h6><h5>Class with Attributes and Operations</h5><p>The Class view in EA will be converted to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Attribute" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Attributes ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operations ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>will not be shown in the Composite Structure diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="partElement_converted.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part Element after Conversion.</h6><h4>Interface</h4><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element data will be normally copied even if the Interface element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. A Property element will be created to represent the Interface element. The Property element will be named after the Interface element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="propertyrepresent_interface.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Representing Interface Element.</h6><h4>Part</h4><p>There are two types of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</p><ul><li>Part with a port; and </li><li>Part that sets a type to other elements.</li></ul><h5><strong>Part with Port</strong></h5><p>If you assign the type of a Part element to a Class, Component, or Node, the Part element can have a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Port ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. Therefore, this Part element can have a Port.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_withoutNestElement.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that does not Nest Element(s) with Ports.</h6><p>If a Part is nested to an element and the type is not set, then the Part type will be used to set type to a dummy Class. This particular Part can have a Port. A dummy Class will be created at the same level of the Part element that sets type to it. The dummy Class will be named after the Part concatenating with <em>_type</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_withNestElement.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Nests Element(s) with Post.</h6><h5>Part that sets type to other elements</h5><p>The type of a Part can be set to another element (such as  a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Part element and selecting  <strong>Advanced </strong>&gt; <strong>Set Property Type </strong>&gt; <strong>Select Property Type.</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="partType.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Setting Part Type.</h6><p>If the Part type is set to a type that cannot be the owner of a port, the port will be removed.</p><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Property element data cannot have any elements. However, in EA, it can have elements. Therefore, if you draw a Part element and it has an element inside it, that particular element will be relocated to an element, which is the Part type.</p><p>If a Part is not nested to any element, the Part element will be exported from EA as a Class. The element inside the Part will not be relocated. If that particular Part assigns a type to other elements, its type will always be set to Class.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_setType_otherElement.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Sets Type to Other Elements.</h6><p>If a Nested Part whose type is set to another element (such as a Class, Actor, or Usecase) and this Part contains Nested elements, all of the Nested elements of the Part will be relocated to the element that is the type of this Part.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_setType_otherElementwithNest.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Part that Sets Type to Other Elements has Nested Element Inside Itself.</h6><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfb33356-5197-4c86-acec-96a4507efd85"><ac:parameter ac:name="">port</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Port</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a Property element that can have a Port is the Property that sets type to a Class, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Component" /></ac:link>, or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Node" /></ac:link>, and other elements cannot have a Port. However, in EA, most of the elements can have a Port.</p><p>If a Port is created with an element that is not a Class, Component or Node, the Port will be removed from the diagram view. However, its data will be preserved.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_component_nodewithport.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Only Class, Component, and Node Can Have a Port.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that its type is set to Class, Component and Node only.</pre></blockquote><h5>Port whose type is set to other elements</h5><p>You can set the type of Port element to another element (a Class, Actor, Component, or Usecase) by right-clicking the Port element and selecting <strong>Advanced </strong>&gt; <strong>Set Property Type </strong>&gt; <strong>Select Property Type.</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="portType.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Setting Port Type.</h6><h5>Port whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces</h5><p>If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the Port type is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element. For example, if the Port1 type is set to Actor, an Interface Realization (the Expose Interface data) will be placed inside the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>after conversion.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_setTypetoElement.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Port that Sets Type to Other Element Contains Expose Interface.<strong><br /></strong></h6><h4>Collaboration</h4><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Collaboration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Collaboration ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element data and its nested data will normally be copied. However, if the Collaboration element is drawn in a Composite Structure diagram, a Collaboration use element will be created to represent the Collaboration element. The Collaboration use element will be named after the Collaboration element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaborationUse.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Collaboration use.</h6><p><strong>Collaboration containing other elements</strong></p><p>A Collaboration can contain only Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction. Elements other than these will be removed. If any element is drawn inside the Collaboration, it will be removed from the diagram view.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_containElement.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Collaboration Containing Other Elements.</h6><p>The following transformation messages will open:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine and Interaction only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element view. Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li></ul></blockquote><h4>Expose Interface</h4><p>Expose Interfaces are the Provided and Required interfaces in MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM. You can draw a Provided Interface or Required Interface in a Port only and set the type of the Port to another element (an Actor, Use Case, or Class). You can create that particular Port on a Part element whose type is set to a Class, Component, or Node only.</p><h5>Expose Interface with Port</h5><p>If you draw an Expose Interface in a Port and the Port does not assign a type to any elements, that particular Port will be used to set type to a dummy Class. The dummy Class will be created and named after the Port concatenating with <em>_type</em>. The Interface element will be moved to the same level of the Port.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withPort.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: right;">Expose Interface with Port.</h6><p>If an Expose Interface is created on a Port and the type of the Port is set to another element, the Expose Interface data will be relocated to the Port's type element (see <ac:link ac:anchor="port"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Port ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ac:link>whose type is set to another element and containing Expose Interfaces for more information).</p><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, some elements can be used as a Port type. These elements can have either a Provided Interface or Required Interface, or both.</p><p>The following is a list of the Port's type elements with specific conditions:</p><ul><li>Interfaces can only have a Provided Interface.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Artifact" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Artifacts ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li>Information Items cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Signal Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Signals ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot have a Provided Interface.</li><li>Components always have both Provided and Required Interfaces.</li><li>The other elements can have both Provided and Required Interfaces.</li><li>After conversion, if the type of a Port is set to an Interface and the Port has an Expose Interface, only the Provided Interface will be shown and the Interface Realization data will be removed.</li><li>Artifacts, Information Items, and Signals cannot hold Interface Realization. If the type of a Port is set to one of them, the Provided Interface will be removed. However, this does not affect the Required Interface.</li><li>If the type of a Port is set to a Component and the Port has an Expose Interface, it will always show both the Provided and Required Interfaces.</li></ul><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_withTypeElement.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Port’s Type Element with Specific Conditions.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.</pre></blockquote><p>If an Expose Interface is created with an element that is not a Port, the element will be removed both from the Containment tree and diagram pane.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withElement.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Expose Interface with Element that is not Port.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.</pre></blockquote><h5>Expose Interface with relationship</h5><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, the Provided and Required Interfaces cannot be connected with any relationship. If there is any relationship connecting to the Expose Interface, it will be removed from both the Containment tree and diagram pane.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exposeInterface_withLines.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Expose Interface with Lines.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.</pre></blockquote><h4>Package</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>cannot be drawn in a Composite Structure diagram. If it is drawn in EA, it will be removed from the diagram once it has been converted to MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="package.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Package.</h6><h4>Assembly</h4><p>In MagicDraw, CEA, or CSM, there is no Assembly line. It will be updated to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Connector" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Assembly updated to Connector.</pre></blockquote><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="assemblyLine_toConnectorLine.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Updating the Assembly Line to the Connector Line.</h6><h4>Dependency</h4><p>There are many relationships that EA exporter exports to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link> as the following elements:</p><ul><li>Delegate</li><li>Role Binding</li><li>Represents</li><li>Occurrence</li><li>Nest</li><li>Derive</li><li>Import</li><li>Instantiate</li><li>Usage</li><li>Realize</li><li>Trace</li></ul><p>The above relationships will be shown in the diagram view as Dependencies with a stereotype.</p><h4>Removed relationships</h4><p>If an Association, Direct Association, Aggregation, Composition, Generalization, or Specialization is created in a Composite Structure diagram, it will be removed from the diagram view, but its data will be preserved.</p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="removeLines.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Removing lines.</h6><p>The following transformation messages will open:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li></ul></blockquote><h3>Transformation report</h3><p>A report containing the conflicts solved during transformation, along with other transformation information (such as special mapping and removal of some irrelevant data), is required to be provided to the users.</p><p>The following is a list of the transformation messages:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Port. Port can be added to Part that set type to Class, Component, and Node only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Collaboration can contain Property, Activity, State Machine, and Interaction only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Collaboration cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: UseCase cannot be contained in any element view.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface can be added to Port only.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid Element. Expose Interface cannot be connected with the element.</pre></li><li><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Assembly updated to Connector.</pre></li><li><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Provided Interface conflicts with Port type. The Port type is updated to an owner of Interface Realization.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Association cannot be shown in CompositeStructure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Direct Association cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Aggregation cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Composition cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Generalization cannot be shown in Composite Structure diagram.</pre></li></ul></blockquote></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f3bce0e-d6b4-4f89-8bf3-c27fcd3116b7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">559293207</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714073 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00229: Concepts

- page_id: `136714073`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714073/Concepts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:06.859+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard includes the following concepts:

- Template specifies the document layout, format, corresponding model elements, and custom defined fields.
- Report Data specifies a set of custom fields and data in the selected template. A template can have a number of different custom defined fields for a project or different projects organized in a report.
- Report a generated user document with data from the project displayed in the selected template style.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard includes the following concepts:</p><ul><li><strong>Template<br /></strong>specifies the document layout, format, corresponding model elements, and custom defined fields.</li><li><strong>Report Data</strong> <br />specifies a set of custom fields and data in the selected template. A template can have a number of different custom <br />defined fields for a project or different projects organized in a report.</li><li><strong>Report</strong> <br />a generated user document with data from the project displayed in the selected template style.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709306 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00230: Configuration files

- page_id: `136709306`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709306/Configuration+files

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Sample property value

1228358741

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Sample property value

1228358744

1228387255

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Sample property value

1228358743

#### EXCERPT: Sample property value

INLINE

A configuration file is where the initial parameters and settings of a program are defined. All default parameter values come with a program installation but you can edit configuration files and define your own values, for example, define the Java home directory. This page describes the locations where you can find the configuration files of your modeling tool, and how you can modify them and change their default storage location. Configuration files can be used to:

- Define application arguments
- Set the Java Home
- Change Java parameters (such as memory allocation)
- Update default Java classpath (advanced: for plugin developers)

The configuration files can be opened and modified using any standard text or code editor. Once modified, changing the default location of configuration files can allow you to store multiple configuration files for different versions of the tool.

This page covers all modeling tools:

- MagicDraw
- Cameo Enterprise Architecture
- Cameo Systems Modeler
- Magic Software Architect
- Magic Cyber Systems Engineer
- Magic Systems of Systems Architect

By default the configuration and auxiliary files of a modeling tool are stored in the following location:

| OS | Configuration files storage |
| --- | --- |
| WindowsVista/7/8/10 | C:\Users\<USERNAME>\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version number> |
| Other OS | <user.home>/.<modeling tool name>/< modeling tool version number> |

#### INFO: Modeling tool file naming

Modeling tool file naming

In a file system, modeling tools' folders are named as follows:

| Modeling tool | File name |
| --- | --- |
| MagicDraw | magicdraw |
| Cameo Enterprise Architecture | cameoea |
| Cameo Systems Modeler | cameo.systems.modeler |
| Magic Software Architect | magic.software.architect |
| Magic Cyber Systems Engineer | magic.cyber.systems.engineer |
| Magic Systems of Systems Architect | magic.systems.of.systems.architect |

##### Getting the path to configuration files

If you do not know the exact path to your modeling tool configuration files, follow the steps below to get it.

To get the path to the configuration files

1. From the Help menu, select About <modeling tool name>. The About dialog opens.
2. Click the Environment tab.
3. Click the hyperlink next to Configuration Files . The folder containing modeling tool configuration files opens.

You can also save configuration files in any of the following locations:

- <modeling tool installation directory>
- Common Application Data location (on Windows OS)
- Another directory

##### Storing configuration files in the installation directory or Common Application Data location on Windows

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The Common Application Data location on Windows Vista/7/8/10 is *C:\ProgramData*.

To store configuration files in the modeling tool installation directory or Common Application Data location on Windows

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, change the -DLOCALCONFIG property value according to the following table:

| -DLOCALCONFIG | =true | =false |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Windows OSx | Common Application Data | Modeling tool installation directory |
| Other | <user.home>/.<modeling tool name>/< modeling tool version number> | Modeling tool installation directory |

#### WARNING: Important!

Important!

It is important to add the **-DWINCONFIG=false** parameter on Window OS, otherwise, the configuration files will not be stored in the modeling tool installation directory.

You may also store configuration files in your custom location.

##### Storing configuration files in a custom location

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you have defined to store files in the modeling tool installation directory, files will not be stored in the defined location.

To store modeling tool configuration files in a custom location

Do either:

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dlocalconfig.location=<absolute path to a custom location> Sample property value-Dlocalconfig.location\=E\:\\temp\\innerPack

Or:

1. Go to the folder, where .
2. Create a new file named *magicdrawredirect*.
3. In the file, type the absolute path to the folder, wherein modeling configuration files will be saved, e.g., C:/<directory name>.

1228358739

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715083 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00231: Configure test environment

- page_id: `136715083`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715083/Configure+test+environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:04.370+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Plugins > Creating test cases
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

502787105

502787126

502787116

Test cases can be run as a regular JUnit test case using the JUnit *TestRunner* class or configuration tools such as Ant or Maven. However, some test case specific system properties should be set.

The program installation directory should be also specified using the *install.root* system property which can be passed to JVM as a runtime argument *-Dinstall.root=path_to_modeling_tool_installation_directory*.

It is possible to set up the program installation to use the floating license server or license file. For more information, see the "Providing licensing information" chapter at [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Licensing information' space='MD2024x'].

Test cases may use external resources such as configuration files and projects during tests. It is recommended to keep external test resources apart from test case implementation class files. The test framework provides methods for retrieving test resources from the one specific resource directory. The resource directory for test cases can be configured by specifying the*tests.resources* system property of JVM. The tests resource property can be passed to JVM as a runtime argument

```text

```

The Log4j logger used in test cases can be configured by specifying the *test.properties* file which should be placed in *<modeling tool installation directory>\data*.

The program is started in a "silent" mode when running tests - the user interference requiring dialogs is not shown, instead of the warning "*WARN GENERAL - TRYING TO DISPLAY "<dialog title>" DIALOG IN SILENT MODE*" is logged. The stack trace is logged too if a special system property *-DprintSilentDialogStackTrace=true* is set.

502787104

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Developing plugins using IDE' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Licensing information' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713309 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00232: Configure used projects

- page_id: `136713309`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713309/Configure+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:25.579+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all used projects and their versions In the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog.

To open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog

1. Login to the Teamwork Cloud server.
2. Open your server project.
3. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages . The Project Usages dialog opens.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The ****Advanced Project Usages Configuration**** dialog opens. Search barYou can use the search bar to faster filter used projects by name. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this dialog you can make various modifications for one or more projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and:

- Click the Notify on New Version check-box to get notifications when a new version of a particular project is available on the server.
- Use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space='MD2024x'] to the server project you are working with
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stop using the project in your project' space='MD2024x'] from the server project you are working with.
  - Start using an indirectly used project directly.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the used project version' space='MD2024x'] of a selected used project.
  - Review the information of a selected used project.
  - Reset changes to a selected used project. You must do this before applying selected actions!

In the **Action**column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the **Apply** button is clicked.

To turn the action on, click one of the following:

- Apply - to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note, the applied changes cannot be undone.
- OK - to apply changes and close the dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong><strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong></strong> dialog opens.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2f9b9a6-313b-47ff-b4ef-12b29d090646"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Search bar</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can use the search bar to faster filter used projects by name.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="advanced_project_usages_configuration_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configure used projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><p>In this dialog you can make various modifications for one or more projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and:</p><ul><li>Click the <strong>Notify on New Version </strong>check-box to get notifications when a new version of a particular project is available on the server.</li><li>Use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:<ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Start using a project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add a new project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the server project you are working with</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stop using the project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove a used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the server project you are working with.</li></ul><ul><li>Start using an indirectly used project directly.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Changing the used project version" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change the version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a selected used project.</li><li>Review the information of a selected used project.</li><li>Reset changes to a selected used project. You must do this before applying selected actions!</li></ul></li></ul><p>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the <strong>Apply</strong> button is clicked.</p><p>To turn the action on, click one of the following:</p><ul><li><strong>Apply </strong>- to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note, the applied changes cannot be undone.</li><li><strong>OK </strong>- to apply changes and close the dialog.</li></ul>

## PAGE 00233: Configuring cell entries - dependencies

- space_key: `MD2024x`

- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:24:16.540+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Working with Dependency Matrix > Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['dependency-extractor', 'configure-dependency-handlers', 'dependency-matrix-configurator', 'get-dependencies', 'dependency-entry', 'dependency-editor', 'can-create', 'create-add-actions', 'can-edit', 'create-edit-actions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In order to get the dependencies between row and column elements in the dependency matrix, components of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyExtractor* interface are used. These components allow to

- Define custom dependencies that can be created in a cell.
- Add shortcut menu commands for navigation (other than opening the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of the element and selecting the element in the Containment tree).
- Construct smart listener configurations that define cases when some element dependencies should be updated instantly (without the full rebuild of the matrix).

Use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator.configureDependencyHandlers(java.util.Collection<**DependencyExtractor>, java.util.Collection<DependencyEditor>)* method to register a custom dependency extractor. The method can remove the default dependency extractor from the collection and leave only a custom extractor.

The most important method is com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyExtractor.getDependencies(ElementNode, ElementNode). It is called to add dependencies in the matrix cell. By using custom algorithms you can create additional instances of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.DependencyEntry* class for each cell.

###### Configuring the shortcut menu of a cell

All commands of the shortcut menu of a cell, are available only from the components of the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.editing.DependencyEditor* interface. These components allow for

- Defining if new dependency can be created in the cell.
- Defining if current cell value can be edited.
- Adding custom actions for creating or editing dependencies.

Use the *DependencyMatrixConfigurator.configureDependencyHandlers(java.util.Collection<**DependencyExtractor>, java.util.Collection<DependencyEditor>)* method to register a custom dependency editor. The method implementation can remove the default dependency editor from the collection and leave only a custom editor.

Dependency matrix cells with the default renderer can be displayed either as editable or as read-only. If it is possible to create or delete at least one dependency in the cell, the cell is displayed as editable, that is, the cell's background is white.

When adding a new command, the *DependencyEditor.canCreate(**PersistenceManager, ElementNode, ElemenNode, AbstractMatrixCell)* method is first called and if it returns *true*, the *DependencyEditor.createAddActions(**PersistenceManager, *ElementNode*, *ElementNode*, AbstractMatrixCell, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory, ActionsCategory)* method is called to add the command.

When editing a command, the *DependencyEditor.canEdit(**PersistenceManager, **ElementNode**, **ElementNode**, AbstractMatrixCell)* method is first called and if it returns *true*, the *DependencyEditor.createEditActions(PersistenceManager[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)**ElementNode**[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)**ElementNode**[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)AbstractMatrixCell[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)ActionsCategory[,](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)ActionsCategory[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/datamodel/editing/DependencyEditor.html#createEditActions-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.persistence.PersistenceManager-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element-com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.datamodel.cell.AbstractMatrixCell-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-com.nomagic.actions.ActionsCategory-)* method is called to edit the command.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715283 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00234: Configuring element Specification window

- page_id: `136715283`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715283/Configuring+element+Specification+window
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:25:01.234+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Using and extending other UI components
- labels: ['ispecification-node-configurator', 'node', 'tree']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Open API provides a way to configure the elements' [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. With your own configurator you can create new nodes or remove already existing nodes. Nodes are items of the tree visible on the left side in every [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

The *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dialogs.specifications.SpecificationDialogManager*****class should be used for registering a specification dialog configurator***com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dialogs.specifications.configurator.ISpecificationNodeConfigurator*.

More information is available in javadoc.

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>/openapi/examples/elementspecification*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Open API provides a way to configure the elements' <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification windows]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. With your own configurator you can create new nodes or remove already existing nodes. Nodes are items of the tree visible on the left side in every <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dialogs.specifications.SpecificationDialogManager<strong> </strong></em>class should be used for registering a specification dialog configurator<em> </em><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.dialogs.specifications.configurator.ISpecificationNodeConfigurator</em>.</p><p>More information is available in javadoc.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;/openapi/examples/elementspecification</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715281 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00235: Configuring node text and icon

- page_id: `136715281`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715281/Configuring+node+text+and+icon
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:47:37.750+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You might need to change a text or icon for some Elements(nodes) in trees. For example, the adorn standard Element icon for showing that Element is annotated like the Validation framework does.

**Option #1 for changing just a text**

The text can be changed using a custom representation provider as described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating textual element representation' space='']. Using this approach, the text will be changed everywhere in UI, not just in trees.

**Option #2 for changing a node text or icon**

Use *com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.TreeNodeAdornmentManager.addIconAdornment(**IconAdornment)* to register a node icon customizer.

Use *TreeNodeAdornmentManager.addTextAdornment(TextAdornment[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/browser/TreeNodeAdornmentManager.html#addTextAdornment-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.TextAdornment-)* to register a node text customizer.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating textual element representation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You might need to change a text or icon for some Elements(nodes) in trees. For example, the adorn standard Element icon for showing that Element is annotated like the Validation framework does.</p><p><strong>Option #1 for changing just a text</strong></p><p>The text can be changed using a custom representation provider as described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating textual element representation" /></ac:link>. Using this approach, the text will be changed everywhere in UI, not just in trees.</p><p><strong>Option #2 for changing a node text or icon</strong></p><p>Use <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.TreeNodeAdornmentManager.addIconAdornment(</em><em>IconAdornment)</em> to register a node icon customizer.</p><p>Use <em>TreeNodeAdornmentManager.addTextAdornment(TextAdornment<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/ui/browser/TreeNodeAdornmentManager.html#addTextAdornment-com.nomagic.magicdraw.ui.browser.TextAdornment-">)</a></em> to register a node text customizer.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f80fb8f-62ab-4a95-b916-c5df66cb9b23"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating textual element representation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00236: Configuring report engine properties

- page_id: `136714342`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714342/Configuring+report+engine+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:25.075+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard > Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can configure the report properties of Report Wizard in the **Environment Options** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Configure report properties pane.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can select a report property in the **Configure report properties** pane, and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to see its description.

To edit, add, or delete a report property

1. Click **Options** > **Environment** to open the **Environment Options** dialog.
2. Select **Report Wizard** from the tree view to open the **Configure report properties** pane.
3. Click: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to edit a selected property name and value in the **Report Properties**dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a property name and value in the **Report Properties** dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to delete a selected property name and value from the **Configure report properties** pane.

The following table lists the fields of the **Report Properties**dialog:

| Field Name | Function | Default Value | Possible Value | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name | The property name | Blank | Text | Yes |
| Value | The property value | Blank | Text | No |
| Description | The property description |  | Text | No |

## PAGE 00238: Configuring Resource Usage Map URL

- page_id: `136713418`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713418/Configuring+Resource+Usage+Map+URL
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:30:40.527+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

1452332894

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

1452332905

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

1452332895

You can specify an URLto have quick access to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='TWCloud2024x'] right from your modeling tool. It is especially helpful when you need to inspect the project usages of a current Teamwork Cloud project.

To specify the Resource Usage Map URL

1. In the main menu, click Collaborate > Open in Resource Usage Map .
2. In the open dialog, specify the URL to access the Resource Usage Map. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] URL PatternThe URL you enter must follow this pattern:**http(s)://<domain_name>:<port>/webapp**Example[https://www.example.com:8443/webapp](https://www.example.com:8443/webapp)
3. Click **Open**.

The Resource Usage Map application opens in an external web browser. Once the configuration is complete, the specified URL will be automatically opened when you click **Collaborate** > **Open in Resource Usage Map**.

To remove the specified Resource Usage Map URL

1. In the main menu, click Options > Environment .
2. On the left side of the Environment Options dialog, select the Collaboration option group.
3. Under General , next to WebApp URL , click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then delete the existing URL. [ATTACHMENT filename='webapp_url_environment_options.png']
4. Click OK when you are done.

The URL is removed, meaning that it will no longer be automatically opened when you click **Collaborate** > **Open in Resource Usage Map**. You can now set up another URL or leave the value undefined.

1452332893

**Related pages**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='TWCloud2024x']**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="417826ec-3fd3-4682-bf42-8353b9d57238"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1452332894</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="300b099a-9313-4eb0-ad3d-cd77cc101ed4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1452332905</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21c2b8de-919b-469c-81ee-fa7caabd6db9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1452332895</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5a857d3f-03f0-4394-acdf-355f3bfcc0cf">specify an URL </ac:inline-comment-marker>to have quick access to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Usage Map" /></ac:link> right from your modeling tool. It is especially helpful when you need to inspect <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="96700331-3969-4872-af25-789520bd6047">the project usages</ac:inline-comment-marker> of a current Teamwork Cloud project.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fd6e8286-fb68-4da6-97da-313b6a2c9de9">To specify the Resource Usage Map URL</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Open in Resource Usage Map</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6903157b-dc92-4d98-8ac7-cf609da0043c">In the open dialog, specify the URL to access the Resource Usage Map.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="enter_web_application_url.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring Resource Usage Map URL" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf7676e8-7287-4316-90fc-3155d6f1a85f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">URL Pattern</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0727bd54-cfcb-4866-b2f3-736ae89d53e8">The URL you enter must follow this pattern:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><em><em>http(s)://&lt;domain_name&gt;:&lt;port&gt;/webapp</em><br /></em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03f0f932-c992-4cbd-8beb-9757f01bc384"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><a href="https://www.example.com:8443/webapp">https://www.example.com:8443/webapp</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Open</strong>. </p></li></ol><p>The Resource Usage Map application opens in an external web browser. Once the configuration is complete, the specified URL will be automatically opened when you click <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Open in Resource Usage Map</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove the specified Resource Usage Map URL</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. </li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Collaboration</strong> option group.</li><li>Under <strong>General</strong>, next to <strong>WebApp URL</strong>, click<strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring Resource Usage Map URL" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>and then <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="44a52bb8-d021-4ef4-80dc-77709c023c7b">delete the existing URL.</ac:inline-comment-marker> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webapp_url_environment_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring Resource Usage Map URL" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>The URL is removed, meaning that it will no longer be automatically opened when you click <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Open in Resource Usage Map</strong>. You can now set up another URL or leave the value undefined. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68b077b1-9aae-4a8c-afb6-2acf915d9c29"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1452332893</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="21b478b8-76a8-48fb-aec1-81a382ace77c"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Usage Map" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715252 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00239: Configuring shortcut menus of a row or column header element and a filter panel

- page_id: `136715252`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715252/Configuring+shortcut+menus+of+a+row+or+column+header+element+and+a+filter+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:16.415+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Working with Dependency Matrix > Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['dependency-matrix-action-registry', 'amconfigurator', 'for-row']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a custom command to the shortcut menu of the row or column header element, or to the one of the filter panel, use the [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixActionRegistry*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/configuration/DependencyMatrixActionRegistry.html) class.

The [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixAMConfigurator*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/configuration/DependencyMatrixAMConfigurator.html) interface has a single method that adds custom commands. The location of a new command depends on the parameters, such as the following:

- If the collection of element nodes is empty, the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the filter panel.
- If the collection of element nodes is not empty and forRow is true , the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the row header.
- If the collection of element nodes is not empty and forRow is false the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the column header element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To add a custom command to the shortcut menu of the row or column header element, or to the one of the filter panel, use the <a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/configuration/DependencyMatrixActionRegistry.html"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixActionRegistry</em></a> class.</p><p>The <a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/dependencymatrix/configuration/DependencyMatrixAMConfigurator.html"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixAMConfigurator</em></a> interface has a single method that adds custom commands. The location of a new command depends on the parameters, such as the following:</p><ul><li>If the collection of element nodes is empty, the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the filter panel.</li><li>If the collection of element nodes is not empty and <em>forRow</em> is <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">true</em>, the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the row header.</li><li>If the collection of element nodes is not empty and <em>forRow</em> is <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">false </em>the command will be added to the shortcut menu of the column header element.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714356 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00240: Configuring template mappings

- page_id: `136714356`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714356/Configuring+template+mappings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:25.828+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard > Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the **Environment Options** dialog to configure the template mappings of Report Wizard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Configure template mappings pane.

To edit, create, or delete a template mapping

1. Click **Options** > **Environment**. The **Environment Options** dialog will open.
2. Select **Report Wizard** from the tree view. The **Configure template mappings** pane will open.
3. Click: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to edit a selected filename extension and template type in the **T****emplate mappings**dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a filename extension and template type in the **T****emplate mappings** dialog, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to delete a selected filename extension and template type from the **Configure template mappings**pane.

The following table lists the fields of the **T****emplate mappings** dialog:

| Field Name | Function | Default Value | Possible Value | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| File extension | The filename extension (not case-sensitive). | Blank | Text | Yes |
| Template type | The template type. You can select any of the following: html, odp, docbook, dbk, odt, ods, docx. | Blank | Text | Yes |

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Deleting a template mapping from the**Environment Options**dialog will cause the file extension mapping to be removed from MagicDraw. To restore the default setting, restart MagicDraw.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
