The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Report Wizard</strong> from the tree view. The <strong>Configure template mappings</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Click:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="EditProperties_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to edit a selected filename extension and template type in the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings </strong>dialog, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="AddProperties_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create a filename extension and template type in the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings</strong> dialog, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="RemoveProperties_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Configuring template mappings" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to delete a selected filename extension and template type from the <strong>Configure template mappings </strong>pane.</p><p> </p></li></ol><p>The following table lists the fields of the <strong>T</strong><strong>emplate mappings</strong> dialog:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Field Name </th><th>Function </th><th>Default Value </th><th>Possible Value </th><th>Required</th></tr><tr><td>File extension</td><td>The filename extension (not case-sensitive).</td><td>Blank</td><td>Text </td><td>Yes </td></tr><tr><td>Template type </td><td><p>The template type. You can select any of the following: html, odp, docbook, dbk, odt, ods, docx.</p></td><td>Blank</td><td>Text  </td><td>Yes</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0af6f61f-2dff-4a2d-9654-2a5d417c0307"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Deleting a template mapping from the </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> dialog will cause the file extension mapping to be removed from MagicDraw. To restore the default setting, restart MagicDraw.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715251 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00241: Configuring the command bar and shortcut keys

- page_id: `136715251`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715251/Configuring+the+command+bar+and+shortcut+keys
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:22:23.016+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Working with Dependency Matrix > Customizing a dependency matrix
- labels: ['amconfigurator', 'actions-configurator-manager', 'dependency-matrix-diagram-descriptor', 'dependency-matrix-configurator']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add custom commands to the command bar of a dependency matrix as well as assign custom shortcut keys for the commands.

To add a new command to the command bar, add an instance of the *com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator* interface to the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager* class, as is shown in the following example:

```java

```

When customizing the default dependency matrix type, provide the name defined in the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator.DEFAULT_DEPENDENCY_MATRIX_DIAGRAM_TYPE* constant*.*And when customizing a custom dependency matrix type, provide the name defined in the instance of the *DependencyMatrixConfigurator* class as well as in the **Customize Dependency Matrix Wizard**dialog.

The same code example can be used for the shortcut key configuration:

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>You can add custom commands to the command bar of a dependency matrix as well as assign custom shortcut keys for the commands.</p><p>To add a new command to the command bar, add an instance of the <em>com.nomagic.actions.AMConfigurator</em> interface to the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.actions.ActionsConfiguratorsManager</em> class, as is shown in the following example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0b586e6-5698-4f89-bd97-44da0e21109f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().addDiagramCommandBarConfigurator(
			DependencyMatrixConfigurator.DEFAULT_DEPENDENCY_MATRIX_DIAGRAM_TYPE, new AMConfigurator()
    {
        @Override
        public void configure(ActionsManager mngr)
        {
            mngr.addCategory(new ActionsCategory("ID", "My Category"));
        }
 
        @Override
        public int getPriority()
        {
             return AMConfigurator.HIGH_PRIORITY;
        }
    });]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>When customizing the default dependency matrix type, provide the name defined in the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.dependencymatrix.configuration.DependencyMatrixConfigurator.DEFAULT_DEPENDENCY_MATRIX_DIAGRAM_TYPE</em> constant<em>. </em>And when customizing a custom dependency matrix type, provide the name defined in the instance of the <em>DependencyMatrixConfigurator</em> class as well as in the <strong>Customize Dependency Matrix Wizard </strong>dialog.</p><p>The same code example can be used for the shortcut key configuration:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65e7f44d-19b8-4e4d-90f4-c1a5551bdd10"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    ActionsConfiguratorsManager.getInstance().addDiagramShortcutsConfigurator(...)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713528 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00242: Configuring used projects in 3DEXPERIENCE

- page_id: `136713528`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713528/Configuring+used+projects+in+3DEXPERIENCE
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-12-12T14:22:51.814+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all used projects and their versions in the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog.

To open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog

1. Login to the 3D EXPERIENCE platform.
2. Open your server project.
3. On the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages . The Project Usages dialog opens.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The ****Advanced Project Usages Configuration**** dialog opens. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Search barYou can use the search bar for faster filtering of used projects by name.

In this dialog you can make various modifications for one or more projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and use the commands in the toolbar or shortcut menu to:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using a 3DEXPERIENCE server project in your project' space=''] to the server project you are working with
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stop using 3DEXPERIENCE project in your project' space=''] from the server project you are working with.
- Start using an indirectly used project directly.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing used project version in 3DEXPERIENCE' space=''] of a selected used project.
- Review the information of a selected used project.
- Reset changes to a selected used project. NOTE: You must do this before applying the selected actions!

In the **Action**column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the **Apply** button is clicked.

To turn the action on, click one of the following:

- Apply - to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note that the applied changes cannot be undone.
- OK - to apply changes and close the dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can see all used projects and their versions in the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Login to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE<strong> </strong>platform.</li><li>Open your server project.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Collaborate </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>. The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The <strong><strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong></strong> dialog opens.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="advanced_usages_configuration.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2f9b9a6-313b-47ff-b4ef-12b29d090646"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Search bar</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can use the search bar for faster filtering of used projects by name.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>In this dialog you can make various modifications for one or more projects at a time. Select one or more used projects and u<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ea8e0aae-4b61-415d-bd5c-322e1a1fa7e6">se the commands in the toolbar</ac:inline-comment-marker> or shortcut menu to:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using a 3DEXPERIENCE server project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add a new project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the server project you are working with</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stop using 3DEXPERIENCE project in your project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove a used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the server project you are working with.</li><li>Start using an indirectly used project directly.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing used project version in 3DEXPERIENCE" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change the version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a selected used project.</li><li>Review the information of a selected used project.</li><li>Reset changes to a selected used project. NOTE: You must do this before applying the selected actions!</li></ul><p>In the <strong>Action </strong>column, you can see the action to be performed on a particular project after the <strong>Apply</strong> button is clicked.</p><p>To turn the action on, click one of the following:</p><ul><li><strong>Apply </strong>- to apply changes and keep the dialog open in order to see what changes are applied. Please note that the applied changes cannot be undone.</li><li><strong>OK </strong>- to apply changes and close the dialog.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711342 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00243: Connection Point Reference

- page_id: `136711342`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711342/Connection+Point+Reference
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:34.181+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['connection-point-reference']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Connection Point Reference represents an entry to or an exit from the submachine State. It can be used as the source or target of a transition.

The following procedures show you how to work with Connection Point Reference:

##### Drawing the Connection Point Reference

To draw the Connection Point Reference on the submachine State

1. On the State diagram palette, click the **Connection Point Reference** button.
2. Click the shape of the submachine State. The **Select Entry/Exit Point** dialog opens.
3. Select *entry point* to define the entry into the submachine State, or *exit point* to define the exit from the submachine State. The Connection Point Reference is drawn on the submachine State with a defined entry or exit point.

##### Checking the assigned entry/exit point to Connection Point Reference

To see the assigned entry/exit point

- Open the **Connection Point Reference** dialog. The **Entry** or **Exit** properties display the defined entries.

##### Assigning the entry/exit points to the Connection Point Reference

To assign the entry/exit points to the Connection Point Reference

- Right-click the Connection Point Reference shape, and, on the shortcut menu ,click **Select****Entry/Exit Point**.

In the **Select** **Entry/Exit** **Point** dialog, only the entry and exit points that are created at the same State Machine as the submachine State are listed. If there are no entry/exit points at the same State machine, the **Select Entry/Exit Point** dialog is not opened when drawing the Connection Point Reference.

##### Displaying the Connection Point References

To display the Connection Point References on the submachine State shape

1. Select the submachine State shape of the Connection Point References you want to display.
2. Open the **Select****Points**dialog by performing one of the following steps: **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** ****
  - From the shortcut menu, select **Display** > **Display Points**.
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select **Display Points**.
3. In the dialog, select the Connection Point References that you want to display, and click **OK**. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_points_dialog.png'] Selected Connection Point References are displayed on the submachine State shape. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Connection Point Reference represents an entry to or an exit from the submachine State. It can be used as the source or target of a transition. </span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The following procedures show you how to work with <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connection Point Reference</span>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3229193-db21-4314-9e91-fe1c8834856b" /></p><h3 style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drawing the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connection Point Reference</span><br /></span></h3><p style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To draw the Connection Point Reference on the submachine State</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">On the State diagram palette, click the <strong>Connection Point Reference</strong> button.  </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click the shape of the submachine State. The <strong>Select Entry/Exit Point</strong> dialog opens.  </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select <em>entry point</em> to define the entry into the submachine State, or <em>exit point</em> to define the exit from the submachine State. The Connection Point Reference is drawn on the submachine State with a defined entry or exit point.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></li></ol><h3 style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Checking the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">assigned entry/exit point</span> to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connection Point Reference</span><br /></span></h3><p style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To see the assigned entry/exit point</span></p><hr /><ul><li style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Open the <strong>Connection Point Reference</strong> dialog. The <strong>Entry</strong> or <strong>Exit</strong> properties display the defined entries. </span></li></ul><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Assigning the entry/exit points to the Connection Point Reference</span></h3><p style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To assign the entry/exit points to the Connection Point Reference</span></p><hr /><ul><li style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click the Connection Point Reference shape, and, on the shortcut menu ,click <strong>Select</strong><strong> Entry/Exit Point</strong>. </span></li></ul><p style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9595a6e2-98a6-440d-9250-f30dd1fc2ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong>Select</strong> <strong>Entry/Exit</strong> <strong>Point</strong> dialog, only the entry and exit points that are created at the same State Machine as the submachine State are listed. If there are no entry/exit points at the same State machine, the <strong>Select Entry/Exit Point</strong> dialog is not opened when drawing the Connection Point Reference.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Displaying the Connection Point References</span></h3><p>To display the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connection Point Reference</span>s on the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">submachine State shape<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">submachine State shape of the</span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connection Point Reference</span>s you want to display.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>Open the <strong>Select </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Points </strong></span></span></span>dialog by performing one of the following steps:</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Points</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">On the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Connection Point Reference" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and select <strong>Display Points</strong>.</span></span></span></li></ul><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:title="The Display Points command from the shortcut menu of the Handle Failure submachine State." ac:alt="The Display Points command from the shortcut menu of the Handle Failure submachine State."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_points_command_from_shortcut_menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Connection Point Reference" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong><br /><br /></strong></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the dialog, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connection Point Reference</span>s that you want to display, and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /></span><ac:image ac:title="The Select Points dialog of the Handle Failure submachine State." ac:alt="The Select Points dialog of the Handle Failure submachine State."><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_points_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Connection Point Reference" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Selected <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connection Point Reference</span>s are displayed on the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">submachine State shape</span>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="displayed_points_on_submachine_State_shape.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Connection Point Reference" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710960 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00244: Connector

- page_id: `136710960`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710960/Connector
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:08.852+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1896492679

1896492681

1896492680

A Connector specifies a link that enables communication between two or more instances. This link can be an instance of an association, or it can represent the possibility of the instances being able to communicate because their identities are known by virtue of being passed on as parameters, held in variables or slots, or because the communicating instances are the same instance.

The link can be realized by something as simple as a pointer or by something as complex as a network connection. In contrast to the associations, which specify the links between any instance of the associated classifiers, the connectors specify the links between instances playing the connected parts only.

Each connector can be attached to two or more connectable elements, each representing a set of instances.

You can specify connector and connector end properties in the connector and connector end Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

##### Drawing a connector end

A Connector has at least two ends, each representing the participation of instances of the classifiers typing the connectable elements attached to the end. The set of Connector ends is ordered. The connector end information can be displayed on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting a Port automatically when drawing a Connector

To select a Port automatically when drawing a Connector from a Port to a Part

1. Select a Port on the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Part shape. The **Select Port** menu appears. The menu appears if a new Port can be created on the Part or the Part has hidden Ports.
3. Select one of the following command:

- None - if you want to connect connector straight to the part.
- On a port in the list. Hidden ports of the part are listed. The selected port will be represented on the diagram and the connector will be connected to it.
- New Port - to create a new port with the same name, type and multiplicity as the port from which the connector is drawn.
- New Nested Port - to create a new nested port. Note that the command exists only if the connector is drawn from the nested port.

You can draw a connector to a Port too and the menu appears. As a result, the nested Port is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Hiding Connector type

You can hide the type name of the represented Connector on the diagram pane.

To hide the type name of Connector

1. Select a single or any number of Connectors on the diagram pane.
2. Right-click to open the shortcut menu.
3. From the shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties.
4. In the Symbol Properties dialog, set the Show Type option value to false. The name of the Connector type is hidden and no longer visible in the Connector label.

1896492678

**On this page**

- [Drawing a connector end](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Connector#Connector-Drawingaconnectorend)
- [Selecting a Port automatically when drawing a Connector](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Connector#Connector-SelectingaPortautomaticallywhendrawingaConnector)
- [Hiding Connector type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Connector#Connector-HidingConnectortype)

**Related pages**

- Editing property values
- [Setting an Information Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Information+Flow)
- Reversing Information Flow direction
- Model Elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710964 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00245: Constraint

- page_id: `136710964`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710964/Constraint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:09.101+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Constraint represents additional semantic information attached to the constrained elements. It is an assertion that indicates a restriction that must be satisfied by a correct design of the system. The constrained elements are those elements required to evaluate the constraint specification. In addition, the context of the Constraint can be accessed, and can be used as the namespace for interpreting names used in the specification.

A Constraint is represented as a linguistic, enclosed in braces ({}), statement in some formal (OCL, C++, and other), or a natural language. There are 14 standard constraints in UML such as association, global, and parameter. You can also define your own constraints.

You can create the following constraints:

- Time Constraint. It specifies the combination of min and max timing interval values.
- Duration Constraint. It defines a value specification that indicates the temporal distance between two time instants.
- Constraint. It represents an additional semantic information attached to the constrained elements.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Link' space='MD2024x'] . It is a graphical representation of a constraint element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Constraints for validation

Constraints for validation

Constraints can also be used to define [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024x'] for your model.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The constrained elements are those elements required to evaluate the constraint specification. In addition, the context of the Constraint can be accessed, and can be used as the namespace for interpreting names used in the specification.</p><p>A Constraint is represented as a linguistic, enclosed in braces ({}), statement in some formal (OCL, C++, and other), or a natural language. There are 14 standard constraints in UML such as association, global, and parameter. You can also define your own constraints.</p><p>You can create the following constraints:</p><ul><li>Time Constraint. It specifies the combination of min and max timing interval values.</li><li>Duration Constraint. It defines a value specification that indicates the temporal distance between two time instants.</li><li>Constraint. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710967 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00246: Constraint Link

- page_id: `136710967`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710967/Constraint+Link
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:09.369+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Constraint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Constraint Link is a graphical representation of a constraint element. The Constraint Link is shown on the diagram pane as a dashed arrow from one shape to another. You can assign a particular constraint to the Constraint Link. The constraint assigned to the Constraint Link is labeled in braces: {} . See an example of the constraint representation in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

On the Constraint Link, you can display the constraint direction arrow. The direction of the arrow represents a relevant information within the constraint. The client (the tail of the arrow) is mapped to the first position and the supplier (arrowhead) is mapped to the second position in the constraint.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']the constraint symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x'].

After applying the constraint to the Constraint Link, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MD2024x']the constraint in the Constraint specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of all constraint properties. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

To draw a Constraint Link

1. In the Class, Use Case, Sequence, or Activity [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024x'] , under the Common category, expand the Constraint command and select the Constraint Link.
2. Draw a Constraint Link between desired shapes.

To add a constraint expression to the Constraint Link

- From the Constraint Link shortcut menu, choose the Select Constraint and do one of the following:
  - Select Create Constraint to create a new constraint.
  - Choose a constraint from the list.

To display a Constraint Link direction arrow

1. Select the Constraint Link and from the shortcut menu, select **Show Arrow**. 
The direction of the Constraint Link will be displayed. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Constraint Link is a graphical representation of a constraint element. The Constraint Link is shown on the diagram pane as a dashed arrow from one shape to another. You can assign a particular constraint to the Constraint Link. The constraint assigned to the Constraint Link is labeled in braces: {} . See an example of the constraint representation in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Constraint Link between relationships" ac:alt="Constraint Link between relationships"><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraint_link.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint Link" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>On the Constraint Link, you can display the constraint direction arrow. The direction of the arrow represents a relevant information within the constraint. The client (the tail of the arrow) is mapped to the first position and the supplier (arrowhead) is mapped to the second position in the constraint.</p><p>You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[format ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the constraint symbol properties in the  <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link>.</p><p>After applying the constraint to the Constraint Link, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the constraint in the Constraint specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of all constraint properties. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p><p> </p><p>To draw a Constraint Link</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Class, Use Case, Sequence, or Activity <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, under the Common category, expand the <strong>Constraint</strong> command and select the Constraint Link.</li><li>Draw a Constraint Link between desired shapes.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a constraint expression to the Constraint Link</p><hr /><ul><li>From the Constraint Link shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select Constraint</strong> and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select <strong>Create Constraint</strong> to create a new constraint. </li><li>Choose a constraint from the list.</li></ul></li></ul><p> </p><p>To display a Constraint Link direction arrow</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select the Constraint Link and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Arrow</strong>. <br />The direction of the Constraint Link will be displayed. See an example in the following figure.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of constraint path with direction arrow" ac:alt="Example of constraint path with direction arrow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraint_path_arrow.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint Link" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying duration constraint on the Action]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

This example shows the Class *myClass* that has Operation *myClass(*), which is the constructor, and Operation ~myClass(), which is the destructor.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### ExampleConstructor and Destructor in the Class diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Constructor and the Destructor operations.

To create a Constructor

1. Create a Class with an Operation.
2. Go to the Class Specification window.
3. Select Operation
4. Apply the stereotype «C++Constructor » for Operation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Destructor

1. Create a Class with an Operation.
2. Go to the Class Specification window.
3. Select Operation
4. Apply the stereotype «C++Destructor » for Operation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

## PAGE 00248: Containment

- page_id: `136710971`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710971/Containment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:09.579+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943409377

943409379

943409378

The Containment relationship represents the containment of elements on the diagram pane. A Containment path replaces a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Nesting Requirements' space='SYSMLP185']. For example, instead of drawing one package nested into another, you can draw a containment relationship from one package to another (see the figure below).This example shows that the Containment relationship is a graphical representation as well as a nesting of elements.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of package nesting (on the left) and package nesting using containment relationship (on the right)

In the example above, the Containment end with a circle plus symbol shows a containing element. The *User* package is a contained element and the*Domain*package is a containing element: the owner of the*User*package is the*Domain*package.

You can format containment symbol properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

943409376

- Customizing Environment Options

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709218 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00249: Containment tab

- page_id: `136709218`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709218/Containment+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:38.434+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

469061243

469061246

469098799

**On this page**

469061245

INLINE

The Containment tab displays model data grouped in logical sets. Using the Containment tab, you can quickly access each model element, change the model element's specification, or create a new model element.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 To open the Containment tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Containment tab.
- If the Containment tab is hidden, from the **Window** menu, select **Containment**. You can change some Containment tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024x']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Containment tab consists of the following two components:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Containment tree includes

- The root package *Model*[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x']:

The Relations branch 
The UML Standard Profile 
The Hyperlinks branch

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x']

##### #datamodelThe root package *Model*

The root package*Model* in the Containment tree is the top model element wherein the entire model structure is created. The root package***Model* represents the model element that is named *Model*. All model elements are stored in the root package***Model*. You can create the structure of your project by creating packages in the root package *Model*. This helps to distribute model elements into logical groups.

Along with model elements that are created by the user, the root package *Model* may contain the following default items:

- The Relations branch. This branch appears when at least one relationship is created in the project. The Relations branch collects all relationships from the particular owner. For example, if the Relations branch is in the root package Model , that means, all relationships from the root package Model are listed in the Relations branch owned by the root package Model . The Relations branch can exist in other elements which may have inner elements and relations among these elements. For example, in packages, classes, and others.
- The **UML Standard Profile** package. This is a profile that is included in every MagicDraw project. It contains a list of stereotypes, data types, and other elements from the UML 2 metamodel. By default, the *UML Standard Profile* is hidden. To display the *UML Standard Profile*, click the Options button, and in the shortcut menu, choose**Show****Auxiliary****Resources**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
There can be more standard profiles (such as SysML, UPDM, Java, and others). We highly recommend not to modify our provided standard profiles and libraries as it could cause problems with version updates, plugins, core MagicDraw tool malfunctions, and model corruptions.
- The File View package. This package appears when at least one code engineering set is created. The File View package contains components that are created on code reverse and represents source files.
- The Hyperlinks branch. This branch appears when at least one hyperlink is added to a particular element. The Hyperlinks branch contains the list of hyperlinks to the file, element/ symbol, or web page.

##### #codeengineeringsetsCode engineering sets

Code engineering is available in Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

Code engineering sets can be considered a gateway between your source code and model data. Using these sets, you can perform Java, C++, IDL, CORBA IDL, and EJB 2.0 round-trip code engineering, that is, code generation and reverse engineering. 
 
 To display Code engineering sets

- In the Containment tab toolbar, click the Options [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button, then click to select the Show Code Engineering Sets check box.

In the Code engineering Set branch, you can create a new code engineering set, generate code, and perform other actions. 
****

##### Containment tab toolbar

#containmenttoolbar

The Containment tab toolbar contains the following buttons:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the currently expanded elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Open in New Tree | Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.You can choose to open elements in:New Containment TreeNew Structure TreeNew Inheritance Tree |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favorites Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Filter - opens the Select Element Type dialog wherein you can select element types to be visible in the Containment tree. Learn more on the page.Show Auxiliary Resources - shows or hides modules and profiles that are used in your project.Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.Show Full Types - shows or hides the full types of typed elements (for example, operations, attributes, relationships, and other) in the Containment tree.Show Code Engineering Sets - shows or hides the code engineering sets. |

The same actions you may perform using the shortcut menu of the Containment tree.

##### Working with model elements in the Containment tree

#containmenttree

You can work with elements in the Containment tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu.

Particular actions are available for particular elements.

The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Creates a new element inside the selected element.This command is available if the selected element can be edited. |
|  | Creates a new diagram inside the selected element.This command is available if the selected element can be edited. |
| Create Relation > Outgoing / Incoming | Creates a new relation to or from the selected element. |
|  | Opens the element Specification window wherein you can specify various element properties. |
|  | Open the validation commands for checking the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model. |
|  | Opens or selects the item associated with the selected element. The item may be a diagram, model element, or hyperlink. |
|  | Converts the selected element to the element indicated by a user. |
|  | Opens a list of functions available for the selected element. |
| Tools | Opens a list of tools that are available for the selected element. Depends on the selected element. |
|  | Opens the drop-down list, wherein you can apply an existing stereotype, or create a new one. |
|  | Opens the Find dialog. |
| Apply Profiles | Opens the list of the available to apply profiles.This command is available in the package, model, and system boundary shortcut menu.Only custom profiles can be applied.To see this command you have to expand the shortcut menu, that is, click on the little arrow at the bottom of the shortcut menu. |
|  | Opens share packages and export packages to new project commands.This command is available in the package and model shortcut menu. |
| Generate Code Framework | Generates code for the selected item. Opens the Notification Window with the information. |
| Check Syntax | Checks syntax in the model according to the default code engineering language. Opens the Notification Window with the information. |
|  | Opens the list of the available report templates. |
| Reverse from Classpath | This command is available in the Class shortcut menu. |

469061241

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x']

<strong>Containment</strong> tab.</li><li><p>If the Containment tab is hidden, from the <strong>Window</strong> menu, select <strong>Containment</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4fbcff2-82da-43fc-a287-2d84df172d7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="_mce_tagged_br">You can change some Containment tab properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> in the <strong>Browser</strong> options group.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p>The Containment tab consists of the following two components:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Containment tab toolbar"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Working with model elements in the Containment tree"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of Containment Tree" ac:alt="Example of Containment Tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="containment-tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>The Containment tree includes <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="datamodel"><ac:link-body>The root package <em>Model</em></ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link>:</span></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Relations branch </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The UML Standard Profile </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Hyperlinks branch</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="codeengineeringsets"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Code engineering sets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed27d0b4-2ff3-43ce-9c24-c3eaf3f1ae2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#datamodel</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The root package <em>Model</em>  </h3><p>The root package<em> Model</em> in the Containment tree is the top model element wherein the entire model structure is created. The root package<em><em> </em>Model</em> represents the model element that is named <em>Model</em>. All model elements are stored in the root package<em> </em><em>Model</em>. You can create the structure of your project by creating packages in the root package <em>Model</em>. This helps to distribute model elements into logical groups.</p><p>Along with model elements that are created by the user, the root package <em>Model</em> may contain the following default items:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Relations</strong> branch. This branch appears when at least one relationship is created in the project. The <em>Relations</em> branch collects all relationships from the particular owner. For example, if the <em>Relations</em> branch is in the root package <em>Model</em>, that means, all relationships from the root package <em>Model</em> are listed in the <em>Relations</em> branch owned by the root package <em>Model</em>. The <em>Relations</em> branch can exist in other elements which may have inner elements and relations among these elements. For example, in packages, classes, and others.</li><li><p>The <strong>UML Standard Profile</strong> package. This is a profile that is included in every MagicDraw project. It contains a list of stereotypes, data types, and other elements from the UML 2 metamodel. By default, the <em>UML Standard Profile</em> is hidden. To display the <em>UML Standard Profile</em>, click the <span>Options button, and in the shortcut menu, choose </span><strong>Show </strong><strong>Auxiliary</strong><strong> Resources</strong><span>.</span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_grouping.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. <br />There can be more standard profiles (such as SysML, UPDM, Java, and others).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="030d0e6e-9c79-493e-a204-a0922e9bd97f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We highly recommend not to modify our provided standard profiles and libraries as it could cause problems with version updates, plugins, core MagicDraw tool malfunctions, and model corruptions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>The <strong>File View</strong> package. This package appears when at least one code engineering set is created. The <em>File View</em> package contains components that are created on code reverse and represents source files.  </li><li><span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span>The <strong>Hyperlinks</strong> branch. This branch appears when at least one hyperlink is added to a particular element. The Hyperlinks branch contains the list of hyperlinks to the file, element/ symbol, or web page. </li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c775a7ce-dbfa-4301-8f19-30ddc23f6b71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#codeengineeringsets</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Code engineering sets<br /><br /></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9c1422f-2e78-418a-bfb1-24be66986ceb"><ac:rich-text-body>Code engineering is available in Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />Code engineering sets can be considered a gateway between your source code and model data. Using these sets, you can perform Java, C++, IDL, CORBA IDL, and EJB 2.0 round-trip code engineering, that is, code generation and reverse engineering. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To display Code engineering sets</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span> In the Containment tab toolbar, click the <strong>Options <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="29"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>button, then click to select the<strong> Show Code Engineering Sets</strong> check box.</li></ul><p>In the Code engineering Set branch, you can create a new code engineering set, generate code, and perform other actions.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong> </strong></p><h3>Containment tab toolbar</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6f9fa5c-f261-4396-afd8-48eadb346e30"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#containmenttoolbar</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> The Containment tab toolbar contains the following buttons:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Button</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Tooltip text</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Collapse_all.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Collapse All</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>C<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ollapses all of the currently expanded elements in the Containment tree.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Collapse_selected_recursively.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Collapse Selected Recursively</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="18" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_new_tab_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Open in New Tree</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.</p><p>You can choose to open elements in:</p><ul><li>New Containment Tree</li><li>New Structure Tree</li><li>New Inheritance Tree</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_in_new_tree_containment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="17" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorite_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Favorites</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Add a selected element to favorites</li><li>Open the dialog for managing favorites<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li>Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="19" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Quick Find</p></td><td><p>Opens the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Options</p></td><td><p>Click to open the Options menu and then select:</p><ul><li><strong>Filter</strong> - opens the <strong>Select Element Type</strong> dialog wherein you can select element types to be visible in the Containment tree. Learn more on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><strong>Show</strong> <strong>Auxiliary</strong> <strong>Resources</strong> - shows or hides modules and profiles that are used in your project.</li><li><strong>Show</strong> <strong>Applied Stereotypes</strong> - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.</li><li><strong>Show Full Types</strong> - shows or hides the full types of typed elements (for example, operations, attributes, relationships, and other) in the Containment tree.</li><li><strong>Show Code Engineering Sets</strong> - shows or hides the code engineering sets.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> The same actions you may perform using the shortcut menu of the Containment tree.</p><h3>Working with model elements in the Containment tree</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46f48b83-812d-4be3-b05c-4df98ca374bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#containmenttree</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can work with elements in the Containment tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6964714-4978-4e31-bd38-a2f91d266d08"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Particular actions are available for particular elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>The shortcut menu contains the following commands:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Working with model elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create Element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper">Creates a new element inside the selected element.<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2dfa794-8bb3-49eb-a669-a485c39ef534"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command is available if the selected element can be edited.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Creates a new diagram inside the selected element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aff42e4f-4a72-4c79-a0f6-c2f8fc99f4ef"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command is available if the selected element can be edited.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a relationship" /><ac:link-body><strong>Create Relation</strong> &gt; <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Outgoing / Incoming</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link></p></td><td><p>Creates a new relation to or from the selected element.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the element Specification window wherein you can specify various element properties.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link></strong></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="675a2501-f9cb-4a74-9a75-313a02dd432a">Open the validation co</ac:inline-comment-marker>mmands for checking the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Manage Navigation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Go To]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p></td><td><p>Opens or selects the item associated with the selected element. The item may be a diagram, model element, or hyperlink.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Changing diagram type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Refactor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p></td><td><p>Converts the selected element to the element indicated by a user.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p></td><td><p>Opens a list of functions available for the selected element.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tools</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens a list of tools that are available for the selected element. Depends on the selected element.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the drop-down list, wherein you can apply an existing stereotype, or create a new one.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Find dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Find]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the <strong>Find</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e56a4f74-6e25-4584-a627-82cb7c158ee4"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1b0ba43e-01e9-40cc-9b70-3c30e7ef80f6">Apply Profiles</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the list of the available to apply profiles.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de3e96ff-ae72-43b7-aa03-b5184f9abcf9"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>This command is available in the package, model, and system boundary shortcut menu.</li><li>Only custom profiles can be applied.</li><li>To see this command you have to expand the shortcut menu, that is, click on the little arrow at the bottom of the shortcut menu.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens share packages and export packages to new project commands.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67107529-18ff-4924-a1d2-5cee106d7d0c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command is available in the package and model shortcut menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Generate Code</strong> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Framework</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Generates code for the selected item. Opens the Notification Window with the information.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Check Syntax</strong></td><td colspan="1">Checks syntax in the model according to the default code engineering language. Opens the Notification Window with the information.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generating reports from the Containment tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generate Report]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></td><td colspan="1">Opens the list of the available report templates.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Reverse from Classpath</strong></td><td colspan="1">This command is available in the Class shortcut menu.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7be94ff-fb5d-43f1-871c-d060106d2ea9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">469061241</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710974 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00250: Containment Tree

- page_id: `136710974`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710974/Containment+Tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:09.789+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Containment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943711221

943711224

943711223

You can create the Containment tree when drawing more than one Containment relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Example of several containment relationships (on the left) and example of containment tree (on the right)

To group containment paths into a tree

1. Draw a Containment path from each contained element to the containing element.
2. Now, do one of the following:

- Through the diagram pane.
  1. Select the Containment relationship.
  2. Click and hold the little square (the end of the Containment path) next to the Containment end with the circle plus symbol.
  3. Drag and drop the selected Containment end on the other Containment path.
  4. While dragging, the Containment path turns blue, indicating an allowable connection. Containment paths merge together. [ATTACHMENT filename='DnD_containment_path.png']

- Through the shortcut menu.
  1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH185'] of the containing element, in the example above: Domain .
  2. Click Refactor > Make Sub Tree .

To ungroup a containment tree

1. Right-click the Containment tree's end with the circle plus symbol pointing to a containing element.
2. In the opened shortcut menu, click the Ungroup Tree command. [ATTACHMENT filename='ungroup_containment_tree.png']

To separate the containment path from the containment tree

- Select the containment path you want to remove from the tree. Open its shortcut menu and click the **Remove From Tree** command. The contained element is added to the containing element separately. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_containment_from_tree.png']
- Drag one Containment path to another element:
  1. On the diagram pane, select the vertical part of the particular Containment path.
  2. Click and hold the upper little square.
  3. Drag and drop end of the Containment path to the other containing element.
  4. The Containment path is removed from the Containment tree and the owner of the containing element has been changed.

943711219

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="581e1113-efd9-4aa7-8590-b53885fa85e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943711221</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8329dda1-534d-4121-afee-b67bf62728d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943711224</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0a2e1dc-36bc-4e9d-8628-e0267255fe3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943711223</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create the Containment tree when drawing more than one Containment relationship.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;font-weight: bold;">Example of several containment relationships (on the left) and example of containment tree (on the right) </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To group containment paths into a tree</p><hr /><ol><li>Draw a Containment path from each contained element to the containing element.</li><li>Now, do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li>Through the diagram pane.<ol><li>Select the Containment relationship. </li><li>Click and hold the little square (the end of the Containment path) next to the Containment end with the circle plus symbol. </li><li>Drag and drop the selected Containment end on the other Containment path. </li><li>While dragging, the Containment path turns blue, indicating an allowable connection. Containment paths merge together.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="DnD_containment_path.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li></ul><ul><li><span>Through the shortcut menu.</span><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH185" ri:content-title="Shortcut menu" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shortcut menu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the containing element, in the example above: <em>Domain</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt; <strong>Make Sub Tree</strong>.</li></ol></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To ungroup a containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Containment tree's end with the circle plus symbol pointing to a containing element.</li><li>In the opened shortcut menu, click the <strong>Ungroup Tree</strong> command.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ungroup_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To separate the containment path from the containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Select the containment path you want to remove from the tree. Open its shortcut menu and click the <strong>Remove From Tree</strong> command. The contained element is added to the containing element separately.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_containment_from_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Drag one Containment path to another element: <ol><li>On the diagram pane, select the vertical part of the particular Containment path. </li><li>Click and hold the upper little square. </li><li>Drag and drop end of the Containment path to the other containing element. </li><li>The Containment path is removed from the Containment tree and the owner of the containing element has been changed. </li></ol></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14a3464e-4ec1-432b-b684-38dae1c43080"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943711219</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Generating reports from the Containment tree" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Customizing Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00251: Content diagram

- page_id: `136710071`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710071/Content+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:23.427+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Extension diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

23

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

24

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

26

INLINE

#### NOTE: Functionality Availability

Functionality Availability

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Content diagram is an extension of UML notation. The purpose of the Content diagram is to generate or represent a project structure (diagrams) and the relations between them. The Content table works as a table of contents for a project. The Content Shape creates a table of contents of all diagrams of the project.

All Content diagrams have their own specifications. You can specify their name, documentation, and view the relationships in which they participate. You can also add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing tagged value' space='MD2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint' space='MD2024x']. For more information about diagram creation, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MD2024x'].

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Content diagram palette

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example a Content diagram for the Free Form diagram

27

INLINE

**Related References:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML diagrams' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extension diagrams' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712960 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00252: Content Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `136712960`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712960/Content+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:11.386+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

638104837

638104840

638104838

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The **Content Diagram Wizard** generates the content of diagrams used in the project.

To create a content diagram using the **Content Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Content Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Content Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Content Diagram Wizard and click **Start** .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

638104835

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content diagram' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710982 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00253: Control Flow

- page_id: `136710982`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710982/Control+Flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:10.175+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Control flow is an edge that starts an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MD2024x'] node after the previous one is finished.

You can specify a Control flow by changing its property values in the Control flow[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

You can format the Control flow symbol properties in the[Symbol Properties dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Formatting+symbols).

A Control flow can have guards (constraints that provide fine-grained control over the firing of the Control Flow). To specify guards, choose the Guard box in the Control Flow dialog box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specification of the Control Flow, Guard property

Set the Control Flow symbol properties in the Symbol Properties dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Control flow is an edge that starts an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link> node after the previous one is finished.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can specify a Control flow by changing its property values in the Control flow </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can format the Control flow symbol properties in the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Formatting+symbols">Symbol Properties dialog</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span><br /></span></p><p>A Control flow can have guards (constraints that provide fine-grained control over the firing of the Control Flow). To specify guards, choose the Guard box in the <span class="hcp1">Control</span> <span class="hcp1">Flow</span> dialog box.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Control_flow_Guard.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Control Flow" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Specification of the Control Flow, Guard property</h6><p><span>S</span>et the Control Flow symbol properties in the <a>Symbol Properties dialog</a>.</p><p><br /></p>
````
