<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709623 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00254: Controlling dependency creation between used projects

- page_id: `136709623`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709623/Controlling+dependency+creation+between+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:02.320+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Automated project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could accidentally create unneeded or incorrect (from the user standpoint) dependencies between used projects in the read/write mode. Careless editing often caused cyclic module dependencies or inadvertent expansion of the scope of the other projects when the same used project was used in several other projects.

Automated project usages fix this problem.

Every automatically created usage must be backed by an existing user-defined used project usage. If it is not backed, the automated used project usage must be explicitly confirmed by the user. The usage cannot cause undesired effects in other projects while it is unconfirmed.

Automated used project usage *A**→**B* is called**unconfirmed**when there is no user-defined usage path from A to B (neither direct user-defined usage*A**→**B* nor user-defined usage path of arbitrary length *A**→ ... → X →**B*).

User-defined usages are always confirmed.

In ordinary circumstances, automated usages are not visible to the user. When the user modifies the main project’s model and adds dependencies to used projects or sub-used projects, these usages are proper. They are backed by the fact that the used project or sub-used project is attached to the project in some way; thus, the user-defined usage path refers from the main project to the used project or sub-used project.

However, there are situations when automated used project usages are not backed and thus unconfirmed.

An unconfirmed automated used project usage is considered to be invalid.

This is just one of the project composition errors. Other project composition errors include similarity to recovered element errors, conflicting user-defined usage parameters, and cyclic dependencies.

Unconfirmed automated used project usages are caught by special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active Validation Rules for Links' space='MD2024x']. They are displayed in validation results as warnings or errors depending on the project composition. The validation results prompt the user to remedy the situation by either confirming or rejecting the usage.

Unconfirmed usages can occur in several cases. They are described as follows.

#### INFO: Case #1

Case #1

When you edit a model part belonging to a used project with read/write permissions, an additional model-level reference can be created either back to the project itself (cycle) or to another used project.

These model-level usages may or may not be valid from the user point of view. Since the creation of usages is very easy, you can inadvertently create undesired usages. On the other hand, usages can be entirely benign. Our modeling tool cannot decide whether the usage is good or bad. For example, creating references from the used project *Implementation* to the used project *Requirements* is valid, while creating references in the opposite direction may be invalid since requirements usually are standalone, and implementation refers to them.

#### INFO: Case #2

Case #2

Reorganization or removal of sub-used projects may affect the projects or other used projects using the used project. This happens only in cases when a new refactored used project does not carry all of the contents that the old used project contained.

Consider a used project *ElectricComponents* with three sub-used projects: *Capacitors, Inductances,* and *Resistances.* Also consider a project *RadioSet,* which uses the used project *ElectricComponents.*Now let’s use one model element from each sub-used project in the project *RadioSet.* As a result, the three automated usages

*RadioSet* → *Capacitors,*

*RadioSet* → *Inductances,*and**

*RadioSet* → *Resistances*

will be created.

If the used project *ElectricComponents* was refactored by re-importing the sub-used project *Capacitors* and *Inductances* and completely separating the sub-used project *Resistances,* then when loading the project *RadioSet,* the automated usages *RadioSet* → *Capacitors* and *RadioSet* → *Inductances* will be automatically updated. However, the usage *RadioSet* → *Resistors* will remain unaddressed. It will appear as an unconfirmed used project usage, and you will have to confirm it, i.e., explicitly attach the used project *Resistances* to the project by creating the user-defined usage.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could accidentally create unneeded or incorrect (from the user standpoint) dependencies between used projects in the read/write mode. Careless editing often caused cyclic module dependencies or inadvertent expansion of the scope of the other projects when the same used project was used in several other projects.</span></p><p><span style="text-align: center;">Automated project usages fix this problem.</span></p><p><span>Every automatically created usage must be backed by an existing user-defined used project usage. If it is not backed, the automated used project usage must be explicitly confirmed by the user. The usage cannot cause undesired effects in other projects while it is unconfirmed. </span></p><p><span>Automated used project usage <em>A </em></span><em>→ </em><span><em>B</em> is called </span><span><strong>unconfirmed</strong> </span><span>when there is no user-defined usage path from A to B (neither direct user-defined usage<em> A </em></span><em>→ </em><span><em>B</em> nor user-defined usage path of arbitrary length <em>A </em></span><em>→ ... → X → </em><span><em>B</em>). </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87a6ff2e-53c4-4353-beee-84af03df8344"><ac:rich-text-body>User-defined usages are always confirmed.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>In ordinary circumstances, automated usages are not visible to the user. When the user modifies the main project’s model and adds dependencies to used projects or sub-used projects, these usages are proper. They are backed by the fact that the used project or sub-used project is attached to the project in some way; thus, the user-defined usage path refers from the main project to the used project or sub-used project.</p><p>However, there are situations when automated used project usages are not backed and thus unconfirmed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="808f52e0-54e8-48ad-97c6-d303a33d4766"><ac:rich-text-body>An unconfirmed automated used project usage is considered to be invalid.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This is just one of the project composition errors. Other project composition errors include similarity to recovered element errors, conflicting user-defined usage parameters, and cyclic dependencies.</p><p>Unconfirmed automated used project usages are caught by special <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Active Validation Rules for Links" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[automatic validation rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. They are displayed in validation results as warnings or errors depending on the project composition. The validation results prompt the user to remedy the situation by either confirming or rejecting the usage.</p><p>Unconfirmed usages can occur in several cases. They are described as follows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f54675e-1ffa-442e-9bb7-c0df1efb9642"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Case #1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you edit a model part belonging to a used project with read/write permissions, an additional model-level reference can be created either back to the project itself (cycle) or to another used project.</p><p>These model-level usages may or may not be valid from the user point of view. Since the creation of usages is very easy, you can inadvertently create undesired usages. On the other hand, usages can be entirely benign. Our modeling tool cannot decide whether the usage is good or bad. For example, creating references from the used project <em>Implementation</em> to the used project <em>Requirements</em> is valid, while creating references in the opposite direction may be invalid since requirements usually are standalone, and implementation refers to them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a59384f-809c-41ea-b15f-791023c1e3f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Case #2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Reorganization or removal of sub-used projects may affect the projects or other used projects using the used project. This happens only in cases when a new refactored used project does not carry all of the contents that the old used project contained.</p><p>Consider a used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> with three sub-used projects: <em>Capacitors, Inductances,</em> and <em>Resistances.</em> Also consider a project <em>RadioSet,</em> which uses the used project <em>ElectricComponents. </em>Now let’s use one model element from each sub-used project in the project <em>RadioSet.</em> As a result, the three automated usages</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Capacitors,</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Inductances, </em>and<em> </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Resistances</em></p><p>will be created.</p><p>If the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> was refactored by re-importing the sub-used project <em>Capacitors</em> and <em>Inductances</em> and completely separating the sub-used project <em>Resistances,</em> then when loading the project <em>RadioSet,</em> the automated usages <em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Capacitors</em> and <em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Inductances</em> will be automatically updated. However, the usage <em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Resistors</em> will remain unaddressed. It will appear as an unconfirmed used project usage, and you will have to confirm it, i.e., explicitly attach the used project <em>Resistances</em> to the project by creating the user-defined usage.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712035 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00255: Controlling direction

- page_id: `136712035`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712035/Controlling+direction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:05.930+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Controlling Type Mapping Rule Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, the same type map can be applied in two directions: forward and backward. The backward direction can be set by selecting the **Run type mapping in reverse order** check box in the third step of the Model Transformation Wizard. This is useful, when there are two related opposite transformations for some domain; for example, the same type map is used for both UML to XML schema and XML schema to UML transformations.

If you want to limit the directions, in which type map can be used, you can set the**defaultDirection** tag for your type map package. Possible values are **forward**, **reverse**, and **both** (default).

The direction can also be limited on a per-rule basis. This is controlled by setting the **direction** tag on the type map dependency. Then the mapping rule is excluded from the rule set when the type map is run in a different direction than specified for this rule.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>By default, the same type map can be applied in two directions: forward and backward. The backward direction can be set by selecting the <strong>Run type mapping in reverse order</strong> check box in the third step of the Model Transformation Wizard. This is useful, when there are two related opposite transformations for some domain; for example, the same type map is used for both UML to XML schema and XML schema to UML transformations.</p><p>If you want to limit the directions, in which type map can be used, you can set the<strong> defaultDirection</strong> tag for your type map package. Possible values are <strong>forward</strong>, <strong>reverse</strong>, and <strong>both</strong> (default).</p><p>The direction can also be limited on a per-rule basis. This is controlled by setting the <strong>direction</strong> tag on the type map dependency. Then the mapping rule is excluded from the rule set when the type map is run in a different direction than specified for this rule.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712037 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00256: Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types

- page_id: `136712037`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712037/Controlling+type+inheritance+any+and+empty+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:06.123+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Controlling Type Mapping Rule Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can also control mapping behavior for the type inheritance. By default, derived subtypes are also mapped by the rule governing the parent type (unless, of course, they have their own rules for mapping). If the **blockInheritedSourceTypes** tagged value is set, derived types are not affected by this rule. Let’s review the following example:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Here T1, T2, and T5 are types in the source domain, while T2 and T4 are types in the destination domain. Given these two mappings (T1 > T2 and T3 > T4), the following statement is true: T1, and all types derived from it (such as T5), are mapped to the T2 type, except T3 and any of the types derived from it. These types are 
mapped to T4.

Now consider an example where blockInheritedSourceTypes is set:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this case, T3, along with the types derived from it, are still mapped to T4. T1 is still mapped to T2. However, unlike the previous example, T5 and all the types inherited from T1 are NOT mapped to T2.

You can also control the mapping behavior of the type inheritance in the destination model. This is only effective on the transformation updates, the second (and successive) reapplications of the transformation. By default, derived subtypes in the destination model are not overwritten, since they are considered suitable substitutes of their parent. Let’s review the following example:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Here T1 is a type in the source domain, while T2 and T4 are types in the destination domain. Given this mapping (T1 > T2), on the first application of the transformation, type T1 residing in the source model will be mapped to type T2 in the destination model.

Now let us look at a case, where the user refines the destination model by changing the type on the destination model attribute from T2 to T4. This situation is quite common, for example, the user refines an attribute type from string to basic URI in the XML schema, or from Integer to *nonNegativeInteger*, and so forth. The essence is that the mapping for inherited types of T2 is performed as if there was a mapping T1 > T2 (default), T1 > T4, T1 > <any_other_type_inherited_from_T2>.

Now consider what happens, when we apply the **blockInheritedDestinationTypes** tagged value:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this case, type T4 has no special treatment. If the user applies the transformation, T1 is mapped to T2. Afterwards the user refines the destination model, changing the attribute type from T2 to T4. If the user now updates the transformation, the attribute type is overwritten: T4 is reset back to T2.

When the user loads the type map in the reverse direction, the roles of the **blockInheritedSourceTypes** and **blockInheritedDestinationTypes** are transposed (unless of course the **direction** tag mandates that this mapping is not used in the reverse direction).

The special type **EmptySourceType** (residing in the *Model_Transformation_Profile.xml.zip*) is used in type maps to indicate that the attributes with no type should be mapped with this dependency.

The special type **EmptyDestinationType** (residing in the *Model_Transformation_Profile.xml.zip*) is used to indicate that the attributes in the destination classes should have no type after remapping (type removal).

The special type **AnySourceType** is a template that matches any type in the source model (see mapping rules for type inheritance). By using this type, together with the inheritance mapping rules, the user can specify that any other types not defined by the mapping should be interpreted by this mapping.

The special type **AnyDestinationType** is a template that matches any type in the destination model (see mapping rules for type inheritance).

Here is an example of template type usage:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

According to this rule, any types in the source model for which there are no other mapping rules should be stripped in the destination model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can also control mapping behavior for the type inheritance. By default, derived subtypes are also mapped by the rule governing the parent type (unless, of course, they have their own rules for mapping). If the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">blockInheritedSourceTypes</strong> tagged value is set, derived types are not affected by this rule. Let’s review the following example:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_BlockInherited.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Here T1, T2, and T5 are types in the source domain, while T2 and T4 are types in the destination domain. Given these two mappings (T1 &gt; T2 and T3 &gt; T4), the following statement is true: T1, and all types derived from it (such as T5), are mapped to the T2 type, except T3 and any of the types derived from it. These types are <br />mapped to T4.</p><p>Now consider an example where blockInheritedSourceTypes is set:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_BlockInherited1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this case, T3, along with the types derived from it, are still mapped to T4. T1 is still mapped to T2. However, unlike the previous example, T5 and all the types inherited from T1 are NOT mapped to T2.</p><p>You can also control the mapping behavior of the type inheritance in the destination model. This is only effective on the transformation updates, the second (and successive) reapplications of the transformation. By default, derived subtypes in the destination model are not overwritten, since they are considered suitable substitutes of their parent. Let’s review the following example:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_BlockInheritedDest.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Here T1 is a type in the source domain, while T2 and T4 are types in the destination domain. Given this mapping (T1 &gt; T2), on the first application of the transformation, type T1 residing in the source model will be mapped to type T2 in the destination model.</p><p>Now let us look at a case, where the user refines the destination model by changing the type on the destination model attribute from T2 to T4. This situation is quite common, for example, the user refines an attribute type from string to basic URI in the XML schema, or from Integer to <em>nonNegativeInteger</em>, and so forth. The essence is that the mapping for inherited types of T2 is performed as if there was a mapping T1 &gt; T2 (default), T1 &gt; T4, T1 &gt; &lt;any_other_type_inherited_from_T2&gt;.</p><p>Now consider what happens, when we apply the <strong>blockInheritedDestinationTypes</strong> tagged value:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_BlockInheritedDest1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this case, type T4 has no special treatment. If the user applies the transformation, T1 is mapped to T2. Afterwards the user refines the destination model, changing the attribute type from T2 to T4. If the user now updates the transformation, the attribute type is overwritten: T4 is reset back to T2.</p><p>When the user loads the type map in the reverse direction, the roles of the <strong>blockInheritedSourceTypes</strong> and <strong>blockInheritedDestinationTypes</strong> are transposed (unless of course the <strong>direction</strong> tag mandates that this mapping is not used in the reverse direction).</p><p>The special type <strong>EmptySourceType</strong> (residing in the <em>Model_Transformation_Profile.xml.zip</em>) is used in type maps to indicate that the attributes with no type should be mapped with this dependency.</p><p>The special type <strong>EmptyDestinationType</strong> (residing in the <em>Model_Transformation_Profile.xml.zip</em>) is used to indicate that the attributes in the destination classes should have no type after remapping (type removal).</p><p>The special type <strong>AnySourceType</strong> is a template that matches any type in the source model (see mapping rules for type inheritance). By using this type, together with the inheritance mapping rules, the user can specify that any other types not defined by the mapping should be interpreted by this mapping.</p><p>The special type <strong>AnyDestinationType</strong> is a template that matches any type in the destination model (see mapping rules for type inheritance).</p><p>Here is an example of template type usage:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_AnySourceType.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>According to this rule, any types in the source model for which there are no other mapping rules should be stripped in the destination model.</p><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 00259: Conversion with Array Type Modifiers

- page_id: `136715563`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715563/Conversion+with+Array+Type+Modifiers
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:35:30.768+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering > Conversion from an Earlier Project Version
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1398284398

1398284400

1398284399

With version 12, type modifiers use the character *$* to specify the type modifiers construct. This allows mapping of complex type modifiers. For example,*const int* const*is mapped to*const $* const*.

##### Type Modifier conversion in Attribute

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Type Modifier conversion in Attribute

To convert a Type modifier in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='MD2024x']:

1. Open the Attribute specification window.
2. Remove *[15]* from **Type Modifier** field.
3. Apply the «C++Attribute» stereotype.
4. Set **Multiplicity** field to 0..*. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Go to Tags and set Array tag value to *[15]*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Type Modifier conversion in Parameter

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Type Modifier conversion in Parameter

To convert a Type modifier in Parameter:

1. Open the Parameter specification window.
2. Remove [15] from Type Modifier field.
3. Apply the «C++Parameter» stereotype.
4. Set Multiplicity field to 0..*. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2018-1-16_17-19-2.png']
5. Go to Tags and set Array tag value to [15] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1398284397

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Conversion from an Earlier Project Version' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711581 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00260: Converting Elements

- page_id: `136711581`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711581/Converting+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:46.025+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Refactoring
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Original element

The original element is the element that will be converted. In other words, it is the conversion source.

###### Conversion target

The conversion target is the element type, to which the original element is going to be converted.

###### Converted element

The converted element is the element that type has been changed during the conversion.

###### Related elements

The related elements are elements having relations to the original element. Related elements can be the following:

- If the original element is a relationship, elements connected by this relationship are considered as related element to relationship.
- If the original element is not a relationship, relationships going from or to the original element are the related elements (such as an association, dependency, generalization, and others).
- Other related elements associated to the original element. They are properties of the original element, such as inner elements, types, and others.

###### Compatible properties

The compatible properties are original element specification that are compatible with the conversion target. The term “properties” includes related elements, inner elements, and all other elements listed in the element Specification window.

Compatible means that the converted element can have or own the particular property or properties of the original element.

###### Incompatible properties

Incompatible properties are original element specification that are incompatible with the conversion target. Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT have or can NOT own the particular property(-ies) of the original element.

Element conversion allows converting one element type to another. To be more specific, the element conversion functionality allows the UML model element conversion from one metaclass to another. For example, a class element can be converted to a use case, component, interface, or other. All compatible properties and all compatible related elements are moved from the source element to the converted element during the conversion.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Relationships conversion is available.
- Multiple elements can be selected on the diagram or in the Model Browser to convert them to other types.

INLINE

To convert elements

1. From the selected element's shortcut menu, select Refactor > Convert To . The list with available conversion target types opens.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element type from the commonly used element type list. The element is converted.
  - Select More Elements to see the whole list of available conversion targets. In the opened More Elements dialog, select the element type you need and click OK . The element is converted and selected element type is included as commonly used element type.

As of the MagicDraw version 17.0.2, the following commands have been moved under the **Refactor** command in the shortcut menu of the particular element:

- Convert To > Output Pin for the selected Object Node or Input Pin.
- Convert To > Input Pin for the selected Object Node or Output Pin.
- Convert To > Object Node for the selected Input Pin or Output Pin.
- Convert To > Attribute(s) for the selected Association that has defined roles, that is, roles have names.
- Convert To > Association(s) for the selected Attribute. NOTE : The Association(s) command is available only if the attribute(s) type is specified.

- If the Refactor command is unavailable, make sure you have permissions to edit the selected element or related element(s).
- Use Undo to restore the original element and its data.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In other words, it is the conversion source.</p><h4>Conversion target</h4><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The conversion target is the element type, to which the original element is going to be converted.</p><h4>Converted element</h4><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The converted element is the element that type has been changed during the conversion.</p><h4>Related elements</h4><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The related elements are elements having relations to the original element. The list with available conversion target types opens.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element type from the commonly used element type list. The element is converted.</li><li>Select <strong>More Elements</strong> to see the whole list of available conversion targets. In the opened <strong>More Elements</strong> dialog, select the element type you need and click <strong>OK</strong>. The element is converted and selected element type is included as commonly used element type.</li></ul></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a456a27-873e-4747-a07d-dd88e376e87d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>As of the MagicDraw version 17.0.2, the following commands have been moved under the <strong>Refactor</strong> command in the shortcut menu of the particular element:</p><ul><li><strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Output Pin</strong> for the selected Object Node or Input Pin. </li><li><strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Input Pin</strong> for the selected Object Node or Output Pin.</li><li><strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Object Node</strong> for the selected Input Pin or Output Pin.</li><li><strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Attribute(s)</strong> for the selected Association that has defined roles, that is, roles have names.</li><li><strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Association(s)</strong> for the selected Attribute. <strong>NOTE</strong>: The Association(s) command is available only if the attribute(s) type is specified.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="104de9ad-feea-4320-8cd5-d8310cb200a9"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the <strong>Refactor</strong> command is unavailable, make sure you have permissions to edit the selected element or related element(s).</li><li>Use <strong>Undo</strong> to restore the original element and its data.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4810fea3-739c-4de7-90a8-6fa7ea57512d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="089ecc4f-400a-472c-916b-c7607d8b8764" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711690 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00261: Copy and paste symbol style

- page_id: `136711690`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711690/Copy+and+paste+symbol+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:50.308+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A symbol style defined in symbol properties can be copied and pasted on another symbol. Copy a symbol and then select one or more other symbols of the same type where you need to paste copied properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To copy and paste symbol style

1. On the diagram pane, select symbol(s) which style properties you want to copy.
2. Copy style properties by doing one of the following:
  - From the Edit menu, select Copy .
  - Press Ctrl+C.
  - Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='copy.png'] Copy button on the diagram toolbar.
3. On the diagram pane, select the symbol(s) on which you want to paste style properties.
4. Paste style properties on the selected symbols by doing one of the following:
  - From the Edit menu, select Paste Style .
  - Press Ctrl+Shift+V.
  - Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='paste_style.png'] Paste Style button on the diagram toolbar.

You can copy and paste symbol properties of the more than one symbol at the time. Symbol properties will be pasted according to the element type.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711847 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00262: Copying and opening element URLs

- page_id: `136711847`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711847/Copying+and+opening+element+URLs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:59.257+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1213345435

1213345437

1213345436

You can copy a project element URL to a clipboard and share it with others as a quick reference to model elements.

Copying and opening element URLs do not work with the no install version (.zip) of a modeling tool.

To copy a project element URL, do one of the following

- Select Copy URL from the element shortcut menu in the Containment tree to copy the URL to a model element.
- Select the element symbol in a diagram and click Edit > Copy URL on the main menu to copy the URL to element symbol.

You can open any element through its URL by clicking the **Open Element from URL** command. The element will then be highlighted in the Containment tree or in the diagram. A custom URL "mdel://" is registered into windows registry. Activating the URL in other applications will allow you to start MagicDraw, open the project (if possible), and select one or more elements. You can paste URLs from the clipboard to any MagicDraw diagram. Hyperlinks also can hold URLs of any model elements.

You cannot open elements using their URLs on an internet browser to start a modeling tool.

1213345434

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710275 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00263: Copying and pasting data

- page_id: `136710275`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710275/Copying+and+pasting+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:34.390+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can transfer information by copying and pasting data between tables and other resources, such as Excel spreadsheets, documents, web pages, email messages, and presentations. You are able to copy and paste data:

33

##### From other resources to modeling tool tables

You can copy information from other resources (e.g. Excel spreadsheets, documents, web pages, email messages, presentations) and paste it into a modeling tool table. After pasting the data to the modeling tool table, the new elements are created in the model. If elements already exist in the model, the information updates. The ID prefixes are set automatically after pasting data into modeling tool tables.

The following rules should be satisfied for a successful copy-and-paste operation to modeling tool tables:

- The cells must be editable.
- The number of columns in the other resource should be the same as the number of columns in the modeling tool table.
- The data types of columns in the modeling tool table must be compatible with the copied information from the other resource.

How to copy data from another resource and paste it into a modeling tool:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='MD2024x']]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space='MD2024x']]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Glossary table' space='MD2024x']]]>

To copy data from another resource and paste it to the modeling tool table

1. Copy the table from another resource. - The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the table.- The data types of columns in the table must be compatible with the copied information.
2. Open the table where you want to paste the copied data.
3. In the Criteria area, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to specify the Element Type (in Generic table), Classifier (in Instance table), or Metric Suite (in Metric table).
4. In the table toolbar, click the **Show Columns/Columns** button and choose the columns that you want to fill with the copied information. The cells must be editable.
5. Do one of the following: 
 - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
 - In the main toolbar, select **Edit** > **Paste.**The new elements are created in the model. If the element already exists in the model, the information updates. If elements have an ID prefix in another resource, they are set automatically after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted elements in the table. Manage ID prefixes in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MD2024x']**Element Numbering** dialog.

##### From modeling tool tables to other resources

You can transfer information by copying and pasting data from modeling tool tables to other resources (e.g. Excel spreadsheets, documents, web pages, email messages, presentations).

To copy data from modeling tool tables and paste it to other resources

1. Select the required cells in the modeling tool table.
2. Do one of the following: - Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on OS X). - In the main toolbar, select Edit > Copy . The data is stored in the clipboard.
3. Open the other resource where you want to paste the data and select the first cell in the area you want to paste what you copied (e.g. Excel spreadsheet).
4. Press Ctrl+V. The contents of the cells are transferred to another resource.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Between cells in the modeling tool table

You can fill the table cells by copying the content from the modeling tool table cell(s) and pasting it to another cell(s) in the same or another modeling tool table(s). After pasting the data to a modeling tool table the new elements are created in the model. If elements already exist in the model, the information updates. The ID prefixes are set automatically after pasting data into modeling tool tables.

The following rules should be satisfied for a successful copy-and-paste operation to modeling tool tables:

- The cells must be editable.
- The data types of columns in the modeling tool table must be compatible with copied information.
- The cells contain the following data types: String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration.

Copy and paste cell content into a modeling tool table

1. Select the cells that contain the data you want to copy.
2. Do one of the following: - Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on OS X). - In the main toolbar, select Edit > Copy . The data is stored in the clipboard.
3. Select single or multiple cells where you want to paste what you copied.
4. Do one of the following: - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). - In the main toolbar, select Edit > Paste. The content of the cell(s) is duplicated in another cell(s).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Copying and pasting data' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Manage ID prefixes in the<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>From modeling tool tables to other resources</h3><p>You can transfer information by copying and pasting data from modeling tool tables to other resources (e.g. Excel spreadsheets, documents, web pages, email messages, presentations).</p><p><br /></p><p>To copy data from modeling tool tables and paste it to other resources</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the required cells in the modeling tool table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on OS X).<br /> - In the main toolbar, select <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Copy</strong>.<br />The data is stored in the clipboard.</li><li>Open the other resource where you want to paste the data and select the first cell in the area you want to paste what you copied (e.g. Excel spreadsheet).</li><li>Press Ctrl+V.<br />The contents of the cells are transferred to another resource.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy_paste_operation_from MD_table_to_excel.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Copying and pasting data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Between cells in the modeling tool table</h3><p>You can fill the table cells by copying the content from the modeling tool table cell(s) and pasting it to another cell(s) in the same or another modeling tool table(s). <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710490 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00264: Copying and pasting objects

- page_id: `136710490`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710490/Copying+and+pasting+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:41.301+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1422299706

1422299708

1422299707

Modeling tools support all common copying/cutting and pasting functions, as well as advanced copying and pasting functions. You can copy and paste objects from one project to another. You can find these options in the **Edit** menu.

Advanced commands for copying:

| Command | Shortcut | Description |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Windows | Mac OS |  |
| Copy URL |  |  | Copies the URL of the selected project element or element symbol to the clipboard (the project element and the element symbol have different URLs). To copy the URL of an element, select Copy URL from the element shortcut menu in the Containment tree. To copy the URL of an element symbol, select the the symbol and click Edit > Copy URL.You can use URLs to open any elements by clicking the Open Element from URL command. The element is then highlighted in the Containment tree or in a diagram. Activating the URL in another application allows you to start MagicDraw, open a project (if possible), and select any elements. You can paste URLs from the clipboard to any MagicDraw diagram. Hyperlinks can also hold URLs of any model elements. |
| Copy as BMP Image | Ctrl+Shift+B | ⌘⇧B | Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .bmp image format provides the best image quality. The image files of this format are typically not compressed, resulting in a large file. |
| Copy as EMF Image | Ctrl+Shift+E | ⌘⇧E | Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .emf format image files are smaller, so using this format reduces network traffic. Additionally, the .emf format supports language specific symbols.EMF does not support gradient fill. Because icons of the modeling tool are stored in the SVG format, it may be exported incorrectly.To render SVG icons as raster images in EMF, on the main menu, click Options > Environment > General > Image Export and set the Render SVG Icons as Raster Images in EMF option to true. |
| Copy as JPG Image | Ctrl+Shift+J | ⌘⇧J | Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .jpg format produces relatively small file sizes, but when compressing, the .jpg image file loses data, which results in lower quality. Thus, this format is suitable for final distribution of images, but is not recommended if images are to be edited. |
| Copy as PNG Image | Ctrl+Shift+P | ⌘⇧P | Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .png format is best suited for storing images during the editing process because of its lossless compression. Compared to the .jpg format, the .png excels when the image has large areas of uniform color. It also works well in online viewing applications, such as web browsers. |

Advanced commands for pasting:

| Command | Shortcut | Description |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Windows | Mac OS |  |
| Paste with New Element | Ctrl+E | ⌘E | Pastes one or more copied model elements by creating new model elements. Use this command when copying or cutting owned symbols from one shape to another in a diagram. |
| Paste Style | Ctrl+Shift+V | ⌘⇧V | Pastes symbol properties to the selected symbol. You can copy and paste symbol properties of more than one symbol at the same time. Symbol properties are pasted according to the element type. For example, on a class symbol, the class symbol properties are pasted, and on a package symbol, the package symbol properties are pasted, etc. |
| Paste as Diagram Overview |  |  | Creates a new diagram overview representing selected elements. A diagram overview shape provides the ability to overview other diagrams on a diagram pane. Moreover, using diagram overview shape you can overview dependency matrices, tables, and relation maps. |

In addition to the described commands, there you have the ability to copy the whole diagram or the selected shapes and paste them to Microsoft Office or other applications.

Not all symbols can be copied/cut and pasted.

1422299705

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e84ca238-c496-48f3-88d2-46ccebbc0ee1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422299706</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21afcc82-def6-4897-a871-955b080fd72d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422299708</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa0d407e-bd77-4bfd-b942-1505c5110401"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422299707</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="33eb3cc6-8f15-460b-851e-359d51991cb5">Modeling t</ac:inline-comment-marker>ools support all common copying/cutting and pasting functions, as well as advanced copying and pasting functions. You can copy and paste objects from one project to another. You can find these options in the <strong>Edit</strong> menu.</p><p>Advanced commands for copying:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 7.5551624%;" /><col style="width: 8.149018%;" /><col style="width: 7.2740707%;" /><col style="width: 77.02307%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Command</th><th colspan="2">Shortcut</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1">Windows</td><td colspan="1">Mac OS</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Copy URL</td><td><br /></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5d625488-ac25-4ec5-8e53-e7dac4a78fe6">Copies the<span> URL of the selected project element</span></ac:inline-comment-marker> or element symbol to the clipboard (the project element and the element symbol have different URLs). <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2155cd2d-7dcf-43d7-9246-85b590cd0edc">To copy the URL of an element, select </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2155cd2d-7dcf-43d7-9246-85b590cd0edc">Copy URL</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2155cd2d-7dcf-43d7-9246-85b590cd0edc"> from the element shortcut menu in the Containment tree. </ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="line-height: 1.42857;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2155cd2d-7dcf-43d7-9246-85b590cd0edc">To copy the URL of an element symbol, select the the symbol and click </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2155cd2d-7dcf-43d7-9246-85b590cd0edc">Edit</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2155cd2d-7dcf-43d7-9246-85b590cd0edc"> &gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2155cd2d-7dcf-43d7-9246-85b590cd0edc">Copy URL</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2155cd2d-7dcf-43d7-9246-85b590cd0edc">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><p>You can use URLs to open any elements by clicking the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Open Element from URL</strong> command. The element is then highlighted in the Containment tree or in a diagram. Activating the URL in another application allows you to start MagicDraw, open a project (if possible), and select any elements. You can paste URLs from the clipboard to any MagicDraw diagram. Hyperlinks can <span>also </span>hold URLs of any model elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Copy as BMP Image</td><td><span>Ctrl+Shift+B</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>⌘<span>⇧B</span></span></td><td><p>Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .bmp image format provides the best image quality. The image files of this format are typically not compressed, resulting in a large file.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Copy as EMF Image</td><td><span>Ctrl+Shift+E</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>⌘</span><span>⇧E</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Copies the selected shapes</span><span> to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied.</span> The .emf format image files are smaller, so using this format reduces network traffic. Additionally, the .emf format <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">supports language specific symbols.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4d74ac2-4794-43ae-9cd5-31c4a96ec579"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>EMF does not support gradient fill. Because icons of the modeling tool are stored in the SVG format, it may be exported incorrectly.</span></p><p>To render SVG icons as raster images in EMF, on the main menu, click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Environment </strong>&gt; <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Image Export </strong>and set the <strong>Render SVG Icons as Raster Images in EMF </strong>option<strong> </strong>to <em>true.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Copy as JPG Image</td><td><span>Ctrl+Shift+J</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>⌘</span><span>⇧J</span></td><td><p><span><span>Copies the selected shapes</span><span> to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied.</span> The .jpg format produces relatively small file sizes, but when compressing, the .jpg image file loses data, which results in lower quality. Thus, this format is suitable for final distribution of images, but is not recommended if images are to be edited.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Copy as PNG Image</td><td><span>Ctrl+Shift+P</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>⌘</span><span>⇧P</span></td><td><p><span><span>Copies the selected shapes</span><span> to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied.</span> The .png format is best suited for<span> <span style="color: rgb(37,37,37);">storing images during the editing process because of its lossless compression. </span><span style="color: rgb(37,37,37);">Compared to the .jpg format, the .png excels when the image has</span> <span style="color: rgb(37,37,37);">large areas of uniform color. </span><span><span style="color: rgb(37,37,37);">It also works well in online viewing applications, such as web browsers.</span></span></span></span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /><br /></p><p>Advanced commands for pasting:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Command</th><th colspan="2">Shortcut</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1">Windows</td><td colspan="1">Mac OS</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Paste with New Element</td><td>Ctrl+E</td><td colspan="1"><span>⌘E</span></td><td><p>Pastes one or more copied model elements by creating new model elements. Use this command when copying or cutting owned symbols from one shape to another in a diagram.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Paste Style</td><td>Ctrl+Shift+V</td><td colspan="1"><span>⌘</span><span>⇧V</span></td><td><span>Pastes symbol properties to the selected symbol. </span><span>You can copy and paste symbol properties of more than one symbol at the same time. </span><span>Symbol properties are pasted according to the element type. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7d28e883-ec78-4491-aa22-34b5d88a2bfd">For example, on a class </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7d28e883-ec78-4491-aa22-34b5d88a2bfd">symbol, the class symbol properties are pasted, and on a package symbol, the package </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7d28e883-ec78-4491-aa22-34b5d88a2bfd">symbol properties are pasted, etc.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></td></tr><tr><td>Paste as Diagram Overview</td><td><br /></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td><span>Creates a new diagram overview representing selected elements. A diagram overview shape provides the ability to overview other diagrams on a diagram pane. Moreover, using </span><span>diagram overview shape you can overview dependency matrices, tables, and relation maps.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>In addition to the described commands, there you have the ability to copy the whole diagram or the selected shapes and paste them to Microsoft Office or other applications.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48b930c1-778a-48b8-88b6-aab3601e1140"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Not all symbols can be copied/cut and pasted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5371c328-5f46-4f7b-b704-334e8ba07f0d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422299705</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Saving as image" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715157 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00265: Copying elements and symbols

- page_id: `136715157`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715157/Copying+elements+and+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:10.227+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can copy model elements and symbols either to another location in the same project or to another project. This must be done in the same session.

There are two modes of making copies:

- Deep copying (with new data)
- Shallow copying (with reused data)

###### **Example #1: Copying an element**

****

```java

```

###### **Example #2: Copying multiple elements and symbols**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in *<installation_directory>**\openapi\examples\copypaste*

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Checking element editing permissions' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>You can copy model elements and symbols either to another location in the same project or to another project. This must be done in the same session.</p><p>There are two modes of making copies:</p><ul><li>Deep copying (with new data)</li><li>Shallow copying (with reused data)</li></ul><p> </p><h4><strong>Example #1: Copying an element</strong></h4><p> </p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="deac64b1-d060-474a-a06a-bf2681845ca5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Element element = ...; // An element to copy/paste.
    Element parent = ...; // A parent to which the element has to be pasted: either the same project or another project.
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Clone");
 
    // A 3rd parameter indicates whether an element name uniqueness should be preserved in the parent.
    CopyPasting.copyPasteElement(element, parent, true);
 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></div><h4><strong>Example #2: Copying multiple elements and symbols</strong></h4><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88ba897e-6dca-4f24-b872-a494d56d1ed5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    List elements = ...; // Elements to copy/paste. 
    List views = ...; // Symbols to copy/paste.
    Element parent = ...; // A parent to which elements should be pasted: either the same project or another project.
    BaseElement symbolParent = ...; // A parent to which symbols should be pasted. 
    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Clone");


    // A 4th parameter indicates whether deep or shallow copy is applied.
    // A 5th parameter indicates whether an element name uniqueness should be preserved in the parent.
    List baseElements = CopyPasting.copyPasteElements(views, parent, symbolParent, true, true);
 
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;</em><em>\openapi\examples\copypaste</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b50b2c6-e274-460d-b17d-b7fdbdbe92c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Checking element editing permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710682 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00266: Copying text or images to diagrams

- page_id: `136710682`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710682/Copying+text+or+images+to+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:53.795+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

570108383

570108385

570108384

You can copy the text or images to a diagram. The copied text or image is pasted into a text box or an image shape. MagicDraw supports the HTML and plain text, and images of gif, jpg, svg, and png image file types. This copy and paste functionality allows you to drag and drop data from other applications, for example, web browsers.

To copy the text or image to a diagram

1. Select the text or image.
2. Press Ctrl+C.
3. Open the diagram.
4. Press Ctrl+V. The Paste Special dialog opens.
5. Select Image , Plain Text , or HTML Text according to what you want to paste on the diagram.

570108382

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024x']

The copied text or image is pasted into a text box or an image shape. MagicDraw supports the HTML and plain text, and images of gif, jpg, svg, and png image file types. This copy and paste functionality allows you to drag and drop data from other applications, for example, web browsers.</p><p><br /></p><p>To copy the text or image to a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the text or image.</li><li>Press Ctrl+C.</li><li>Open the diagram.</li><li>Press Ctrl+V. The <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select <strong>Image</strong>, <strong>Plain Text</strong>, or <strong>HTML Text</strong> according to what you want to paste on the diagram.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54003f95-1740-4eec-bac5-3ee0534d93e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">570108382</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionalities

135158942084

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionalities

345982345

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionalities

23458729345

INLINE

#### NOTE: Functionalities

Functionalities

This feature is available in Architect and Enterprise editions.

The CORBA IDL diagram facilitates the creation of CORBA IDL elements. The following patterns are also available for CORBA IDL: Interface, Value Type, Type Definition, Sequence, Array, Fixed, Union, Enumeration, Struct, and Exception. For more information about CORBA IDL usage in MagicDraw, see MagicDraw Code Engineering UserGuide.pdf, chapter "CORBA IDL Mapping to UML".

You can select either the UML Interface or the UML Class as a base element for the CORBA Interface. For more information about the CORBA IDL Interface implementation, see CORBA Interface Implementation.

Reference: UMLTMProfile for CORBATMSpecification, Version 1.0, April 2002.[http://www.omg.org/technology/documents/formal/profile_corba.htm](http://www.omg.org/technology/documents/formal/profile_corba.htm)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the CORBA IDL diagram *Tickets Purchasing*

43578293475

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715649 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00268: CORBA IDL Mapping To UML

- page_id: `136715649`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715649/CORBA+IDL+Mapping+To+UML
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:41.365+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Reference:**

[http://www.omg.org/technology/documents/profile_catalog.htm#UML_for_CORBA](http://www.omg.org/technology/documents/profile_catalog.htm#UML_for_CORBA).

CORBA IDL mapping to UML is based on the UML Profile for CORBA Specification, Version 1.0, April 2002.

#### INFO: Availability

Availability

This functionality is available in the Enterprise edition only.

The differences between the UML Profile for CORBA specification and mapping in MagicDraw are listed below.

- Constraints defined in the UML Profile for CORBA specification are not checked in MagicDraw.
- The stereotype CORBAAnonymousFixed is introduced. It is used to represent anonymous fixed types of a stereotype. Fixed types without names are mapped to inner classes with the stereotype CORBAAnonymousFixed. These classes are bound to CORBA::fixed classes.IDL Code:

#### TIP: Example

Example

Code:

```xml
high_scale;

fixed <8, 2> low_scale;

};]]>
```

This code is mapped to the following diagram: [IMAGE alt='' src='']

MagicDraw presents a CORBA IDL diagram that simplifies the creation of standard CORBA IDL elements. The following elements are available in the CORBA IDL diagram:

| Button | Shortcut Key | Model Element |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | M | CORBA IDL Module |
|  | I | CORBA IDL Interface |
|  | V | CORBA IDL Value |
|  | Shift + P | Class by Pattern |
|  | G | Generalization |
|  |  | Truncatable Generalization |
|  |  | Value Support Generalization |
|  |  | CORBA IDL Association |
|  | I | Interface |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /><strong>Reference: </strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(90,109,148);"><a href="http://www.omg.org/technology/documents/profile_catalog.htm#UML_for_CORBA">http://www.omg.org/technology/documents/profile_catalog.htm#UML_for_CORBA</a></span>.</p><p>CORBA IDL mapping to UML is based on the UML Profile for CORBA Specification, Version 1.0, April 2002.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28160a67-0c30-43a5-8216-4ec2882915e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Availability</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in the Enterprise edition only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The differences between the UML Profile for CORBA specification and mapping in MagicDraw are listed below.</p><ul><li><span>Constraints defined in the UML Profile for CORBA specification are not checked in MagicDraw. </span></li><li><span>The stereotype CORBAAnonymousFixed is introduced. It is used to represent anonymous fixed types of a stereotype. Fixed types without names are mapped to inner classes with the stereotype CORBAAnonymousFixed. These classes are bound to CORBA::fixed classes.IDL Code:</span><span><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46d9bf5c-9645-4ef2-8269-6db18f837565"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target">Code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9d90256-3566-4149-a7aa-69bb9bb4f5f9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[struct baz
{

fixed <8, 4> high_scale;

fixed <8, 2> low_scale;

};]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">This code is mapped to the following diagram: <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-25_18-48-8.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="CORBA IDL Mapping To UML" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>MagicDraw presents a CORBA IDL diagram that simplifies the creation of standard CORBA IDL elements. The following elements are available in the CORBA IDL diagram: </p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Shortcut Key</th><th>Model Element</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="31"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-25_18-48-43.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="CORBA IDL Mapping To UML" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>M</td><td>CORBA IDL Module</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-25_18-48-55.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="CORBA IDL Mapping To UML" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>I</td><td>CORBA IDL Interface</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-25_18-49-2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="CORBA IDL Mapping To UML" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>V</td><td>CORBA IDL Value</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="30"><ri:attachment /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>G</td><td>Generalization</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-25_18-49-25.png"><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715660 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00269: CORBA interface implementation

- page_id: `136715660`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715660/CORBA+interface+implementation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:41.511+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > CORBA IDL Mapping To UML
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can select the UML Interface or the UML Class as a base element for the CORBA Interface. The default element as a base element for CORBA Interfaces is:

- **UML Class**: a class as a base element for CORBA Interfaces appears in projects created in MagicDraw version 17.0.1. The CORBA Interfaces are modeled as classes in these projects.
- **UML Interface**: an interface as a base element for CORBA Interfaces appears in projects created in MagicDraw version 17.0.1 and later. The CORBA Interfaces are modeled as interfaces in these projects.

#### WARNING: Important!

Important!

The generalization relationship can be used only between CORBA Interfaces based on the same element. Thus, you can use the generalization relationship between two CORBA Intarfaces based on a class or between two CORBA Interfaces based on an interface, but not between a CORBA Interface based on a class and a CORBA Interface based on an interface.

To set a base element for CORBA Interfaces

1.On the**Options**menu, click**Project**. The**Project Options**dialog opens.

2.In the Tab tree, select**General**.

3.In the**General**list, click the**CORBA IDL**and then **CORBA Interfaces implemented as**value cell.The list of available values opens.

4.Do one of the following:

- Select UML Class to set a class as the base element for the CORBA Interface.
- Select UML Interface to set an interface as the base element for the CORBA Interface.

5.Click**OK**when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change a base element of CORBA Interfaces

1.Select the CORBA Interface element in the Model Browser or its symbol on the diagram pane.

2.From the shortcut menu of the selected CORBA Interface, point to**Refactor**>**Convert To**, and then do one of the following:

- Click Class if an interface is the current base element for CORBA Interface.
- Click Interface if a class is the current base element for CORBA Interface.

#### WARNING: Important!

Important!

Convert the selected CORBA Interface only to the class or interface element. Conversion to other elements changes the CORBA Interfaceelement to the selected element but not to the element the CORBA Interface is based on.

For more information about refactoring, see [Refactoring](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2022x/Refactoring).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The CORBA Interfaces are modeled as classes in these projects.</span></li><li><span><strong>UML Interface</strong>: an interface as a base element for CORBA Interfaces appears in projects created in MagicDraw version 17.0.1 and later. The CORBA Interfaces are modeled as interfaces in these projects.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97969abe-4cfa-49ea-8dc5-12e725e1e54f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important!</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>The generalization relationship can be used only between CORBA Interfaces based on the same element. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711497 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00270: Covering use cases of Excel Import Plugin

- page_id: `136711497`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711497/Covering+use+cases+of+Excel+Import+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:43.297+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page is dedicated for those who used the Excel Import Plugin for data import to the model. As of 2021x version this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x'] (import to table) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x'] (import to model) features. To explain the mapping of deprecated Excel Import Plugin, read the following sections:

5

##### Removing auto usages

Before importing data from Excel or CSV files by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x'] features, you must remove auto usages of Excel Import Plugin, since 20.0 version its no longer available to use. [Learn more about automated project usages >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Automated+project+usages)

To remove the Excel Import Plugin usage from your project

1. Open the project.
2. In the Containment tree, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Show Auxiliary Resources .
3. Right-click an Excel Import Profile package in the Containment tree and, from its shortcut menu, select Project Usages > Remove. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other model places.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Preparing the Excel spreadsheet

Before importing data from Excel file by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x'] features, we recommend to make sure the data in your Excel file is in line. As shown in the following figure, the merged cells are eliminated by showing nested structure in line. [Learn more about preparing Excel file to import >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The comparison of Excel tables available to import by using the Excel Import Plugin and Excel/CSV Sync or Excel/CSV Import features.

##### User interface comparison

To import data from Excel or CSV file by using the Excel Import Plugin, three dialogs were used to import table heading, create mapping and import data. The same cases are covered with one dialog depending where you want to import data:

- To the table use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='MD2024x']**Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog .
- To the model use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog .

You cannot create a diagram when importing data by using the Excel/CSV Sync or the Excel/CSV Import features.

The figure below illustrates the differences between dialogs of Excel Import Plugin and **Excel/CSV Sync Options** or **Excel/CSV Import**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The UI differences between Excel Import Plugin and Excel/CSV Sync and Excel/CSV Import.

##### Mapping comparison

The Excel Import Plugin allows you to create mapping in the **Create Mapping** dialog that generates the class diagram. In the **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog you create mapping directly in the dialog. The comparison of the Excel Import Plugin and Excel/CSV Sync mappings are provided in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The mapping differences between Excel Import Plugin and Excel/CSV Sync.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

