The specific subclasses of an element define their own notation. <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Each UML </span>element<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> has a </span>composition<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> relationship to itself to support the capability for elements to </span>own<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> other elements.</span></span></span></li><li><strong>Use case</strong><br />A specification of behavior. A use case is a kind of BehavioredClassifier that represents a declaration of a set of offered Behaviors. Each use case specifies some behavior that a subject can perform in collaboration with one or more actors. A UseCase may be owned either by a Package or by a Classifier. <span>Use cases are a means to capture the requirements of systems,( i.e. what systems are supposed to do). </span></li></ul><h2>Sample model</h2><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="view-file" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b697956-c268-47c6-a6c6-87b8403e366e"><ac:parameter ac:name="name"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Inventory Control System.mdzip" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">250</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2>More information about creating report templates</h2><p>To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Your First Template" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Linking report templates to the modeling tool" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exercise: Building a simple class diagram report" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710903 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00284: Creating a Structured Class

- page_id: `136710903`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710903/Creating+a+Structured+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:06.032+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Class
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is a way to create a piece of the model with a single click: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'], with a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] inside it and hyperlink them. It can be useful for architects and system engineers.The same applies to SysML Block and IBD.

To create a structured class

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] .
2. In the class diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to the Class symbol to reveal a list of available elements. Select the Structured Class element.
3. Click on the Class diagram. The class element with a hyperlink to a Composite Structure diagram is created.
4. Select the Class in the Browser to see its structure. To do this, choose Select in Containment Tree on the diagram pane from the Class [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH185'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.19.54 PM.png']

In the Browser, you can see the created Class with the Composite Structure diagram inside it. The Class is linked with the diagram. It means that after double-clicking the diagram, or the Browser, the Composite Structure diagram will opened.

#### TIP: Hyperlinks

Hyperlinks

For more information about hyperlinks, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='MD2024x'].

The names of the Class and the Composite Structure diagram are synchronized. Type a name for the Class, for example, *Library Book*, and the name of the diagram is automatically changed to *Library Book* and vice versa. This is synchronization of a diagram name and its context name.

#### TIP: Diagram Name and Context Name

Diagram Name and Context Name

For more information about diagram name and its context synchronization, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram name and its context name synchronization' space='MD2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class Diagram Wizard' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714580 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00285: Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)

- page_id: `136714580`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714580/Creating+a+template+for+a+Rich+Text+Format+or+OpenDocument+Text+document+or+Microsoft+Word+Document+DOCX
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:42.003+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool > Supported file types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358508562

1358508564

1358530037

**On this page**

3

1358508564

#### Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)

You can create a document in .rtf, .odt, or .docx file format and add it to MagicDraw as a report template.

To create a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (*DOCX*)

1. Open OpenOffice.org Writer or Microsoft Word for an *.odt , *.rtf , or *.docx file. (To save a file as an *.odt file in Microsoft Word, an add-on is required. You can get the add-on at [http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/](http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/) .)
2. Add the Velocity Template Language codes to the template file.
3. Save the template file.
4. Add the template to MagicDraw.

When generating a document, Report Wizard will apply the text format used in writing the Velocity code.

For instance, when typing:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

the generated output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Glossary

- Velocity A Java-based template engine. It permits anyone to use a simple, yet powerful, template language to reference objects defined in Java code . Velocity is a project of the[Apache Software Foundation](http://www.apache.org/), charged with the creation and maintenance of open-source software related to the[Apache Velocity Engine](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/index.html). All software created at the Velocity project is available under the[Apache Software License](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.txt)and free of charge for the public.

#### More information about working with report templates in Report Wizard

To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a template for an HTML, a text, an XML, or a DocBook document' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet' space='MD2024x']

1358508560

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3488795d-3fe2-493d-908c-3f92759591ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358508562</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="deed1172-6fd7-4a42-a095-291437141707"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358508564</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61cb3a6e-e719-4ce3-8325-c4fad34c732e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358530037</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="953b2338-4ef1-4527-b79c-11cedeba8074"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12c15adc-dc8a-4f16-af64-5c478ed47c8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358508564</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)</h2><p>You can create a document in .rtf, .odt, or .docx file format and add it to MagicDraw as a report template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (<em>DOCX</em>)</p><hr /><ol><li>Open OpenOffice.org Writer or Microsoft Word for an <em>*.odt</em>, <em>*.rtf</em>, or <em>*.docx</em> file. (To save a file as an <em>*.odt</em> file in Microsoft Word, an add-on is required. You can get the add-on at <em><a class="external-link" href="http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/" rel="nofollow">http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/</a></em>.)</li><li>Add the Velocity Template Language codes to the template file.</li><li>Save the template file.</li><li>Add the template to MagicDraw.</li></ol><p>When generating a document, Report Wizard will apply the text format used in writing the Velocity code.</p><p>For instance, when typing:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="HelloWorld_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> the generated output will be:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="HelloWorld_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Velocity</strong><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);"><br />A</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> Java-based template engine. It permits anyone to use a simple, yet powerful, template language to reference objects defined in Java </span><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);">code</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);">. <span>Velocity is a project of the </span><a href="http://www.apache.org/">Apache Software Foundation</a><span>, charged with the creation and maintenance of open-source software related to the </span><a href="http://velocity.apache.org/engine/index.html">Apache Velocity Engine</a><span>. All software created at the Velocity project is available under the </span><a href="http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.txt">Apache Software License</a><span> and free of charge for the public.</span></span></li></ul><h2>More information about working with report templates in Report Wizard</h2><p>To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for an HTML, a text, an XML, or a DocBook document" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ac:link></p><p><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet" /></ac:link><br /></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f8689b9-d7aa-4ec9-be33-943d3fd4a880"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358508560</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body /></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714566 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00286: Creating a template for an HTML, a text, an XML, or a DocBook document

- page_id: `136714566`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714566/Creating+a+template+for+an+HTML+a+text+an+XML+or+a+DocBook+document
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:41.521+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool > Supported file types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a template for an *HTML*, a *text*, an *XML*, or a *DocBook* document

1. Open a text editor.
2. Add the code to the text file.
3. Save the template file (with a correct filename extension).
4. Add the template to Report Wizard.

- When you generate a report from a particular template, Report Wizard will automatically create an HTML , text , an XML , or a DocBook document depending on a given template filename extension.
- The #forrow , #forcol , and #forpage directives do not support HTML , text, XML , and DocBook format files.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a template for an <em>HTML</em>, a <em>text</em>, an <em>XML</em>, or a <em>DocBook</em> document</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a text editor.</li><li>Add the code to the text file.</li><li>Save the template file (with a correct filename extension).</li><li>Add the template to Report Wizard.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6853e0b9-8381-4096-9c74-cccd903d3c91"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>When you generate a report from a particular template, Report Wizard will automatically create an <em>HTML</em>, <em>text</em>, an <em>XML</em>, or a <em>DocBook</em> document depending on a given template filename extension.</li><li>The <strong>#forrow</strong>,<strong> #forcol</strong>, and <strong>#forpage</strong> directives do not support <em>HTML</em>, <em>text, XML</em>, and <em>DocBook</em> format files.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714571 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00287: Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)

- page_id: `136714571`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714571/Creating+a+template+for+an+OpenDocument+Presentation+ODP+or+Microsoft+PowerPoint+presentation+PPTX
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:41.745+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool > Supported file types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1351510228

1351510229

1351510227

**On this page**

3

1351510229

To create a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (*PPTX*)

1. Open OpenOffice.org Impress or Microsoft PowerPoint.
2. Add the code to the template file.
3. Save the template file.
4. Add the template to Report Wizard.

A presentation document is a special document template that does not contain a content order. It uses a text box to insert text as the content of the document. A text box is an image structure (an image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates). You can change the position of a text box, or place it in the same position as others.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Writing VTL in a text box in [OpenOffice.org](http://OpenOffice.org) Impress.

The following are the rules that you need to follow when using the **#if,****#elseif**, **#else**, **#foreach**, **#forpage**, and **#macro** directives in **.odp* and *.pptx* documents:

- Statement VTL statements that maintain a sequence order, such as **#if** and **#foreach**, must begin and end the statements inside the same text box. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Valid use of multi-line statement in ODP. Do not split the statements into several text boxes, as this will cause an error. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Invalid use of a multi-line statement in ODP.
- #macro statement A #macro statement must be declared in the same text box. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Valid use of macro statement in ODP.
- #forpage statement The **#forpage** statement is different from the others, as it does not contain any order. It can begin and end the statement in different text boxes. Its value can also be used in different text boxes. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Valid use of the #forpage statement in ODP. To use a structured statement such as **#if**, **#macro**, or **#foreach** with the **#forpage** statement, ensure that the statement is in the same text box. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Valid use of #if and #foreach with #forpage statement. Using an example from the previous figure, the generated result will be as follows: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The end result of using #if and #foreach with #forpage statement.

#### Glossary

- VTL TheVelocity Template Language(VTL) is meant to provide an easy, simple, and clean way to incorporate dynamic content in a web page. A VTL statementbegins with the # character and contains a directive: set .

#### More information about working with Velocity Template Language (VTL)

To learn more about working with Velocity Template Language (VTL), visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Velocity templating language' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Velocity variable' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Velocity Directives' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714567 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00288: Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet

- page_id: `136714567`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714567/Creating+a+template+for+an+OpenDocument+spreadsheet+or+Microsoft+Excel+spreadsheet
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:41.610+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool > Supported file types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet

1. Open OpenOffice.org Calc or Microsoft Excel.
2. Add the code to the template file.
3. Save the template file.
4. Add the template to Report Wizard.

All multiline directives such as **#if, #else,****#elseif**, **#foreach**, and **#macro** must be used under the following conditions:

1. The beginning and end of a statement must be declared within a single cell. A #macro statement must be declared in a single cell. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav195c3297d20439835819f6152c9fb7fe.png']
2. You can use #foreach in a single cell record only. To create data for multiple rows, use the #forrow directive instead, see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav6f99f5e91aea399e24eac62e0953d947.png']
3. You can use #forcol to create data for multiple columns, see figure below. You can use the statement in conjunction with #forrow . [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav55c194a898ab2d279c85ddb229cdf770.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet</p><hr /><ol><li>Open OpenOffice.org Calc or Microsoft Excel.</li><li>Add the code to the template file.</li><li>Save the template file.</li><li>Add the template to Report Wizard.</li></ol><p>All multiline directives such as <strong>#if, #else, </strong><strong>#elseif</strong>, <strong>#foreach</strong>, and <strong>#macro</strong> must be used under the following conditions:</p><ol><li>The beginning and end of a statement must be declared within a single cell. A <strong>#macro</strong> statement must be declared in a single cell.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav195c3297d20439835819f6152c9fb7fe.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>You can use <strong>#foreach</strong> in a single cell record only. To create data for multiple rows, use the <strong>#forrow</strong> directive instead, see figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav6f99f5e91aea399e24eac62e0953d947.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>You can use <strong>#forcol</strong> to create data for multiple columns, see figure below. You can use the statement in conjunction with <strong>#forrow</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav55c194a898ab2d279c85ddb229cdf770.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol>
````
